name: Student Organization Resource Center
description:
The Student Organization Resource Center (SORC) is the student center designed to support all aspects of student organization operations and development. Through the offerings of our physical space in the Rush Building, to the service provided by our team of professional staff and student workers, we help to foster engagement on campus and around the globe, and work with student organization officers to develop as leaders. 

 

The duties/responsibilities of SORC are as follows:

  • Process all student organization purchase requests.
  • Process all student organization event forms
  • Advise student organizations on all aspects pertaining to operations within their organization.
  • Administer all contracting/payment for outside vendors.
  • Process all equipment (cotton candy machine, projectors, etc.) reservations and rentals.
  • Handle the day-to-day operations of the Student Organization Resource Center (SORC), including printing services, package pickup, poster approval, arts and crafts supplies, and more.
  • Manage DragonLink, the student organization management and engagement software for Drexel
  • Manage WebFinance for student organizations, including providing passwords to officers
  • Handle all deposits and organization monies
  • Manage student organization and commuter printing
  • Manage the Student Life Leadership Curriculum
  • Manage annual New Organization process
  • Manage Recognition, the annual transition and training of officers
  • Manage the Annual Space Allocation process
  • Collaborate and liaise with offices and departments across campus (General Counsel, Accounts Payable, University Marketing and Communications, Information Technology, Event Services, Public Safety, et al.) to ensure the overall success of all student organizations.

 

Business Hours: Monday-Friday, 9am-5pm (staff is reduced Summer Friday afternoons)

 

View the Student Organization Handbook 2025-2026 at the link below. This document is extremely comprehensive, providing a centralized, written document of all policies and procedures that organizations must adhere to. Similar to the Code of Conduct, student organizations are expected to be familiar with and abide by the content in this document. 

https://dragonlink.drexel.edu/organization/CE/documents/view/2515152

 

 

Office Hours:

Tuesdays, 1:30 - 2:30 PM on Zoom (https://drexel.zoom.us/j/82669894061?from=addon)

Wednesdays, 2:00 - 3:00 PM in Rush 311

Fridays, 11:00 - 12:00 PM in Rush 311
summary: The Student Organization Resource Center (SORC) is the student center designed to support all aspects of student organization operations and development.

name: Student Activity Fee Allocation Committee
description:
The Student Activity Fee Allocation Committee (SAFAC) exists to support the initiatives, goals, and activities of recognized Drexel student organizations and Student Life by allocating the student activity fees. Every year SAFAC receives $2 million to distribute to our undergraduate organizations on campus. SAFAC works with organizations to distribute this funding to positively impact all students on campus. We apply consistent unbiased funding decisions to represent the interests of the undergraduate students and enhance campus life by supporting the educational mission of Drexel University.

One of our missions is to ensure that an enthusiastic and diverse group runs this organization. We accept applications at the beginning of every fall term. Please use our application (found under 'Forms') to become involved in our organization. Becoming a part of a professional organization here on campus will increase involvement and create lasting connections!
summary: The Student Activity Fee Allocation Committee (SAFAC) exists to support the initiatives, goals, and activities of recognized Drexel student organizations and Student Life by allocating the student activity fees.

name: Queer Student Union
description: QSU is a non-exclusive organization that strives to promote acceptance, understanding, and unity among the LGBTQ+ community and with other communities at Drexel University, as well as within the city of Philadelphia. This goal will be accomplished through the creation of a supportive, constructive, active, and social atmosphere through educational and social activities as well as through the discussion of issues that affect the community, both externally and internally. In addition, we want to hold true to Drexel University’s values of civic engagement by advocacy for the LGBTQ+ community at large. QSU encourages the formation of positive friendships to provide the foundation for a strong LGBTQ+ community and offers opportunities for guidance, support, information, and recreation. Moreover, QSU pledges to offer unconditional solidarity with marginalized people and communities.
summary: QSU is a non-exclusive organization for LBGTQIA+ students and their allies at Drexel University. Our goal is to provide students with an open community, resources, and space for them to connect with Drexel's and Philadelphia's LGBTQIA+ communities.

name: Drexel Nutrition Association
description:
Drexel Nutrition Association is the nutrition organization on campus at Drexel University! We hope to connect all undergraduate and graduate students interested in nutrition and food.

We are involved in building nutrition awareness on campus and in the community, guided by the direction of the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics. We are involved in nutrition and health community service and other fun activities that allow us to grow as a strong organization!
summary: Drexel Nutrition Association is the student nutrition organization on campus at Drexel University! We hope to connect all undergraduate and graduate students interested in nutrition and food.

name: International Students Union
description:
The International Students Union's mission is to help international students have a sense of community and family at Drexel - a home away from home. We help international students with concerns or questions they may have before and during their move to Drexel. We will hold executive board elections every winter term, and open management committee applications once a year for students to have a chance to develop their leadership skills and actively participate in Drexel's community. 

The International Students Union aims to achieve multicultural awareness, promote mutual respect of diverse backgrounds and perspectives on campus, and help develop mutual respect for diverse national heritage. We strive towards endorsing and participating in civic engagement to help students bring about positive changes by helping the community and expanding their global perspective. Moreover, we advocate for international students and act as the official middleman by engaging with the administration to improve the international student experience in terms of institutional inclusion, equity, and support.

Please follow us on Instagram for the latest events and updates!
summary: The International Students Union aims to create a sense of community for all international students at Drexel by increasing multicultural awareness and acceptance, and promoting mutual respect of diverse heritages.

name: Drexel University Judo Team
description:
Whether you've been doing martial arts your whole life or are interested in trying something you've never done before, Drexel Judo is a great place for you. We pride ourselves on having the ability to teach students of all experience levels while providing them with a positive competitive team environment, an excellent fitness regimen, and knowledgeable instructors.

 

Judo is an internationally recognized Olympic sport with a large following across the globe.  It consists of a combination of grappling, throws, takedowns, and submissions. It's an effective skillset that adapts well to anyone and everyone!

 

We have a linktree that links our socials as well as a checklist of all the paperwork necessary in order to practice.

 

IMPORTANT!

Members who request to join will be added to the official roster upon completion of the required paperwork and consistent attendance to practice. Members who do not submit their paperwork or engage with our social platforms and e-board in any way will be considered inactive and removed from the pending list. If you have any questions regarding the paperwork or the club in general do not hesitate to ask in our Discord server.
summary: Drexel Judo Club prides itself on having the ability to teach students of all experience levels while providing them with a positive competitive team environment, an excellent fitness regimen, and knowledgeable instructors.

name: Drexel Architectural Engineering Institute
description:
The association will stand as a local representation of the national Architectural Engineering Institute. This organization is affiliated with Drexel University as a recognized student organization and as such, agrees to abide by the rules, policies, and procedures of Drexel University. Drexel University’s chapter of AEI will serve its members by providing a forum for issues relative to the building industry, acting as an avenue of communication between members if this educational institution and the professional community, and developing a dialogue to convey praise or voice concerns and suggest improvements to the engineering curriculum at the University. Additionally, AEI will serve its members in any way deemed appropriate or necessary in both the educational and professional environments. AEI shall be the student organization which designs and fabricates the steel bridge for the annual Student Steel Bridge Competition sponsored by the American Institute of Steel Construction. With this, AEI will educate its members on the real-world logistics involved in actual projects, from design to fabrication.

To see what we are doing right now, check out our website or Facebook page.
summary: Drexel AEI is a collaborative group for those interested in any aspects of the engineered world we see on a daily basis. We also oversee the Drexel's Steel Bridge Team, which competes in the annual AISC Student Steel Bridge Competition (SSBC).

name: Jewish Student Association
description: JSA is dedicated to creating a pluralistic Jewish undergraduate community that fosters social, educational, cultural, social action and religious experiences that help students develop their own Jewish identities and make enduring commitments to Jewish life, learning and Israel.
summary: JSA is dedicated to creating a pluralistic Jewish undergraduate community that fosters social, educational, cultural, social action and religious experiences that help students develop their own Jewish identities.

name: Drexel Karate Club
description:
The Drexel Karate Club offers traditional Shotokan Karate training to all Drexel students. We welcome all members - from the inexperienced saplings to battled-hardened warriors! Shotokan Karate is a way in both mind and body, per our first dojo kun, or lesson of our dojo: Seek perfection of character.

 

We train every Monday, Wednesday, and Friday night from 6-8 pm all year round. Practices are usually held at the blue and yellow mats outside the Daskalakis Athletic Center (near the horse statue) and in the DAC, but it varies from time to time. Joining the Discord is a surefire way to be sure you know what’s happening and when: https://discord.gg/EBzrCBCr8P

 

The Club has won the National Collegiate Karate Championship in 2008, 2009, 2010, and 2012. We have also taken top three in every participating division in 2025 Nationals. We encourage new members to compete in events, but it is not required.

 

New members are accepted during the first 4 weeks of every term. To join, you must request Dragonlink membership in both Karate Club and Drexel University Club Sports, the parent organization of all sports clubs. Don't worry if your membership request for Karate isn't approved immediately--it isn't required for you to participate. We'll be helping you through the activation process once you join our Discord! Check us out at the regularly hosted Involvement Fairs on Drexel campus!
summary: We offer traditional Shotokan Karate training to all Drexel students, no experience required. We train every MWF from 6-8 pm all year round, though times may vary. Begin your Karate-do with us!

name: 8 to the Bar
description:
8 to the Bar is Drexel University's premiere all-male-identifying and non-binary a cappella group! We perform original arrangements of contemporary music using only the human voice as our instrument. Each year, we organize concerts and perform at other universities throughout the city, state, and east coast. We also support charities and benefit foundations by performing in support of their causes. Our mission is to broaden the musical horizons of the Drexel community and beyond by performing unique interpretations of various songs infused with our infectious brand of humor and stage presence. For those of you interested in joining the craze, auditions are held during the first two weeks of Fall Term! For more info:

 

Come sing with us! (Audition Form): https://forms.gle/uwv2zKqBqJPdzPrm8

E-mail Us:  8tothebar@gmail.com

Find us on YouTube!   http://www.youtube.com/8ttb

We're also on Facebook, Instagram, and Twitter as @8totheBar!
summary: 8 to the Bar is Drexel University's premiere all-male a cappella group! We perform original arrangements of contemporary music using only the human voice as our instrument. You'll see us performing at concerts on campus and all over Philadelphia!

name: Drexel Sierra Club
description:
Our primary method of communication is through our discord: https://discord.gg/H2mnDEfv6D 

You can also email drexelsierraclub@gmail.com with any questions if you prefer.

Drexel Sierra Club seeks to spread environmental awareness and instill an appreciation of the natural world in order to reduce Drexel’s environmental impact through its policies and student involvement, and to educate students to continue to help them in their environmental efforts after they graduate.

linktr.ee/drexel_sierraclub
summary: Drexel Sierra Club seeks to protect and appreciate the environment by working to reduce Drexel's environmental impact and taking hikes, going camping, and other outdoor trips.

name: Filipino Intercultural Society of Drexel University
description:
FISDU's objective is to strengthen, represent, and spread Filipino culture throughout the Drexel Community. However, FISDU does not solely focus on Filipinos, as it welcomes all individuals interested in learning more about the history and culture of the Philippines. 

FISDU interacts with other organizations on campus and is recognized on a national level. We offer support and aid to various non-profit organizations, as well as promote Filipino awareness through various cultural and social events. FISDU strives to be open and welcomes the entire Drexel community.
summary: FISDU's objective is to strengthen, represent, and spread Filipino culture throughout the Drexel Community. However, FISDU does not solely focus on Filipinos, as it welcomes all individuals interested in learning more about Filipino culture and history.

name: Drexel Pre-PA Club
description: Drexel Pre-PA club is a student organization aimed to prepare our members academically and professionally to be the best applicants possible for Physician Assistant graduate schools. We are dedicated to becoming more involved in the Physician Assistant community, by gaining awareness of current issues related to Physician Assistants (PA), healthcare, and PA education. We seek to build health awareness through promotion of service and hands-on volunteer experiences in Philadelphia. In order to achieve excellence, we provide members with opportunities such as networking in the medical community, providing outlets for patient contact experiences, and guest lectures.
summary: This club exists to make members the best applicants possible for Physician Assistant school. In order to achieve this, we provide members with opportunities such as networking in the medical community, gaining awareness of current issues in healthcare.

name: DAC Pack
description:
Since its inception in 2002, the DAC Pack- Drexel Athletics’ student fan section- has become one of the largest, most visible, and most respected student organizations at Drexel University and throughout the Coastal Athletic Association.

Unlike many organizations, one does not have to “join” the DAC Pack; there are no fees, and nothing is ever mandatory. The DAC Pack is simply the representation of the student body that shows its support for Drexel and its student-athletes by standing together at the games, having fun, and partaking in the wonderful experience that is collegiate athletics. The organization’s primary goal is to help build and foster the Drexel Community by spreading school spirit and pride. The now famous gold DAC Pack shirts are free, and are seen not only at the games, but also in almost every classroom, dining establishment, and residence hall on campus.

The DAC Pack also organizes a variety of free events throughout the year, such as pre-game Bar-B-Qs, road trips to local away games, Dragon Tipoff, and a weekend trip to Washington, D.C. for the men’s CAA conference tournament.
summary: Since its inception in 2002, the DAC Pack - Drexel Athletics' student fan section - has become one of the largest, most visible, and most respected student organizations at Drexel University and throughout the Coastal Athletic Association.

name: Drexel Undergraduate Mock Trial Association
description:
The Mock Trial Association provides students in all majors the ability to actively participate in mock trial competitions. These competitions benefit those students who are interested in refining their public speaking and critical reasoning skills, as well as those looking for a learning experience prior to attending law school. The primary focus of the Mock Trial Association is to participate and compete successfully in regional and/or national competitions that will academically challenge and stimulate the organization.

Please check out our Instagram, @dumocktrial, for further information.
summary: Drexel's Mock Trial Association provides students with the opportunity to gain courtroom and litigation experience on the national level, strengthening critical skills like public speaking, complex analysis, organization, and storytelling.

name: Pi Tau Sigma, Mechanical Engineering Honor Society
description: PI TAU SIGMA  is the International Honor Society for Mechanical Engineers. Pi Tau Sigma members are chosen on a basis of sound engineering ability, scholarship service, leadership, and integrity. The Pi Tau Sigma mission is to strive to create better engineers through commitment to academic excellence and dedication to service. Pi Tau Sigma prides itself in the core values: Integrity (soundness of character and moral conduct), Service (serving the mechanical engineering profession and the community), and Leadership (fostering initiative through example in a professional manner). Pi Tau Sigma is a member of the Association of College Honor Societies. There are presently 180 collegiate chapters at universities in the United States, Puerto Rico, Mexico, Qatar, and United Arab Emirates.
summary: Pi Tau Sigma is the International Honor Society for Mechanical Engineers. The Pi Tau Sigma mission is to strive to create better engineers through commitment to academic excellence and dedication to service.

name: Drexel Students for Christ
description:
Drexel Students for Christ is a student-led organization committed to seeing lives continually changed by and for the Gospel of Jesus Christ. As an independent student organization of Drexel University, we strive to build close bonds with and seek directional counsel from our local churches.

We exist to make and build disciples of Jesus Christ and strive to accomplish that by building a community that loves and serves Drexel’s campus by proclaiming the good news of Jesus Christ and welcoming students into diverse local churches.
summary: Drexel Students for Christ seeks to build a community of Christians on Drexel's campus, and helps students find churches nearby. Join us!

name: Tau Beta Pi
description: Founded in 1930, Pennsylvania Zeta is the Tau Beta Pi chapter at Drexel University. Juniors and Seniors (by credit) from approved curricula of the College of Engineering and the School of Biomedical Engineering, Science & Health Systems who meet the requirements of academic excellence and exemplary character are eligible to be elected into the Pennsylvania Zeta Chapter of Tau Beta Pi.
summary: Tau Beta Pi is the National Engineering Honors Society, which encourages an open-minded liberal culture within the engineering discipline.

name: Drexel American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description:
ASME is a professional organization focused on engineering development through technical workshops and professional events. We host events ranging from 3D printing workshops to professional socials that bring together students from diverse backgrounds.

 

We provide assistance with projects, especially those that are technically oriented and fall within the lines of mechanical and/or electrical engineering. Additionally, we are a competition-focused organization, participating in the annual Student Design Competition, which has varying challenges each year.

 

ASME has an event almost every week, with each one being open to ANY student who wishes to join. Free food is always available, so stop by!

 

Check out our google calendar for an up-to-date event schedule!

 

- Join our Discord for event updates and convenient access to our members!
summary: ASME is an academic organization focused on engineering development through technical workshops and professional events. We host events ranging from 3D printing workshops to professional socials that bring together students from diverse backgrounds.

name: Drexel Muslim Students Association
description:
Muslim students are an integral part of the student body at Drexel University, and as they make their journey on their quest of knowledge and self-betterment, Drexel Muslim Students Association (DMSA) aims to support them by being a source of community and Islamic education on campus – helping them to maintain and strengthen their Islamic identity while they develop as academic professionals.

From its inception in 2010 to its present-day status, DMSA has been committed to fostering unity, brotherhood, and sisterhood within Drexel’s Muslim community, through conducting social, religious, community service, and other activities in the best interest of Islam. It has also strived to be a convergence of inter-faith communication, promoting tolerance and understanding and opening channels of healthy discussion and debate with those of other faiths and traditions. By establishing these fora, DMSA intends to build confidence in students’ abilities to express themselves as religious individuals and as members of society, questioning the status quo and shaping the voices of the next generation.

An essential part of DMSA’s mission involves providing students, Muslim and non-Muslim, with opportunities for learning and discussion in the form of guest lecturers and Qu’ranic Study sessions. DMSA has also reserved a prayer room – open at any time for students wishing to conduct their daily prayers on campus, and congregational prayers such as Jummah and Eid prayers are scheduled. Ramadan Iftars, as well as Eid Dinners are also organized to help students cohere through celebration and common experience. Through collaboration with other Philadelphia MSAs on community service initiatives, academic competitions and other important events, DMSA is helping to make Philadelphia a tighter-knit environment for Muslim students.

We hope that you take advantage of the services we offer, while you are at Drexel, and we encourage you to get involved in any way possible. Come along to an event and see what we’re all about, and if you have any questions, do not hesitate to contact us.
summary: Muslim students are an integral part of the student body at Drexel University. DMSA aims to support them by being a source of community and Islamic education on campus. Aiming to make Drexel an enjoyable and tighter-knit environment for all students.

name: Vietnamese Student Association
description: The Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) is a student-run organization that aims to promote Vietnamese culture, heritage, and traditions among students and the wider community. VSA members typically organize cultural events, social activities, and community service projects, providing opportunities for Vietnamese students and others interested in Vietnamese culture to connect, network, and learn from one another.
summary: Drexel Vietnamese Student Association is dedicated to providing those with a different background a better understanding of the Vietnamese culture through events that would promote beneficial outcome to the university and other members of the community.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers - Drexel University Student Chapter
description:
Drexel University Chapter of the
American Society of Civil Engineers

The American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE) is an organization that fosters professional and academic excellence in the civil engineering industry and promotes a greater understanding of the industry. We provide opportunities for professional development, learning, networking, and leadership for students, bridging the gap between them and professionals by serving as a connection to City, Regional, and National sections of ASCE.
summary: We are organization that fosters professional development, academic excellence, and leadership in the civil engineering field, bridging gaps between students and professionals by serving as a connection to City, Regional, and National sections of ASCE.

name: Drexel Institute of Electrical & Electronics Engineers
description:
The Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) is the world's largest technical professional society. There are 50,000 student members within a total membership of nearly 360,000. The technical objective of the IEEE is to focus on advancing the theory and practice of electrical, electronics and computer engineering and computer science. The vision of IEEE is to be essential to the global technical community and to technical professionals everywhere, and be universally recognized for the contributions of technology and of technical professionals in improving global conditions.

To realize these objectives, the IEEE sponsors nearly 800 Student branches worldwide, as well as scholarships and awareness programs, technical conferences, symposia and local meetings; publishes 30% of world's technical papers in electrical, electronics and computer engineering; and provides educational programs to keep its members' knowledge and expertise state-of-the-art.

Drexel University's chapter is one of the largest by membership in the United States. We are focused on providing students with academic assistance and sponsoring events that promote the IEEE related technical fields. IEEE hosts technical, social, and outreach events. IEEE also hosts Drexel's largest student-run hackathon, DragonHacks. Additionally, we continually acts as a liason with the ECE department to help students and faculty communicate through events such as the quarterly ECE dialogue.
summary: The Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) is the world's largest technical professional society. IEEE's core purpose is to foster technological innovation and excellence for the benefit of humanity. We are the Drexel student branch.

name: Drexel Women in Business
description:
Drexel Women in Business is a joint student organization. Drexel Women in Business (DWIB) is a network of dynamic, like-minded women achieving their business goals through support, inclusion, inspiration, and mentoring.

DWIB maintains a strong network of women in the business community by coordinating networking events, speaker series, workshops, and similar activities. These events are open to the entire Drexel University community in order to foster growth, relationships and future opportunities. This organization emphasizes LeBow's ties to the alumni network and to the greater Philadelphia business community, and upholds LeBow's commitment to excellence.
summary: Drexel Women in Business is a joint student organization. Drexel Women in Business (DWIB) is a network of dynamic, like-minded women achieving their business goals through support, inclusion, inspiration, and mentoring.

name: WKDU Philadelphia 91.7FM
description:
WKDU is an award-winning non-commercial college radio station located in Philadelphia, PA, broadcasting on 91.7 FM. It is operated by students and alumni of Drexel University. It is the only free-format, non-commercial, student-run FM station in Philadelphia, and proudly provides its listeners with a variety of programming content ranging from lively sounds that resonate through the various communities of Philadelphia to educational public affairs.

To contact us about joining WKDU, request a tour, or ask general membership questions: email us at new@wkdu.org.
summary: WKDU is an award winning, free format, non-commercial FM college radio station located in Philadelphia, PA. Broadcasting on 91.7 FM, WKDU is owned and operated by Drexel University and its students.

name: Drexel University Club Cricket
description:
WELCOME TO DUCC 24-2025 SEASON:

We have practices scheduled for the spring and summer term. So if you are interested in joining our club, please request to join our club's membership on DragonLink and complete individual activation requirements to be on the official master roster and able to attend our practices and future games. (Individual Activation instructions can be found in the documents section)

 

Also, please use this link below to join the WhatsApp group chat to ask questions and to stay up to date with this club.

Link: https://chat.whatsapp.com/C3XAWg6bzi9AOLWjCPqnQa

 

2024-25 Executive Board

  • President: Vivek Thankachan
  • Co-President: Anandhu Sunos
  • Vice-President: Vishnuvardhan Reddy Kannapu
  • VP - Social Media: Keril Shah
  • Treasurer: Anandhu Sunos
  • Travel Coordinator: Amal Ashok


Drexel University Club Cricket is one of the top-ranked clubs in the country. It was established to promote the Sport of Cricket at Drexel University through dedicated practices and intercollegiate competitions fueled by Drexel funding and has extended the university's reputation. Even our alumni are in touch with the club as they have taken the role of volunteer coaches to ensure the club is constantly improving. Alumni like Maaez Veqar are playing First-class cricket in Pakistan.

Cricket is the second most popular sport with around 2.5 billion fans. USA Cricket team after gaining the One-Day-International (ODI) status from the International Cricket Council (ICC) becomes the only American Team Sport to play in an international format. This is a massive step for cricket in America, with over 200,000 Players playing domestic cricket. This not only enables the US to play cricket internationally but also host International Teams with the same status and qualifies them for the ICC Cricket World Cup.

Saurabh Netravalkar plays for the USA Cricket team and is a graduate of Cornell University. He used to play for Cornell University in the American College Cricket League, which is the same league under which Drexel University Club Cricket participates along with other American Universities.

Drexel University Club Cricket takes part in two American College Cricket main events:


MID-ATLANTIC REGIONALS: Held in Collegeville, Pennsylvania

  • Runner's Up (2018) - MVP - Harshil U. Patel
  • Champions (2017) - MVPs - Harshil U. Patel and Sneh Patel - Our Dragons!
  • Champions (2015)
  • Runner's Up (2014)
  • Semi-Finalists (2013)

 

NATIONAL CHAMPIONSHIP: Held in Fort Lauderdale, Florida (Currently held in Houston, Texas)

After the 2014 Regionals, Drexel qualified for Nationals for the first time in the 2015 season.

  • Semi-Finalists (2013, 2016, 2022, 2023) 
  • Runners Up (2015)
summary:
Drexel University Club Cricket is one of the top-ranked clubs in the country. It is established to promote the Sport of Cricket at Drexel University through dedicated practices and intercollegiate competitions fueled by Drexel funding.

Go Dragons!

name: Drexel University Circle K
description: Drexel University Circle K is an open group dedicated to community service, leadership, and fellowship. We encourage students to participate in our club if they enjoy (or need to fulfill requirements for) service activities, seek a meaningful leadership position, or love having fun. As a recognized chapter of Circle K International, Drexel Circle K members are given the opportunity to interact with Circle K'ers from all around the world! Drexel University allows our branch of Circle K to tap into Philadelphia's breadth of volunteering options, as well as a plethora of communities to involve ourselves with. Students need not pay to participate in our club!
summary: Circle K is Drexel's leading community service organization, and is a recognized chapter of Circle K International, the largest student-led service organization in the world!

name: Drexel Newman Catholic Community
description:
At Drexel Newman, our motto is, "Heart Speaks to Heart". 

The members of the Drexel Newman Catholic Community are the presence of the Catholic Church here at Drexel University. We strive to live in the spirit of the Gospel of Jesus Christ, and further bring this spirit to the wider University community by providing spiritual, service, and social opportunities for students, staff, and faculty.

For Sunday Mass, we join Sts. Agatha and James Parish, located at 38th & Chestnut Street. We also share a Newman Center with the University of Pennsylvania that is located behind the Church. Our on-campus chapel and campus office are located on the second floor of the Academic Building (33rd & Arch).

We host weekly Mass and monthly Newman Nights every Tuesday at 7PM in the Newman Center. We also have weekly Newman dinners every Thursday at 6:00PM in the Newman Center. Some of our other activities include Bible studies, small groups, swing dancing nights, Christ in the City homeless outreach, retreats, and more!

Whether you are a seeker, a non-Catholic, or a Catholic, you are welcome to join us! We believe we can mutually enrich our understanding of God through charitable dialogue.
summary: "Heart speaks to heart." The Drexel Newman Catholic Community is the presence of the Catholic Church at Drexel. We strive to live out the spirit of the Gospel in our daily lives. We have spiritual, service and social opportunities to foster faith growth.

name: Japanese Undergraduate Student Association
description: The Japanese Undergraduate Student Association (JUSA) welcomes all students, whether you’re Japanese, studying the language, or simply interested in the culture. Our mission is to share and celebrate Japanese traditions with the Drexel community through inclusive, engaging events such as language workshops, game nights, film screenings, sushi-making sessions, and ramen tastings. We also organize trips to festivals like the Sakura Matsuri in New York and the Fairmount Park Sakura Festival in Philadelphia, volunteer with groups such as Shofuso and the Japan America Society of Greater Philadelphia (JASGP), and collaborate with other universities including UPenn and Temple to promote cultural exchange and community.
summary: The Japanese Undergraduate Student Association (JUSA) welcomes everyone interested in Japanese culture. We host weekly events, cultural workshops, and group trips to D.C. and New York City to share and experience Japanese traditions together.

name: Society of Women Engineers
description:
SWE is an organization dedicated to promoting women in the engineering fields by providing a support network to its members, professional development opportunities, and community service opportunities. We welcome all genders and majors!

For more information, please visit our website at http://drexel.swe.org/
summary: SWE is an organization dedicated to promoting women in the engineering fields by providing a support network to its members, professional development opportunities, and community service opportunities. We welcome all genders and majors!

name: West Indian Student Establishment
description: The purpose of this organization is to socially, academically, and culturally unite the West Indian student population at Drexel University in order to provide a channel for the sharing of the Caribbean culture, traditions and beliefs with the rest of the Drexel University community.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to socially, academically, and culturally unite the West-Indian student population at Drexel University, providing a channel for the sharing of the Caribbean culture, traditions and beliefs with the entire community.

name: Campus Activities Board
description:
The Campus Activities Board (CAB) is the largest student-run event programming organization at Drexel University. Our purpose is to plan and execute a variety of events reflective of the diverse interests and needs of the undergraduate student body. We strive to entertain, educate, and enrich the lives of students through our programming. 

CAB events, with the exception of ticketed events, are first come first serve by arrival. We do not use Dragonlink RSVPs.
summary: The Campus Activities Board (CAB) is the largest student-run event programming organization at Drexel University. Our purpose is to plan and execute a variety of events reflective of the diverse interests and needs of the undergraduate student body.

name: Drexel University Student Nurses Association
description: Drexel University Student Nurses Association's mission is to facilitate student involvement in advancing the standards of nursing education and the educational process and to advocate for healthcare, nursing education, and nursing practice through legislative activity at the local, state, and national levels. We strive to promote and encourage civic engagement by participation in community activities and affairs that improve the health and social issues of our communities.
summary: Drexel's pre-professional nursing organization. We host events with opportunities for professional development, mentorship, and networking for student nurses. Through SNAP and NSNA, we connect students with nursing at a local, state, and national level.

name: Indian Undergraduate Students Association
description:
Welcome to DISHA, the Drexel Indian Undergraduate Students Association. This association's main purpose is to celebrate the radiant spirit of India and share India's rich, rhythmic, and fascinating cultural heritage with the diverse student body at Drexel University, through various events all year round.

Prospective Events by quarter:

Fall: Diwali

Winter: Bollywood Party

Spring: Holi

 

WE'RE RECRUITING FOR FALL TERM!! Apply here or learn more about DISHA on our instagram!!

                                                                       

Check out our Instagram page
summary: Welcome to DISHA, the Drexel Indian Undergraduate Students Association. This association's main purpose is to celebrate the radiant spirit of India and share India's rich, rhythmic and fascinating cultural heritage with the diverse student body.

name: Public Relations Student Society of America
description: The Drexel University Chapter of PRSSA strives for national PRSSA goals, including: Serving our members by enhancing their knowledge of public relations and providing access to professional development opportunities; and Serving the public relations profession by helping to develop highly qualified, well-prepared professionals. Specifically to our members at Drexel University, we also aim to enhance our members' co-op experiences through events, leadership roles and team building activities that will provide valuable networking and professional development opportunities
summary: Serving our members by enhancing their knowledge of public relations and providing access to professional development opportunities; and serving the public relations profession by helping to develop highly qualified, well-prepared professionals.

name: Open Door Christian Community
description:
We are an open, welcoming Christian community that helps students of any or no faith, work through life's biggest problems through spiritual guidance and community service.  We invite you to gather with us.

We ask questions and together search out answers, believing faith speaks to us in a multitude of ways. We help one another along our journey, whether that is just beginning or life-long.

We believe our faith shapes our lives, giving us meaning and purpose in everything we do. From figuring out our career paths to spending time serving others, we hope to make the world more reflective of God’s dream for creation.
summary: We provide weekly events to fight food insecurity to provide safe spaces for students to explore faith and build relationships with others. We seek to create space to discuss the intersection of social justice and faith. We affirm the LGBTQ+ community.

name: Hellenic Student Association
description:
To be actively involved with the Hellenic Community of Philadelphia and the Hellenic Nation, in collaboration with the different organizations that exist within the Hellenic Community. We support and promote Hellenic student societies in foreign countries which are actively seeking to resolve common concerns. 
    We offer guidance and information to every Greek student that wishes to enroll at Drexel University, coming from a different state or country with respect to his/her own registration, familiarization, and orientation within the university. 
    We also are working to promote the education of the modern Greek language, culture, and Co-Op opportunities in Greece. Doing so will attract more attention to the study abroad opportunities in Greece or Europe in general.
summary: The Hellenic Student Association (HSA) unites Greek-Americans, Greeks, Cypriots, and Philhellenes under one organization to promote the Greek culture and traditions.

name: Fraternity and Sorority Life
description:
Drexel University fraternities and sororities pride themselves on excellence and high standards. They embody the shared values of leadership, service, scholarship, and lifelong friendships. These chapters of inter/national organizations provide a positive impact to the larger Drexel community.

Key Highlights: 

1. 30+ Chapters: Social, culturally-based and religiously-based

2. 1200+ Undergraduate Members

3. 3.27 Cumulative Average GPA

4. 15+ Unique philanthropic causes supported

5. 400+ events held annually

 

Wondering if fraternity & sorority life is right for you? Fill out our interest form to receive more information!
summary: Fraternities and sororities have been an integral part of the Drexel community since 1900. Our organizations pride themselves on excellence and dedication to leadership, service, scholarship, and lifelong friendships and connections to Drexel.

name: Alpha Epsilon Pi- Delta Rho
description: Alpha Epsilon Pi, the Jewish Fraternity, was founded to provide opportunities for a Jewish man seeking the best possible college and fraternity experience. We have maintained the integrity of our purpose by strengthening our ties to the Jewish community and serving as a link between high school and career. Alpha Epsilon Pi develops leadership for the North American Jewish community at a critical time in a young man's life.
summary: Alpha Epsilon Pi, the Jewish Fraternity, was founded to provide opportunities for a Jewish man seeking the best possible college and fraternity experience. We have maintained the integrity of our purpose by strengthening our ties to the Jewish community.

name: Sigma Psi Zeta Sorority, Inc.
description:
The sisters of Sigma Psi Zeta seek to provide a means to success and support for women, particularly women of color. The sorority builds on the unity of strong and independent leaders to affect change in our organization, our members, our universities, and local communities. Sigma Psi Zeta is an official partner of It’s On Us, the White House Initiative to end sexual assault on college campuses.

 

Our sorority serves as a community of leaders, offering opportunities for women to exercise and develop professional and interpersonal skills in service of the national philanthropy and cultural awareness and education. To provide an international organization uniting strong and independent women to build a network of pioneers and innovators to serve as catalysts for change.
summary: Sigma Psi Zeta is a progressive multicultural Greek organization that provides a means to success and support for women, particularly women in color. The sorority builds on the unity of strong and independent leaders to affect change in communities.

name: Intercultural Greek Council
description: The Intercultural Greek Council shall serve as a coordinating agent for all minority Greek letter organizations recognized at Drexel University.  It will also serve as a liaison between the university and the organizations encompassed within the Intercultural Greek Council. It shall further their national programs; promote unity on college and university campuses and within the community.
summary: The Intercultural Greek Council serves as a coordinating agent for all minority Greek letter organizations recognized at Drexel. It serves as a liaison between the university and the organizations encompassed, while promoting unity on campus.

name: Club Swimming Team
description:
Welcome to the Drexel University Club Swimming Team! We practice together 2-3 times a week and compete against both local teams during the year and at Club Swimming National Championships each year. We welcome swimmers of all experience levels!

Follow us on Instagram @drexelclubswimming.  

Check out our new website here!

Email drexelclubswimming@gmail.com if you have any questions about the team. 

 

Practices: Monday and Wednesday 9:00 - 10:30 pm. Saturday 4-6pm 

Registration and completion of forms is required prior to attending practice
summary: Welcome to the Drexel University Club Swimming Team! We practice together 2-3 times a week and compete against both local teams during the year and at Club Swimming National Championships each year. We welcome swimmers of all experience levels!

name: Drexel Sailing Club
description:
The Drexel University Sailing Team is a club sport that competes at a high level in the Middle Atlantic Intercollegiate Sailing Association (MAISA) which is part of the Intercollegiate Sailing Association (ICSA).  The team is oriented around regattas which are held at schools along the east coast every weekend in the Fall and Spring.
 
The Club practices 2 or 3 times a week at Corinthian Yacht Club of Philadelphia in Essington, PA.  The team owns 8 FJs (2-person boats) and 2 Laser.
 
The team has approximately 12 undergraduate members, some of whom came to Drexel with extensive racing experience and others who started with no sailing experience.  Drexel Sailing Alumni are found racing all over the country in many one-design classes, PHRF fleets, and long-distance racing.  We even have a team alumnus who went on to become an Olympic medalist.
summary: The Drexel University Sailing Team compete in the Middle Atlantic Intercollegiate Sailing Association.  The team has a competitive focus and competes in regattas every weekend during the fall and spring seasons.  The team practice 2 times per week.

name: Drexel University Tae Kwon Do Club
description:
Drexel Tae Kwon Do Club is open to all students interested in learning martial arts and welcomes beginners to join anytime. Tae Kwon Do can help students learn balance, strength, speed, focus, and control in both the mental and physical senses. Club activities mainly consist of practices but we also often compete in tournaments, attend clinics for other martial arts such as bokken training or bagua, and train at sister schools. The club meets from 8PM-10PM Tuesdays and Thursdays in Activity Room #1 at the DAC. Practices consist of basics (basic techniques, a workout-type segment), forms (pre-set sequences of movements), sparring, and wrist-breaks or counter-attacks.

 

Join our discord to get active notifications about club events and other opportunities!

https://discord.gg/bCUhZdDPNG
summary: Drexel Tae Kwon Do Club is open to all students interested in learning martial arts and welcomes beginners to join anytime. Tae Kwon Do can help students learn balance, strength, speed, focus, and control in both the mental and physical senses.

name: Drexel University Men's Club Soccer
description:
Drexel University Men's Club Soccer team competes locally & regionally within NIRSA in the fall term CASA Collegiate Club Soccer in the spring term. We play our home games at Drexel’s Vidas Athletic Complex and travel to other schools for our away games.

Tryouts are scheduled at the discretion of the Board on a term-by-term basis. If you are interested, please check our Instagram page, as we post the dates for tryouts there immediately after they are scheduled. Alternatively, you can send an email to our address listed here. You must tryout to join the team.

This year, the Men's Club Soccer team will have one team competing in the NIRSA Region 1 league, aiming to qualify for the Regional tournament in October. This is our main competitive league and season. The club practices together twice each week in the fall and spring and also participates in an indoor league in the winter.
summary: The Drexel Men's Soccer Club competes within NIRSA in the fall term and CASA Collegiate Club Soccer in the spring term. Home games are played at Drexel's Vidas Athletic Complex. Tryouts are scheduled on a term-by-term basis, please see the full descript

name: Drexel University Club Field Hockey Team
description:
We are a spirited group of co-ed players of all ages and experience levels. We are members of the National Field Hockey League, which includes teams from across the country, but we usually compete against local teams during our regular season.

Our team strikes a balance between being competitive and having fun — we aim to qualify for the National Tournament in Virginia Beach each year, but enjoying the game is always a priority! We typically practice twice a week at night during the Fall and Spring seasons. While our Fall practice schedule is yet to be confirmed, we generally practice at Vidas Athletic Complex. Games are primarily held on weekends in both the Fall and Spring, sometimes in a round-robin format, where we play two teams in one day. This format helps us meet our qualifying requirements since our school starts later than most others.

We offer two team options: a game team and a practice team. The game team participates in all games and practices, while the practice team is for those who may have a busy quarter but still want to stay involved or learn the game. You’re free to choose which team suits you best — no try-outs required! Dues are collected each Fall and Spring, with game team members paying higher dues than those on the practice team.

 

PLEASE FILL OUT THIS FORM IF YOU WOULD LIKE TO JOIN OUR TEAM OR GET IN CONTACT WITH OUR RECRUITMENT OFFICER: https://forms.gle/Ura3AJDZWW8ZWoPY9 

You can also email us at dufieldhockey@gmail.com

 

FALL 2026-2026 PRACTICE SCHEDULE: 

TUESDAY 7-9PM Buckely Field Hockey Field 

THURSDAY 7-9pm Buckley Field Hockey Field
summary: The purpose of this co-ed club is to provide a team for Drexel students to play field hockey. The objective of the club is to promote a safe and fun atmosphere for playing, and to win games in the National Field Hockey League (NFHL) in the Fall & Spring.

name: Drexel University Equestrian Team
description:
DUET strives to allow for time in the saddle, despite Drexel’s fast-paced urban environment; to grow not only as equestrians and individuals, but to take the values learned in the riding ring, and use them to become better students, people, and citizens; and to build team spirit—a team with our fellow students, and a team with our horse.

Email our president at drexelequestrian.president@gmail.com if you are interested in joining our team!
summary: DUET strives to allow for time in the saddle, despite Drexel's fast-paced urban environment; to grow not only as equestrians and individuals, but to take the values learned in the riding ring, and use them to become better students, people, and

name: Drexel Badminton Club
description:
The Drexel Badminton Club allows badminton enthusiasts to share their interest in the sport by practicing and playing competitively with other students. It provides the opportunity to become more familiar with the sport and a platform to meet others who share the same interests. Club members learn about the rules and strategies of badminton during regular practice sessions.

 

Fall 25-26 Schedule: Mondays [6:00pm-7:30pm] AND Fridays [3:30pm-5:00pm] meet on the basketball courts in the gym!

 

The team is highly involved in invitational, collegiate and open badminton tournaments. We have successfully competed in the following prestigious tournaments sponsored by USA Badminton: National Collegiate, Mid Atlantic Classics, DC Open, Pennsylvania Open, Boston Open, and more.

The club is part of the Northeast Collegiate Association and USA Badminton.

 

Major Accomplishments:

2014: we won Drexel Club Sport's "Team of the Year" and came out on top as the winning team of the YONEX Northeast Collegiate Team Championships hosted by Boston Badminton.

2015: we took home our second consecutive win of the YONEX Northeast Collegiate Team Championships hosted by Boston Badminton and our First Place win at the IBC Autumn YONEX Northeast Collegiate Championships in Edison, New Jersey.

2016: We are again the 1st place winners of the YONEX Northeast Collegiate Team Championships, making this our 3rd year in a row as defending champions.

2022: We are the 2nd place winners of the Division 2 - Durabird Eastern Collegiate Team Badminton Championships, Spring 2022. 

2024: Advanced to D1 in Maryland at the Yonex Mid-Atlantic Collegiate Tournament after becoming D2 champs!

*Check out our mentions on TEAM USA!*

Club Feature

YONEX Northeast Collegiate Team Badminton Championships
summary: The Drexel Badminton Club provides an environment for badminton enthusiasts to practice and play competitively and recreationally. Players can share their interests and technically learn about the sport. We welcome players of all levels!

name: Drexel Club Squash
description:
The Drexel Coed Club Squash team is a club sport for students interested in learning and playing squash. Players have the opportunity to hit with fellow students in a recreational and competitive level. Open to all skill levels (especially beginners), practice includes warm ups, drills, games, and matches against other collegiate teams. Practice is Monday's and Wednesday's from 7:00 to 9:00 pm in the DAC lower squash courts, new members welcome.

NEW MEMBERS: Join our Discord through our linktree or email mjk483@drexel.edu with subject line "Club Squash Interest" for more information.
summary: This club intends to further promote the sport of squash at Drexel University by giving students the opportunity to further develop their abilities and participate in a competitive manner below a Varsity level.

name: Club Water Polo
description: The Drexel Water Polo Club competes in Collegiate Water Polo Association's Mid Atlantic Division in the Fall and in American Water Polo's Main Line League in the spring. All prospective members must be able to pass a swim test, but no water polo experience is required.
summary: The Drexel Water Polo Club competes in Collegiate Water Polo Association's Mid Atlantic Division in the Fall and in American Water Polo's Main Line League in the spring. Must able to pass a swim test, but no water polo experience is required.

name: Drexel Men's Club Volleyball
description:
General Information:

We have practice two nights per week for 2 hours each practice. We start playing Week 1 of Drexel's Fall Quarter and are done playing after Nationals (End of Spring Quarter). During Fall Quarter, we play in pre-season tournaments and scrimmages hosted by other collegiate men's club volleyball teams. During Winter Quarter, we participate in the Mid-Atlantic Collegiate Volleyball Conference (MACVC) for our regular season. We play everyone in our division once in Best of 5 format matches and two out-of-division club teams in Best of 3 format matches. During Spring Quarter, we participate in the MACVC Championships (aka Regionals) if we qualify for it based on our regular season results. We then participate in the 3-day long National Collegiate Volleyball Federation (NCVF) Volleyball Championships (aka Nationals).

 

Practices:

Fall Practice Schedule: 

Tuesday 8:30 PM - 10:45 PM

Thursday 7:30 PM - 9:00 PM

 

Club Sports Eligibility Requirements:

1. Join the Drexel University Club Sports DragonLink page (https://drexel.campuslabs.com/engage/organization/clubsports)

2. Complete the 2025-2026 Club Sports Participation Agreement. Found in the forms section on the Drexel University Club Sports DragonLink page once you are a member.

3. Complete ATS online health profile by following the instructions on the last page of the participation agreement.

If you did not complete a participation agreement and the required health forms on the ATS system, YOU WILL NOT BE ELIGIBLE TO PRACTICE.

 

Starting/Backup Positions:

There are tryouts for the team. The better players will be given more playing time in practices and be brought to travel for the games and tournaments to achieve the best possible results for Drexel. The starters and backups are decided by the coach based upon their skill, dedication, and team chemistry. Starting and backup positions are never locked, so do not be discouraged if you are not a starter or backup right away. We currently have an A and B team available.

 

Team Dues:

Dues for the 2025-2026 academic year have yet to be decided. Dues are necessary to help the team pay for games, tournaments, and league dues. A portion of each players dues will go towards their own jerseys and shorts. You will NOT be allowed to play with the team until dues are paid. ALL players are required to pay dues. 

 

Other Information:

Check out our Instagram and YouTube channel by clicking on the respective icon below. Also, check out the team's photos for this season.

 

2025-2026 season:

Coaches:

If you are interested in being a volunteer coach for the 2025-2026 season, please contact the President for more details about the team and required Drexel Club Sports coach training.

New Players:

If you are an incoming freshman, graduate student, or transfer student or a current student that is interested in joining the team for the 2025-2026 season, please contact the President with any questions you have about the team and season. Additionally, you can fill out the following interest form: 

https://forms.gle/wynpLMUDrs2gMPjA9
summary: This club is aimed to create a constructive and friendly environment where students can compete and learn volleyball at a competitive level.  The team will represent Drexel University in collegiate level volleyball leagues and events.

name: Drexel Women's Club Volleyball
description:
General Information: 

We will have practice two nights a week for two hours during the Fall and Winter Quarters. Practices will start in week one of the Fall Quarter, which will begin in an open gym format. In the 2nd week of the fall term, we will host tryouts (more information below). Tryouts will be held on a need basis during the Winter Quarter if necessary. Each quarter, we will compete in 2-3 tournaments in the North East Women’s Volleyball Club League comprised of schools throughout the region held primarily on Saturdays or Sundays. The club is best for experienced players who played at a competitive level during high school; we will not be able to accommodate inexperienced players. The club utilizes Drexel’s primary athletic facility - the Daskalakis Athletic Center - for practices and games.

 

Fall and Winter Quarter Practice Schedule:

Tuesdays and Thursdays in the Rec Center from 6-7:30pm.

 

Club Sports Eligibility Requirements:

1. Join the Drexel University Club Sports DragonLink page (https://drexel.campuslabs.com/engage/organization/clubsports)

2. Complete the 2025-2026 Club Sports Participation Agreement. Found in the forms section on the Drexel University Club Sports DragonLink page once you are a member.

3. Complete the ATS online health profile by following the instructions on the last page of the participation agreement.

If you did not complete a participation agreement and the required health forms on the ATS system, YOU would NOT BE ELIGIBLE TO PRACTICE.

 

Tryout Information:

Club Sports has announced that we are allowed to do tryouts this year. Our goal is to have no more than 18 girls on the team each year. Our coaches will decide these players and will be based on skill, commitment, and seniority. We will provide more information on how tryouts will go during our information session during the first week of the term and through email. 

 

Team Dues:

Dues are necessary to pay for tournament fees, league dues, and fun activities hosted throughout the 2025-2026 academic year. All players are required to pay dues each year, even if you are a returning player. Dues amount will be disclosed during our information session. If you have trouble paying these dues, don't hesitate to contact the President, and we will work something out for you.

 

New Players:

If you are an incoming freshman, graduate student, transfer student, or a current student interested in joining the team for the 2025-2026 season, please contact the President with any questions about the team and season duvolleyball@gmail.com.

 

Coaches:

If you are interested in being a volunteer coach or a paid coach for the 2025-2026 season, please contact the President for more details about the team and required Drexel Club Sports coach training before the season begins in September.
summary: The Drexel Women's Volleyball Club competes intercollegiately within the North East Women's Volleyball Club League comprised of schools throughout the region. The league features tournaments throughout the fall and winter.

name: Upsilon Pi Epsilon
description:
The Upsilon Pi Epsilon Association was founded at Texas A&M University in 1967 for those students and faculty who exhibit superior scholastic and professional achievement in the computing science curriculum. It remains the only National Honor Society for the computing sciences and is recognized as such by the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) and IEEE Computer Society. In 1997, Upsilon Pi Epsilon was admitted as a member of the Association of College Honor Societies - the parent organization for all academic honor societies in North America.

Drexel University was chartered in 1989 as the fifth chapter of the state of Pennsylvania to the Association by the Executive Council of Upsilon Pi Epsilon.

The mission of the Chapter shall be the promotion of high scholarship and original investigation in the various fields of the computing and information disciplines.
summary: The only National Honor Society for the computing sciences recognized as such by the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) and IEEE Computer Society. Drexel University was chartered in 1989 as the fifth chapter of the state of Pennsylvania.

name: Drexel Electric Racing
description:
At Drexel Electric Racing we design and construct a high-performance, all-electric racecar for use in the annual Formula SAE Electric competition. You can find more information regarding the competition here: https://www.sae.org/attend/student-events/formula-sae-electric/

We are open to all majors and backgrounds. If you would like to get involved, please send a email to FormulaEV@drexel.edu or Teams message president Shane Smith at svs67@drexel.edu.

Note that random join requests will not be accepted due to the required safety paperwork needed beforehand. Please email us and we will be more than happy to get you set up to become an official member of the team!
summary: At Drexel Electric Racing we design, build, and race a formula-style electric racecar for the yearly Formula SAE Electric competition. Email FormulaEV@drexel.edu or svs67@drexel.edu to get started.

name: Sigma Beta Rho Fraternity, Inc.
description:
Our pillars of Society, Brotherhood, and Remembrance were established upon the formation of the Alpha Chapter at the University of Pennsylvania in Philadelphia, on August 16, 1996. Our founders realized the unique promise of a Greek-lettered organization that instills positive values amongst its members, provides an avenue for leadership, and promotes self-betterment.

 

The Drexel University chapter of Sigma Beta Rho was founded in the Spring of 2002, and currently has over 20 members from all different walks of life.

 

Take the road less traveled.
summary: Our pillars of Society, Brotherhood, and Remembrance were established upon the formation of the Alpha Chapter at the University of Pennsylvania in Philadelphia, on August 16, 1996.

name: Drexel University Women's Rugby Club
description:
Drexel Women's Rugby is a no-cut squad that welcomes all interested athletes, regardless of skill level or knowledge of the game. We accept and support ALL members of the LGBTQ+ community, especially those identifying as nonbinary or transgender. We want to create a safe and inclusive environment for all while playing the sport we love. No experience is required, we will teach you everything you need to know! New faces welcome for fall & spring seasons!

Practices are TBD based on team/space availability. Games on Saturdays.  

Drexel Women's Rugby emphasizes dedication, fitness, and camaraderie. DWR competes against other collegiate women's teams in the MARC (Mid-Atlantic Rugby Conference).
summary: Drexel Women's Rugby is a no-cut squad that welcomes all interested female athletes, regardless of skill level or knowledge of the game. No experience is required, we will teach you everything you need to know! New faces welcome for fall & spring seasons

name: Drexel University Emergency Medical Services
description:
WHAT IS DREXEL EMS? CLICK HERE!

DUEMS LINKTREE + MEMBERSHIP INTEREST FORM

--------------------------------------------------------

Drexel University EMS is a volunteer student organization that provides emergency medical care to Drexel University's Main Campus and the immediate community in University City, Philadelphia.​ Click the link above for an informational overview. We are licensed as a QRS agency by the state of Pennsylvania and Gold-Tier recognized by NCEMSF.

We accept PA EMTs, Out of State EMTs, NREMTs and AHA BLS CPR-certified students into our membership. The minimum required certification for membership is an AHA Basic Life Support CPR certification. If you are interested in joining but don't have an AHA BLS/CPR certification, Drexel EMS and the Drexel University CNHP provide this course on a regular basis through our certified CPR Instructors. Fill out the CPR Class Interest Form to be sent information on our classes directly, including sign up links and a $30 discount code!
summary: Drexel EMS is a volunteer student organization that provides on-site emergency medical care and 911 response services to Drexel University's campus and the immediate community.

name: Drexel Philosophy Club
description:
Do you consider yourself to be a deep thinker? Have you ever found yourself contemplating your existence at 2am and wondering what the meaning of life is? Would you consider yourself to be an ethical individual or do you think that we’re all just specks floating around without any true purpose other than to act according to society’s standards or trends? If you ever want to find out the answers to any of these questions or if they personally resonate with you, Drexel’s Philosophy Club might be a good place to start! 

Philosophy is for everyone! Meaning “love of wisdom” in Greek, our weekly discussion-based meetings offer in-depth talks about a wide range of topics relating to philosophy and relevant issues in the world today. The underlying mission of the Drexel Philosophy Club is to encourage our members to think critically, expose individuals to novel ideas and perspectives, and answer meaningful questions about our world and ourselves to develop a better understanding of them. We hope that you come join us in our endeavor to think deeper and more critically!
summary: Philosophy isn't just for Philosophy majors. It's for people who ask questions. It's for people who like thinking. It's for you. We talk about a wide range of practical issues, generally once a week. Come check us out!

name: Formula SAE
description:
Drexel Formula SAE is an organization open to students of all backgrounds interested in designing, building, and racing a Formula SAE race car for an annual, international competition.

Contact club president Richard Lindes at rdl57@drexel.edu or fsae-info@drexel.edu if you have questions or would like to get involved.
summary: Drexel Formula SAE is an organization open to students of all backgrounds interested in designing, building, and racing a Formula SAE race car for an annual, international competition.

name: American Chemical Society Drexel Student Affiliates
description:
Drexel ACS provides and sustains a community within and around the chemistry department by communicating the joys of chemistry at various levels. We accomplish this mission by hosting events that provide educational, creative, social, and professional resources and activities.

Follow us on Instagram & join our Discord!
summary: Drexel ACS provides a community based in chemistry which offers educational, creative, social, and professional resources and activities!

name: Drexel Chess Club
description:
The Drexel Chess Team was formed in 1972 and has continued to be active on both the club and team levels. Members of the club consist of a wide range of skill levels, and the team has participated in tournaments such as the World Amateur Team East, the Greater Philadelphia Open, the Liberty Bell Open, and a variety of intercollegiate matches in the Philadelphia area. All players who are interested in chess are welcome, regardless of ability level, and we really enjoy teaching people the game for the first time if you haven't played before.

We currently meet in person Monday and Wednesday from 5:00-7:00. We can be found in Creese next to the Dragon Card Office.

Join our Discord for the most updated information: https://discord.gg/8v3Buddm7r 


Email: chess@drexel.edu or chess.drexel@gmail.com
Website: http://www.dusers.drexel.edu/~chess/
Lichess.org Team: https://lichess.org/team/drexel-chess-club
Official Chess.com Club: https://www.chess.com/l/drexeldragons
Unofficial Chess.com Club: https://www.chess.com/club/drexel-chess-club

We also stream events occasionally on twitch.tv/drexelchess
summary: Anyone in the Drexel community with an interest in playing chess is welcome, from beginner to master level. We function both at the club and the team level for playing in local, state and national tournaments.

name: Drexel University Chapter of the Society of Physics Students
description:
Our goals are the advancement and diffusion of the academic knowledge of physics; the encouragement of interest in physics throughout the local community; the introduction of students to professional societies; fellowship, and collaboration.

Our group is open to any and all with an interest in, or even just curious about, physics.
summary: Our goals are the advancement and diffusion of the academic knowledge of physics; the encouragement of interest in physics throughout the local community; the introduction of students to professional societies; fellowship, and collaboration.

name: Drexel Dandiya
description:
Drexel Dandiya (est. 2006) is the first ever collegiate Garba-Raas team to represent Philadelphia, Pennsylvania and the Tri-State area. Drexel Dandiya is the vision of 18 Drexel University students who hope to put Dragons on the map. We wish to join Drexel with our surrounding community and advocate Gujarati, East-Indian, and South Asian culture to our region and soon the nation.

The purpose of this organization shall be to hope to revive South Asian cultural awareness through a combination of traditional and modern Gujarati folk dance styles. Our team will not focus on a single style of Gujarati folk dance, but will incorporate many different sectors of dance including Garba, Raas, and original Folk Dance. Dandiya plans to raise attention towards South Asian culture with plans to participate in various cultural events and competitions across the University, surrounding community, and the nation.
summary: Drexel Dandiya strives to promote traditional South Asian Culture among the Drexel University community. We are a family of dancers with a soul full of passion for garba/raas and dedication to win.

name: Drexel Economics Society
description:
The Drexel Economics Society seeks to broaden the understanding of economic fundamentals amongst students of all disciplines through educational, professional, and community-building events and with our presence on campus. It strives to encourage people to explore the various paths available within Economics as a career choice and aims to educate the University population about Economics as an emerging option of study. DES also serves as the bridge connecting students to department faculty, promoting research opportunities, and helping students prepare for Graduate School. Whether you're majoring in Economics or just interested in the facets of Economics that appear in your everyday life, DES invites you to join our community to maximize your marginal utility during your time at Drexel! :)

 

To stay updated about all future events, please join our Discord server: discord.gg/vaD47UvErA and/or follow our Instagram: instagram.com/DrexelEconomicsSociety
summary: The Drexel Economics Society seeks to broaden the understanding of economic fundamentals amongst students of all disciplines through educational, professional, and community-building events and presence on campus. Ready to join?

name: Community Student Association
description: The Community Student Association (CSA) is a support network for all commuter and off-campus students. We work towards assisting these student populations with their adjustment to the university environment, create and encourage attendance at engaging campus programming and serve as an informational source to those that do not live on campus. For more information or to join our next meeting, e-mail us at commstudent@drexel.edu, or join our discord: https://discord.gg/KDbPWzG2Vf.
summary: The Community Student Association (CSA) is designed to create programming that supports commuter and off-campus students.

name: Beta Beta Beta Biological Honor Society
description: TriBeta is a biological honor society that promotes the advancement of scientific research and cultivates an appreciation for biological knowledge, with a focus on creating a community amongst its members and the faculty.
summary: TriBeta is a biological honor society that promotes the advancement of scientific research and cultivates an appreciation for biological knowledge, with a focus on creating a community amongst its members and the faculty.

name: National Organization for Business and Engineering - Drexel Chapter
description: The goal of the National Organization for Business and Engineering (NOBE) is to bring driven Drexel students with interests in both business or engineering together and enrich their college experience through interactive events, networking, and shared knowledge. We strive to establish long-lasting relationships with our members and alumni in the hopes of providing our members with an understanding of how business and engineering are essential to solve real-life problems. Through our dedication, ambition, and welcoming community, NOBE aims to use our network of alumni, students, and corporations to influence tomorrow's workforce - molding them into the leaders of tomorrow.
summary: The goal of the National Organization for Business and Engineering (NOBE) is to bring together students with interests in both business and engineering to corporate and academic institutions that promote the integration of these fields into one.

name: Drexel Jhalak
description:
Drexel Jhalak is an all-girls, competitively successful fusion dance team on campus. We express pride and joy in our backgrounds by practicing various South Asian and Western dance styles, from Bollywood to hip-hop. Anyone is welcome to join!

For more info, visit us on Instagram:@drexeljhalak
summary: Drexel Jhalak is an all-girls, competitively successful fusion dance team on campus. We express pride and joy in our backgrounds by practicing various South Asian and Western dance styles, from Bollywood to hip-hop.

name: Drexel Players
description:
The Drexel Players is a student theatre organization that works to create fully student-produced theatrical productions and to promote the love that the Philadelphia Theatre Community omits on a daily basis. As an organization, we strive to provide Drexel students with an opportunity for artistic engagement through the promotion of creative and interdisciplinary thought. 

The Players seek to engage the student body through social theatrical events and by providing opportunities to work professionally in all aspects of performance and production. This includes but is not limited to acting, directing, designing, costuming, lighting design, set construction, sound design, public relations, and general management. 

Finally, the Players utilize their artistic experience and professional connections to provide a strong footing for students to broaden their knowledge and involvement in Philadelphia Theatre and the theatre community as a whole.
summary: The Drexel Players are a student theatre organization that works to produce student written work, engage the student body through seeing shows & taking part in performances, and broaden students' knowledge & involvement in the Philadelphia theatre.

name: Undergraduate Student Government Association
description:
Mission: The USGA exists to fairly represent the interests of the Drexel Student Body and to foster relations between students and the University community at large. The USGA seeks to advocate on behalf of the student body by conducting student outreach and facilitating student involvement.

Vision: The USGA works to create an equitable student experience where all students feel that their voice is heard and that they are fairly represented to the administration, whose policies directly impact the student body.

____________________
