Attend a Joint Session of the USGA. Joint Session is where all members of the USGA meet to update one another on current work as well as to debate new proposals. Joint Sessions are held on Mondays at 7 PM EST and are open to the public with prior approval. Please email usga@drexel.edu for more information. 

____________________

Elections: Representatives must be elected to the organization either through an external or internal election process. Such elections take place at various times throughout the academic year. If you are interested in representing the USGA, please email usgaelections@drexel.edu for more information.
summary: The Undergraduate Student Government Association (USGA) is Drexel University's sole undergraduate student government and works to advocate on behalf of the betterment of the Drexel student experience.

name: Dragon Climbers
description:
Dragon Climbers is an organization aimed at providing Drexel students with the means necessary to experience the joy of rock climbing. If you have any questions about the club, please come to the Climbing Wall from 8-10pm on Tuesday or Thursday, ask in our Discord Server, or DM us on Instagram.

------------------

Please note that you must have had a boulder orientation at the URec Rock Wall to attend club hours, which are not given during these times.

------------------

Add yourself to our Roster!

Join our Discord Server!

Follow us on Instagram!

Subscribe to our YouTube channel!

Visit our LinkTree!
summary: Dragon Climbers is an organization aiming at providing Drexel students with the means necessary to experience the joy of rock climbing.

name: Phi Sigma Pi - National Honor Fraternity
description: Phi Sigma Pi is a gender-inclusive national honor fraternity that is unique in its combination of an honor society and a social fraternity. We are a diverse campus organization that fuses a tripod of ideals - Scholarship, Leadership, and Fellowship - into a wide variety of events and opportunities for our members. Ranging from academic programs and leadership opportunities to service projects and social gatherings, our activities celebrate the diversity of our members and promote the qualities of involvement and brotherhood. Our members are bright, motivated students who come from diverse backgrounds and a range of majors, and are often involved members of other organizations on campus.
summary: Phi Sigma Pi is a gender-inclusive national honor fraternity that is unique in its combination of an honor society and a social fraternity. It is a diverse campus organization that focuses on a tripod of ideals: Scholarship, Leadership, and Fellowship.

name: Unplugged Gaming Organization
description:
The purpose of the Unplugged Gaming Organization is twofold: to bring tabletop game enthusiasts together through diverse gaming events; second, to foster a sense of community between people through the joy of tabletop games. In doing these two tasks, we aim to bring the students of Drexel Campus together in a fun and friendly environment where people can unplug, make friends, and have new experiences.

Check out our discord server for more information!

https://discord.gg/MwHfs8ZSGq
summary: Social organization for board game lovers, card game enjoyers, TTRPG players, wargamers, and everyone who likes to have fun.

name: American Marketing Association Drexel
description: AMAD is a student-led organization that provides academic and professional growth opportunities to Drexel students. From regional and national competitions to community service and networking opportunities, AMAD wants their members to get involved in as much as possible. We have several events per quarter with speakers from large corporations to small start-ups, as well as professional development workshops. Our goal is to make our members competitive candidates when looking for co-ops or post-graduate full-time jobs. AMAD welcomes students from any major or background, as we believe marketing is used in all aspects of life!
summary: The American Marketing Association Drexel aims to further students’ knowledge of all applications of marketing, while also providing the extracurricular opportunities needed to make students competitive in today’s job market.

name: Alpha Chi Rho
summary: Alpha Chi Rho is an IFC fraternity. Located on Greek Row, our chapter house is a meeting place for our brothers. As a chapter, we pride ourselves on our active role in the surrounding community, vibrant social life, and a strong sense of brotherhood.

name: Sigma Phi Epsilon
description:
Want to learn more about SigEp? Fill out our interest form here: https://linktr.ee/sigep_drexel

Are you an incoming freshman or a rising sophomore? Apply for our $2,000 scholarship here: https://forms.gle/AGRJti6i1m4NfeVTA

 

Sigma Phi Epsilon strives year-round to recruit members that will benefit immediately from all leadership, scholarship, athletic, and brotherhood opportunities provided, as well as add involvement into a proven-solid organization committed to building balanced men. Today, the Fraternity as a national organization has grown to more than 300,000 lifetime members, including 15,200 undergraduates on 241 campuses in the United States. SigEp is currently the largest fraternity in terms of undergraduate enrollment. While we continue to actively seek our strongest candidates for membership, we also provide numerous opportunities for students to get involved and experience what SigEp represents here at Drexel University.

Our chapter uses the Balanced Man principle as our guide to expanding membership. We like to define our balanced men as individuals that display the very principles of the term ‘Balanced Man’: a scholar, an athlete, a gentleman, and most importantly, a leader.
summary: Want to learn more about SigEp? Fill out our interest form here: https://linktr.ee/sigep_drexel Sigma Phi Epsilon (SigEp) is a social college fraternity that was founded on three principles: Virtue, Diligence, and Brotherly Love.

name: Theta Chi
description: Theta Chi Fraternity is one of the oldest and largest men's fraternities in North America today. Our past is indeed proud, reflecting a society founded by two young idealistic cadets. For 46 years, Theta Chi was but one chapter, Alpha at Norwich University. Our growth from 1902 and over the next century is a tribute to the standards, ideals, and objectives of those cadets and over 191,000 men who have followed them and taken the vows of Theta Chi Fraternity. Those ideals and standards are as attractive and as meaningful today as in 1856. Our growth and expansion demonstrate that there are many thousands of young men who join our chapters and colonies each year to share in that brotherhood.
summary: Theta Chi Fraternity was founded in 1856 based on the ideal of the helping hand. No brother will be able to say he failed because he didn't have the help of another brother.

name: Drexel University Rugby Football Club
description: DURFC is an organization committed to the sport of rugby. There are two seasons throughout the year; the Fall and Spring. If you're interested in playing rugby, we want you! Experience does not matter. We are a group who wants to win rugby games and have fun doing it. Request to join the organization for more information.
summary: DURFC is an organization committed to the sport of rugby. There are two seasons throughout the year; the Fall and Spring. If you are a male undergraduate at Drexel University, we want you! Experience does not matter. We are a group who wants to win.

name: Drexel Ultimate
description: Our purpose is to offer a competitive and fun experience to all students interested in playing ultimate. As most students joining the team have never played the sport competitively it is important that we have the leadership present to teach our new members. As always we strive to make sure everyone on the team is enjoying their experience while also trying to advance as far as possible in the college series. Through a host of tournaments and weekly training sessions, we will advance our skills as a team.
summary: Our purpose is to offer a competitive and fun experience to all students interested in playing ultimate.

name: Graduate Student Association
description:
The Graduate Student Association (GSA) is both the governing body for graduate students at Drexel and oversees the recognized graduate student organizations registered with Campus Engagement within Student Life.


About Us

The Graduate Student Association (GSA) serves as the governing body and planning board for graduate students in face-to-face quarter programs and recognized graduate student organizations at Drexel University. In collaboration with Campus Engagement within Student Life, the GSA advocates for all graduate students, including those in online and semester programs, working closely with the Graduate College and campus partners to enhance the overall graduate student experience.

Each year, the GSA Executive Board Members are nominated and elected by graduate students in the spring and serve from July 1 to June 30. The GSA, along with recognized graduate student organizations, organizes a wide range of programs and events, catering to the diverse interests of the graduate student community. These events foster connections among students, postdocs, alumni, faculty, and professional staff across various academic disciplines.

From research symposiums, academic panels, and interdisciplinary workshops to social activities like ski trips, white water rafting, and happy hours, the GSA offers something for everyone. These activities create a vibrant and inclusive environment that enriches the Drexel graduate student community and supports their academic and social growth.

Goals

  1. Bridge Communication: Act as the connecting link between graduate students, Student Life, the Graduate College, and University leadership, ensuring open dialogue and collaboration.
  2. Student Advocacy: Engage with the graduate student body to understand their needs better and actively champion their interests and concerns.
  3. Accessible Communication Channels: Establish clear and easy-to-use communication platforms where graduate students can voice their opinions, concerns, and suggestions.
  4. Community Engagement: Foster a sense of community among graduate students through regular updates, social media interactions, and a variety of events—both in-person and virtual—focusing on personal, professional, academic, cultural, and social growth.
  5. Collaboration with Organizations: Partner with IGSA, USGA, and other student groups to enrich student life at Drexel, creating a more vibrant and supportive environment.
  6. GSA Expansion: Encourage more graduate students to participate in GSA committees and the senate, helping to grow the organization’s reach and impact.
  7. Service Culture: Promote a spirit of service among students, encouraging them to contribute to the greater Philadelphia community through various initiatives.
  8. Inclusive Excellence: Ensure that all GSA activities and events uphold the principles of justice, diversity, equity, and inclusion, creating a welcoming environment for all.

 

The GSA also advocates for and serves all graduate students across the University collaborating with the Graduate College and campus partners to enhance the graduate student experience.

 

From research symposiums, academic panels, interdisciplinary workshops, and writing boot camps, to ski trips, white water rafting, intramural sports, and happy hours, there is something for everyone in our vibrant Drexel graduate student community.
summary: The mission of this organization shall be to advocate the interests and address concerns of graduate students, to enhance graduate student life at the University, and to provide a formal means of communication between graduate students.

name: Mathematics Student Organization
summary: The Math Student Organization is committed to fostering a community of students with a common interest in mathematics so that they can learn about, appreciate, and have fun with all aspects of math.

name: TechServ
description:
TechServ is a student organization at Drexel University, founded in March 2002. We are comprised of students of all majors and are all volunteers. All Drexel students are welcome to join TechServ, regardless of prior technical knowledge. Our mission is to help bridge the Digital Divide in the Philadelphia area, which we primarily do by refurbishing computers and donating them to non-profit organizations and individuals in need. Our other activities include genius bars where we provide free technical support for the greater community, hosting student organization websites, and organizing fun events throughout the year.

All that is needed to participate is to join us here on DragonLink and just show up at any of our meetings or events!

 

Please check out our website for more information. In particular, the "Meetings" section is updated with each term's new meeting times, and has a map to our location.

Join us on Discord to get the latest announcements and information.
summary: TechServ helps bridge the Digital Divide by refurbishing and donating computers to non-profit organizations and individuals in need. We host Genius Bars where we help students and community members with computer problems. Any student can be a member!

name: TrebleMakers
description: The TrebleMakers formed in January of 2001 and is presently the only all-treble a cappella group at Drexel. The group hosts concerts with guest groups once per academic term and performs at other concerts, both on and off campus, even out of state. We also compete in competitions such as the International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella and sing at various fundraiser events. We take requests to sing at weddings, parties and any other events. All music is arranged and taught by our own group members. In short, the TrebleMakers is an organization made to promote the gathering and cooperation of girls with a shared passion for music and performance.
summary: The TrebleMakers are the only women-centered a cappella group at Drexel. We host concerts on campus, perform at events across the country, and compete in the ICCAs competition.

name: Harvest Christian Fellowship
description:
We are so happy that you have found us. As you take a look around, we hope you will catch a glimpse of what God is doing on Drexel campus.

Harvest Christian Fellowship is a student organization, branched from Grace Covenant Church, that seeks to raise and train spiritual leaders in the Drexel community. Whether you are a spiritual seeker just beginning to ask questions about God or a committed Christian wanting to deepen your faith, HCF is a place you can find spiritual help, hope, and encouragement. 

For more information on Grace Covenant Church, please visit our website! If you are interested in joining a family group for weekly Bible studies, need help finding a church, or have any other questions, please contact us at drexel@gracecovenant.net!
summary: Harvest Christian Fellowship (HCF) is a student organization, branched from Grace Covenant Church, that seeks to develop members spiritually and raise spiritual leaders in the Drexel community.

name: Association of Latino Professionals For America
description: ALPFA creates opportunities, adds value, builds relationships for its members, the community and its business partners while expanding Latino leadership in the global workforce. The organization connects individuals to an extensive professional network and endures that members acquire skills that will aid them throughout their careers. ALPFA is open to anyone who is interested in expanding their networking and communication skills. ALPFA is a fun way to get involved in an academic organization that can bring YOU to the top.
summary: ALPFA Drexel empowers minority students by helping them by connecting them with the resources of the ALPFA National, building relationships with companies, and creating new opportunities for them.

name: Asian Students Association
description: The full-time undergraduate student body of Drexel University, through the ratification of this constitution, created the Asian Students Association. Officers of the ASA are empowered to work on behalf of the students in order to promote the general interests of the Asian students; to represent the diversity of Asian American Pacific Islander community; to serve the community by advocating for issues in and around the area through the events, workshops, speakers, activities, etc. and, most importantly, to unite our cultural backgrounds.
summary: The full-time undergraduate student body of Drexel, through the ratification of this constitution, created ASA to promote the general interests of the AAPI community and unite all cultural backgrounds.

name: The Triangle
description:
First appearing on campus in 1926, The Triangle is the only independent newspaper at Drexel University. The paper is published online every other Friday year-round with a couple of special print editions per quarter. 

Being both financially and editorially independent of the University, all decisions about content and the paper's growth are made by the Editor-in-Chief and Editorial Board.

The Triangle covers campus and Philadelphia news, sports and entertainment as it affects the Drexel community. Each issue also contains opinion and editorial commentaries and comics.

We have a wide range of departments including News, Arts & Entertainment, Opinion, Sports, Graphics, Business, Web/IT, Copy Editing, Social Media, Photography and Recruitment.

Interested in joining? Visit https://www.thetriangle.org/join-the-triangle/

Visit us online at https://www.thetriangle.org
Follow us on Twitter! http://twitter.com/DrexelTriangle or Instagram! https://www.instagram.com/drexeltriangle/
Like us on Facebook https://www.facebook.com/drexel.triangle?fref=ts
summary: First appearing on campus in 1926, The Triangle is the only independent newspaper at Drexel University. Our completely student-run paper is published every other Friday year-round with a couple of special print editions per quarter.

name: Broad Street Baadshahz
description: The Broad Street Baadshahz is an all-male Bollywood-fusion dance team at Drexel University. Our team combines western styles of dance with South Asian dance forms including bhangra, bollywood, tollywood, and many more. We take pride in creating intricate performances and performing at competitions every year all over the nation. As one of the nations top teams, we pride ourselves in making non-dancers into dancers. With hardwork and dedication, our team has been able to entertain the Drexel community as well as the audiences at competitions. Every year we have a theme that we base our performance on. Using dance as our medium, we communicate important lessons to thousands of people. This year will be our 17th year as a team, and we are excited to show the nation what Drexel and the Broad Street Baadshahz have to show this year!
summary: The Broad Street Baadshaz are a fusion dance team at Drexel University who perform to bhangra, hip-hop, modern, bollywood, and more. This team has the passion and pride to go above and beyond their limits in order to entertain the public.

name: Drexel University Track Club
description:
Practice: 

Mon - Thurs: 5:15PM @ Steinbright Offices (on Arch Street Across from Buckley Field)

Drexel University Club Track (DUCT) is Drexel University's Cross Country and Track & Field team. It is one of the university's multiple club sports programs. DUTC is a NIRCA affiliated club team in the Mid-Atlantic collegiate division.

The club is a co-ed team and welcomes runners of all ability levels from beginners to serious competitors. Our member interests vary from long distance to mid-distance, and sprinters. 

Practice is held four days a week year round and races run seasonally. The club participates in Cross Country races in the Fall, Track meets in the Winter/Spring, and road races year round. 

Please email ekt48@drexel.edu to provide your contact information if interested in the club. We will add you to our "Discord" group chat for further updates!
summary: Drexel Track Club is the university's running, cross country and track & field club and is NIRCA affiliated in the Mid-Atlantic collegiate division.  The club is co-ed team and welcomes runners, sprinters, jumpers and throwers of all ability levels.

name: Drexel University Women's Club Basketball
description: The Drexel Women's Club Basketball Team plays other women's club basketball teams from local universities. It provides an opportunity for those interested in playing in semi-competitive, organized, intercollegiate basketball. We compete in tournaments year-round, practice twice a week, host fundraisers, and give back to our local community. We are looking for committed student athletes of all skill levels who want to develop their skills in a relaxed but competitive atmosphere.
summary: The Drexel Women's Club Basketball team plays other women's club basketball teams from local and neighboring universities. It provides an opportunity for those interested in playing in semi-competitive, organized, intercollegiate basketball.

name: Student Government Organization (SGO) of the School of Public Health
description:
All students of the Drexel University School of Public Health shall become members of the Student Government Organization upon enrollment.

The Purpose of the Organization is to:

  • Provide opportunities for meaningful contributions to and engagement in the field and discipline of public health
  • Promote and represent the rights, concerns, and interests of students in matters involving student-faculty and student-administration relations
  • Regulate the aspects of student activities delegated to the Student Government Organization by the faculty and administration
  • Develop and promote interest and pride in the school and uphold the school's and university’s academic standards
  • Act as a channel of communication and cooperation between and among the students, faculty, and administration concerning the opinions and attitudes of the student body in order to achieve mutual agreement toward the advancement of the school
  • Support aspects of collaboration amongst the students, faculty, administration and community by promoting intellectual, professional and social development and civic engagement
  • Promote matters, which may aid in the planning, supervising, and coordination of student activities in accordance with school and university regulations.
summary: This organization represents all students within the School of Public Health, serving their needs in a variety of capacities. The organization also provides opportunities for meaningful contributions to and engagement in the field of public health.

name: Chemical and Biological Engineering Graduate Student Association
description:
As a graduate student organization, we are aware of the instrumental roles played by graduate students in modern research institutions. We also understand that those roles can only be sustained by having an environment that is conducive to research and learning activities. It is thus the responsibility of Drexel's CBEGSA, to facilitate the realization of that environment.

CBEGSA sponsors graduate-student-initiated social events, discussions, and seminars that promote interactions between graduate students, staff, faculty, and other visiting researchers. These interactions will ultimately define the research direction and working atmosphere here, at Drexel University's Department of Chemical and Biological Engineering.

To incoming and current graduate students: we hope that you will join us in making the graduate experience a positive influence in the accomplishments of future Drexel engineers and scientists.

See you at the next CBEGSA event!
summary: Providing academic, social, and professional development events/opportunities for Chemical Engineering graduate students and others graduate associations.

name: Biology & BEES Graduate Student Association
description:
The Biology & BEES Graduate Student Association (BGSA) is an organization that falls under the auspices of the university-wide Graduate Student Association (GSA). Through this sponsorship, BGSA obtains funding to support the academic needs of biology graduate students while cultivating a strong social experience.

Our activities fall into four main areas:

1) Academics: BGSA represents graduate students with issues pertaining to coursework, teaching responsibilities, and degree requirements. We provide a forum for information exchange between graduate students, faculty and administration.

2) Research: BGSA frequently sponsors events that strengthen the research activities of graduate students. Importantly, many of our events are co-sponsored with other graduate student organizations and foster interdepartmental interactions and collaborations.

3) Community Outreach: Since its inception, BGSA has maintained a strong presence in the larger Philadelphia community. We frequently sponsor graduate student participation in volunteer work at local high schools, universities and other community organizations.

4) Social: Encouraging community building and relationships between the Biology and BEES departments and between graduate students, faculty, and staff.
summary: The BGSA is an organization dedicated to building a strong social and academic community through the unification of graduate students from the Department of Biology and the Department of Biodiversity, Earth, and Environmental Science.

name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics
description:
The American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics student chapter at Drexel University is a student organization that is dedicated to the continuing education of its members in the field of aerospace science and engineering. The club promotes academic workshops, professional development, exciting projects, and educational trips for its members.

Please join us on DragonLink to get involved with AIAA!

Also join our Discord for the most up to date information about meetings and other club news! https://discord.gg/kKF3PgTPxd
summary: The American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics student chapter at Drexel University is a student organization that is dedicated to the continuing education of its members in the field of aerospace science and engineering.

name: Drexel Biomedical Engineering Undergraduate Association
description:
The Drexel University Biomedical Engineering Undergraduate Association (DBUA) strives to encourage undergraduate participation in the BME community at Drexel and beyond, by organizing and participating in a large array of events, from lectures with distinguished professionals to volunteering in our community. DBUA seeks to both stimulate academic curiosity about biomedical engineering and promote camaraderie within its student population.

True to the interdisciplinary nature of our future field, membership is open to all majors at Drexel and because of this, DBUA has the potential to possess one of the largest memberships of any academically focused organization with a diverse array of experiences, career goals, and interests. Therefore, members of the organization are strongly encouraged to voice their opinion to the Executive Board on what events they would like to see organized. In order to provide the most beneficial events to the undergraduate BME community, DBUA will actively collaborate with other Biomed and Engineering-related organizations for events and workshops.
summary: DBUA strives to encourage undergraduate participation in the BME community at Drexel and beyond by organizing academic, philanthropic, and social events. In this way, DBUA hopes to help undergraduate students clarify their BME interests and career goals.

name: Drexel Men's Ice Hockey
description:
Founded in 1978, Drexel Ice Hockey has a rich tradition as one of the premier ice hockey programs in the Philadelphia area. Starting off as a club sport with less than 20 students rostered controlling their own budget and travel, the Drexel Ice Hockey experience has grown to over 60 student-athletes from an array of different backgrounds striving towards national prominence. Winning their first Eastern Collegiate Hockey Association (ECHA) Championship back in 2001, the Dragons have added 7 more championships to their trophy cabinet, including most recently an ESCHL Championship in 2024! Along with 7 ECHA Championships, the Dragons have qualified for 4 straight ACHA National Tournament appearances dating back to the 2016/17 season. After rising to national prominence, the Drexel Dragon family has grown far and wide to housing both a Division I ACHA team as well as a Division II ACHA Team. With the introduction of more student athletes came added responsibility, only for the Division II team to add one more trophy to the cabinet, winning the DVCHC Championship in their inaugural season. Since then, both the Division I and Division II team have moved conferences, competing against some of the top teams across the nation in Niagara University, the University of Pittsburgh, Stony Brook University, Syracuse University, and the University of Delaware.

Housing over sixty student-athletes, Drexel Ice Hockey provides the opportunity for players to engage in high level competition while also receiving higher education from a world-class comprehensive research institution. Dragons student-athletes have excelled in the classroom, maintaining a team average GPA of 3.3 or greater over the last 4 seasons, as well as being involved in the surrounding community as much as possible. Every day someone learns of the Drexel Ice Hockey experience is a day that will change their lives forever. Every person that has been apart of this organization understand what the meaning of family is and represents the blue and gold in a way no one else could. The Drexel Dragons Ice Hockey brand is an astronomically growing name that is something every wants to be apart of.
summary: Drexel Ice Hockey is a student-run organization showcasing the best talent the university has to offer in the game of Ice Hockey. One of the premier sports teams in Philadelphia, Drexel Ice Hockey has won 7 ECHA Championships, as well as an ESCHL title.

name: Drexel Veterans Association
description:
The Drexel Veterans Association was formed and exists to support and enhance student veteran’s educational experience. The association functions as a support structure to help veterans cope with the unique challenges associated with transitioning from military life to student life. Drexel Veterans Association works closely with all faculty and networks within the Drexel campus to connect veterans and dependents with the resources they need to succeed after the military and college. The association strives to assist student veterans and dependents of veterans in all aspects and provide them with the resources needed for their success.  

Drexel Veterans Association collaborates with The Office of Veteran Student Services, led by Rebecca Weidensaul,  Assistant Vice President for Student Life. Angela (Angie) Brennan is the organization's adviser and an associate teaching professor with Drexel’s Steinbright Career Development Center. Bryant Morris and Ryan Fonio are Drexel's School Certifying Officials and withhold a wealth of knowledge regarding VA Educational benefits and resources.  All of these wonderful individuals are strong advocates for military-associated students.  
 

It is our goal for all student veterans and dependents to understand that Drexel has worked hard to create a support system. DVA is widely known as a Drexel organization, while also being nationally recognized by Student Veterans of America (SVA). If there are any questions, concerns, or ideas to better your experience here at Drexel University, please contact your DVA President, Ben Hinish or any of the faculty below. Thank you! 


-Follow us on Discord for the most up to date events!

name: Drexel Game Developers Group
description:
Check out games made by our members!

 

The goals of the Drexel Game Developers are to:

  • To encourage the academic, social, and professional development of the University’s student body.
  • To promote fields involved with game development, including Computer Science, Digital Media, Art and Music, to the University’s student body.
  • To serve as the University’s student chapter of professional organizations related to the fields associated with game development.
  • To represent the interests of the University’s student body to the related departments, notably Computer Science and Interactive Digital Media, and the Colleges that administrate said departments.
  • To complement classroom learning of relevant skills, both intellectually and socially.
  • To assist students in professional development by encouraging projects, discussions, networking, and other forms of community involvement.
  • To create and foster a game development community within the University's student body.
  • To involve Alumni and create a constant connection between all Drexel game development students, past and future.

Join us on Discord: discord.gg/BmsFKFyBAC
summary: The goals of the Drexel Game Developers are to: To encourage the academic and social development of The University's student body. To promote fields involved with game development, including Computer Science and Digital Media

name: Drexel Women's Club Soccer
summary: Drexel's Women's Club Soccer team is all about continuing playing soccer at a competitive level but without all of the time commitment of a varsity sport. DUWCS focuses on the love of soccer, personal skill and fitness, team work, and fun.

name: Drexel University Men's Baseball Club
description:
Our program is part of the North Atlantic Region – East (NAR-East) in the National Club Baseball Association (NCBA). Aside from Drexel, The NAR-East is home to the University of Delaware, Penn State, West Chester, St. Joe's University,  and Temple University. 

The team participates in both the Fall and Spring seasons, with weekly practices throughout the Winter term. Our Fall season begins with Tryouts the first week of classes and ends the first week in November, with games being played pretty much every weekend. Throughout the winter, we utilize Drexel's "bubble" facility.

Every Spring Break, the team travels down to Florida (this year, to Panama City, FL) for the NCBA Swing into Spring Showcase. This is one of the highlights of our program and is a great way to build camaraderie. It also allows us to get a few more games under the belt before conference play starts up again in April. In 2019, Drexel went 4-1 in the showcase, which gave us great momentum entering the rest of the Spring season, where we won the North-Atlantic East Conference Championship for the 3rd straight year. (This trip is not mandatory, however, it is consistently players' favorite moment of the season, and always makes the team incredibly tight with one another.)

 

Conference play consists of a 3-game series against each other team in the North-Atlantic East Conference. League play is followed by the North Atlantic Regional Playoffs, which consists of three conference winners from the NAR-North, West, and East, as well as one at-large bid (Wild Card). Playoffs are structured as a double-elimination tournament, where the winner gets a chance to play in the NCBA World Series Tournament. The Dragons have several Conference Championships to their name but have yet to crack through the regional tournament to play for the World Series title. This year, we have a strong core group of players who are determined to play further into the summer and bring home a national title. 

 

The team does travel a good amount. Players can expect to be assigned a car (driven by a teammate) on the way to practices and games (Drexel does not provide transportation). We use a few different locations for practices. Mainly Buckley Recreational Field on Drexel's Campus (33rd St), Father Judge High School (where we also play our home games).

 

The team is mostly self-funded and completely self-run. We take it very seriously which translates to wins on the diamond. A player's commitment to his teammates is something we strongly stand by, which means time management with school and/or work is necessary. Of course, should specific circumstances arise, a player may miss practices/games, with proper communication to team leaders ahead of time.  

 

This year, we have a lot of roster spots to fill, so please reach out to President Martin Simpson Teplin or any other leadership member if you are interested in joining our program! 

President Martin Simpson Teplin: 720-481-0173, mfs88@drexel.edu

Vice President Ben Rosenfeld: 508-873-9991, br585@drexel.edu
summary: Drexel University Club Baseball is a student-run team that is very competitive in the NCBA North-Atlantic East Conference. We hold tryouts in September as school starts up again, so please reach out if interested (see contact info).

name: Drexel University Cycling Club
description: Drexel cycling is a competitive, as well as recreational bike team. We encourage students and alumni of all skill levels to join us on daily rides and workouts as we promote safe and enjoyable bike riding for all. As a team registered in the ECCC (Eastern Collegiate Cycling Conference), there are competitions throughout the year that include road and mountain biking, as well as track and cyclocross racing. Whatever your interests or skill level, there is something for any rider to enjoy as a part of Drexel Cycling
summary: Drexel cycling is a competitive, as well as recreational bike team. We encourage students and alumni of all skill levels to join us on daily rides and workouts as we promote safe and enjoyable bike riding for all.

name: Drexel African Students Association
description:
We are a vibrant mix of students, comprised of many different cultures, who share an interest in matters pertaining to Africa.

We seek to establish a community that is passionate about the continent's traditions and to create a sense of home for those who come from Africa to study at Drexel University.

We welcome all to the DASA family. Email us at drexeldasa@gmail.com or follow us on Instagram @drexel_asa to stay informed and to learn about all our upcoming events.
summary: The Drexel African Students Association exists as a platform to share knowledge about Africa by creating a visible and vocal African presence at Drexel. We also aim to ensure a smooth integration of our members into the American sphere.

name: Engineers Without Borders
description:
Drexel Engineers Without Borders (EWB-USA Drexel Chapter) branches off from the national organization EWB-USA. Thus we share similar interests and mission statements. Overall, our program here at Drexel aims to promote sustainable solutions for both domestic and international issues. We partner with communities to meet basic human needs and improve quality of life. 

The Drexel Chapter uses engineering skills and concepts to help our partner communities address their needs and to develop students into leaders that are equipped for challenges they will face throughout their careers. This allows students to use what is learned in the classroom on real-world engineering projects. Typically, many projects focus on access to clean drinking water, hygiene, and sanitation, as these needs are so basic to human life and are often lacking in underdeveloped parts of the world.
summary: EWB-USA, Drexel Chapter, aims to promote sustainable solutions for domestic and international issues. We partner with communities to meet basic human needs and improve quality of life. Join us to engineer change!

name: Delta Phi Epsilon
description: Delta Phi Epsilon's purpose is to provide a sisterhood experience rich with tradition, innovation, and opportunities for growth
summary: Delta Phi Epsilon's motto is "Esse Quam Videri" which means "be rather than to seem to be"; Our values are Justice, Sisterhood, and Love.

name: Alpha Sigma Alpha, Nu Nu Chapter
description:
Alpha Sigma Alpha is a women's organization that exists to promote high ideals and standards for its members throughout their lives by emphasizing balance among the four aims of intellectual, physical, social and spiritual development. Established on May 13th, 1925, the Nu Nu Chapter of Alpha Sigma Alpha is one of the oldest Greek organizations on Drexel University's campus.

The Nu Nu Chapter has won numerous national awards over our 90+ years of existence at Drexel University, including the Crown of Excellence, the top honor an Alpha Sigma Alpha collegiate chapter can receive. Nu Nu has also won multiple Four Star Chapter Awards, one of the most prestigious awards given by Alpha Sigma Alpha’s National Organization. In May 2014, the Nu Nu Chapter of Alpha Sigma Alpha received the 2013 Dean’s Cup, which is awarded to the top chapter in each of the three councils for excellence in the areas of academic excellence, sisterhood, leadership and service to the community.

On both the local and national levels, Alpha Sigma Alpha supports our philanthropic partners, the Alpha Sigma Alpha Foundation, Girls on the Run, and Special Olympics, through community service and charitable giving. 

In addition to supporting these organizations, the Nu Nu Chapter believes strongly in supporting each other, the Panhellenic, Interfraternal and Multicultural Councils as well as the greater Drexel community. We do this through attending each others' philanthropy events, fundraisers and socials. We also host open events like our annual Pasta Bake Off, where sisters volunteer to make a variety of pasta dishes and members of the Drexel community can come to eat for free!

Every member is a unique individual and contributes something different to our organization.  We strive to develop women who are able to lead and make a difference, whether it is by running an event, participating in a committee, or holding a position. ASA offers opportunities to build skills and friendships that will last a lifetime.
summary: Alpha Sigma Alpha is a women's organization that exists to promote high ideals and standards for its members throughout their lives by emphasizing balance among the four aims of intellectual, physical, social and spiritual development.

name: PRAGATHI- The Indian Graduate Student Association
description:
In 1990, Pragathi blossomed at Drexel University, a vibrant beacon for the Indian Graduate Student Association, weaving a tapestry rich in India’s cultural hues while crafting a nest, warm and familiar, for Indian students to call home. Today, it thrives as a pulsating heart of graduate life on campus, where calendars are painted with events as varied as cozy potlucks, cultural pageants, spirited sports, and elegant balls under the stars.

Embracing inclusivity and the dance of cultural exchange, Pragathi reaches out, hand in hand, with other student collectives, spinning a year-long carnival of events that bridge worlds. These are not merely celebrations but vibrant confluences where diverse streams meet, learn, and celebrate in unity, a mosaic of global camaraderie.

At the core of Pragathi, there lies a balance, delicate yet profound, between the rigor of academia and the warmth of social ties, curating spaces where connections blossom amidst laughter and memories. It is a belief, steadfast and true, that the weave of social fabric strengthens not just the spirit but fortifies the journey through academic realms.

So, we beckon you to embark on a voyage, to dive into the spirit of exploration and camaraderie that even Columbus sought in the 1500s. Join us in the spirited journey of Pragathi at Drexel, where each moment is a stanza in our collective odyssey, and be part of the vibrant fun that defines us.

 

 

Current Board Members of 2024-2025:

President: Swetha Thanabalan
Vice-President: Joshua Philips
Treasurer: Pooja Bandekar
Event Coordinator: Leena Saminathan
Media Manager: Suruchi Gokhale
General Secretary: Sushanth Mahalle
summary: Pragathi – The Indian Graduate Student Association was founded in 1990, with a vision of spreading awareness of India, its culture and rich heritage among the graduate community in Drexel while ensuring a homely atmosphere for Indian students.

name: Drexel Biomedical Graduate Association
description: The purpose of the DBGA is to improve the general welfare of Drexel Graduate students and the School of Biomedical Engineering, Science and Health Systems.  To achieve this goal, the DBGA will provide a forum for communication between students, faculty, and administrators.  In addition, the DBGA will increase awareness of the current state of biomedical engineering research and technological advancements in the medical field.    Meetings of the DBGA will encourage the sharing of knowledge by means of activities including, but not limited to: lectures, presentations, tours, community service and social activities.
summary: The purpose of the DBGA is to improve the general welfare of Drexel Graduate students and the School of Biomedical Engineering, Science and Health Systems.

name: Drexel Graduate Women in Science and Engineering
description: Drexel Graduate Women in Science and Engineering (DGWISE) is focused on expanding and enhancing the experiences of graduate women in Science and Engineering by organizing educational, social and community service events that will contribute to their personal and professional development.
summary: Drexel Graduate Women in Science and Engineering is focused on expanding and enhancing the experiences of all the women and non-binary graduates in Science and Engineering by organizing events that will contribute to their professional development.

name: Interfraternity Council
description: The Interfraternity Council is the governing body for the 12 fraternities that reside at Drexel University. We enhance the collegiate experience of Greek men through community service, philanthropy, continuing education, social, and athletic events. We use these ideals not only to strengthen our beliefs but also the beliefs of Drexel University and the City of Philadelphia.
summary: The Interfraternity Council is the governing body for the 15 fraternities that reside at Drexel University. We enhance the collegiate experience of Greek men through community service, philanthropy, continuing education, social, and athletics.

organizationType.name: Student Life Impact Organizations - CALENDAR YEAR

name: Women in Computing Society
description: The Women in Computing Society (WiCS) is an organization tailored to support women-identifying people in computing fields and students interested in technology. Its purpose is to support, recruit, and retain women pursuing a degree in the broad field of computing. WiCS connects its members with each other and the greater computing world through mentorship programs, social gatherings, guest speakers, workshops, and national and regional conferences. The ultimate goal is to empower women in technology fields to succeed and thrive in the Drexel community and beyond.  We are an official chapter of ACM-W.  This organization is open to anyone who supports women in tech!
summary: Drexel Women in Computing Society (WiCS) empowers women and gender minorities in tech through mentorship, community, and professional development. We host events, workshops, and projects that foster technical skills, leadership, and lasting connections.

name: Drexel University Club Tennis
description: Welcome to Drexel Club Tennis! We are a competitive traveling tennis team representing Drexel University in the USTA national collegiate club tennis league.
summary: Drexel Club Tennis is a competitive traveling tennis team. We participate in the USTA Tennis on Campus program.

name: Men's Club Lacrosse
description: This is the Men's Club Lacrosse Team NCLL Division 2, Liberty Conference. All are invited to join and play. If you are a beginner we are here to help teach you the game of Lacrosse. If you are an experienced player we are here so you can continue to play Lacrosse at a competitive level. We strive to create a bond between players with the sport of lacrosse while competing at a national level.
summary: This is the Men's Club Lacrosse Team NCLL Division 2, Liberty Conference. All are invited to join and play, including new and experienced players to continue playing lacrosse at a competitive level.

name: Drexel University Paintball Club
description:
The Drexel University Paintball Club was one of the first collegiate paintball teams established. Drexel Paintball provides the opportunity for students to compete in collegiate paintball. The Club competes in the Northeast Intercollegiate Conference. National champions in 2002, 2010, and 2019.

Our practices are on Sundays at The Playground Paintball Park, Mantua, NJ. 

The college season runs from September to April.

Contact us on Instagram or email if you are interested in learning more or coming to a practice. 

Disclaimer: Paintball markers are not allowed on campus per the Student Handbook. The University may pursue discplinary actions against anyone found to be in possession of a marker on campus and the club officers may pursue independent actions at the club level. It is your responsibility to contact an officer of the club for proper marker storage.
summary: The Drexel University Paintball Club was one of the first collegiate paintball teams established. Drexel Paintball provides the opportunity for students to compete in collegiate paintball. The Club competes in the Northeast Intercollegiate Conference.

name: Drexel Interiors Group
description:
Drexel Interiors Group focuses on giving Interior Design students the opportunity to connect with professionals, further their education and gain experience in the Architecture and Design industry. 

We will be hosting numerous professional development events such as guest speakers at meetings, tours of showrooms and firms within Philadelphia area, organized field trips to New York City and more. Students will also be able to participate in workshops to improve portfolios, revise resumes, and practice presentation skills.
summary: This organization serves as a resource for Interior Design students to ensure a steady transition into the professional design field.

name: Drexel University Black Student Union
description:
The mission of the Drexel University Black Student Union is to provide a community, a safe space, and a resource for Black students by uniting efforts for student and
community success
summary: DUBSU is a cultural organization that aims to promote unity, cultural competence, and campus involvement amongst the students, faculty, and staff of the African Diaspora within the university.

name: Delta Epsilon Psi Fraternity, Inc.
description: On October 1st, 1998, Delta Epsilon Psi was founded by those 18 individuals determined to mold their fraternity into a prominent organization not only in their campus, but also throughout the nation. Their ideals and principles would allow Delta Epsilon Psi to expand beyond the confinements of a stereotypical fraternity. Since there was no paradigm to steer them in the proper direction, they alienated the concept of conventional Greek life and implemented a new sense of giving back to the community.
summary: On October 1st, 1998, Delta Epsilon Psi was founded by those 18 individuals determined to mold their fraternity into a prominent organization not only in their campus, but also throughout the nation. Their ideals and principles would allow Delta Epsilon

name: Phi Sigma Sigma
description: Phi Sigma Sigma is a dynamic sisterhood of powerful women fostering uncompromising principles, igniting positive change, and embracing individuality. We aim to inspire the personal development of each sister and perpetuate the advancement of womanhood.
summary: Phi Sigma Sigma is a dynamic sisterhood of powerful women fostering uncompromising principles, igniting positive change, and embracing individuality. We aim to inspire the personal development of each sister and perpetuate the advancement of womanhood.

name: Tau Kappa Epsilon
description: Tau Kappa Epsilon is an undergraduate Fraternity of men that focuses on the deveolpment of leadership. We are a social Fraternity that is one of over 250 chapters world-wide. We are the longest standing student organization on Drexel University's campus, established in 1939. Over the seventy-one years of existence over 2,000 men have been initiated into the brotherhood. We are considered one of the elite TKE chapters in the nation, receiving 18 awards for our accomplishments.
summary: Tau Kappa Epsilon is an undergraduate Fraternity of men that focuses on aiding men in their mental, moral and social development for life. We are a social Fraternity that is one of over 230 chapters world-wide.

name: Delta Zeta
description:
We share a common purpose: to make life fuller, more meaningful, more rewarding. Within Delta Zeta, we call it enriching. To our members, it is the promise of friendship, personal development, and opportunity. To the community, we are valued citizens deeply committed to making a difference where we live. Our promise to our Sorority and our commitment to enrich the world are for a lifetime.

For more information about our chapter please check out our Instagram! @drexeldeltazeta
summary: We share a common purpose: to make life fuller, more meaningful, more rewarding. Within Delta Zeta, we call it enriching. To our members, it is the promise of friendship, personal development and opportunity. To the community we are valued citizens.

name: Drexel Women's Club Lacrosse
description:
A fun club lacrosse team! We play competitive, intercollegiate games in the fall and spring seasons!

We welcome players of all levels of experience, if you have any questions feel free to reach out to our president, Kathleen Stubbs (443-966-5250), or DM us on Instagram!

Practices during Fall Term will be held on Mondays 6:30-8:30 and Thursdays 7:00-8:30 at Vidas Field.
summary: A club lacrosse team for young women to play intercollegiately both fall and spring term.

name: Drexel Black Graduate Student Union
description: The mission of DBGSU is to foster an environment of intellectual, political, cultural, and social growth for those who want to connect with Black graduate and professional degree-seeking students.
summary: The mission of DBGSU is to foster an environment of intellectual, political, cultural, and social growth for those who want to connect with Black graduate and professional degree-seeking students.

name: Physics Graduate Student Association
description: The Physics Graduate Student Association hosts lunches and social events to promote a sense of community throughout our department, keep up-to-date with current physics research, provide students with an opportunity to practice public speaking in a low stress environment, and promote outreach within the wider Philly community.
summary: We promote communication within the physics community and our peers within the Drexel graduate community.

name: Drexel American Red Cross
description: Drexel American Red Cross provides students the opportunity to interact with the community through blood donation efforts, disaster awareness, and other community service events in collaboration with the American Red Cross Southeastern Pennsylvania Chapter and other student organizations and institutions. Furthermore, we provide our members with opportunities to receive first aid, CPR, and other emergency training.
summary: Drexel American Red Cross gives students the platform to interact with the community through blood donation efforts, disaster awareness, and other community service events in collaboration with the American Red Cross Southeastern Pennsylvania Chapter.

name: Chinese Students and Scholars Association at Drexel University
description: 德雷塞尔中国学生学者组织 （CSSAD） 成立于 2009 年 3 月，旨在为所有中国国际学生和学者提供支持，帮助他们快速适应德雷塞尔和美国的大学生活。CSSAD 倡导无私奉献、责任、创新和决心的核心价值观，为德雷塞尔大学的每一位中国学生和学者建立一个温暖的大家庭。在此期间，我们还通过在德雷塞尔校区举办文化活动和传统庆祝活动来促进中国文化和历史的多样性。此外，我们计划将德雷塞尔大学的中国学生和学者与费城地区其他大学的学生联合起来，为我们的同龄人创造更多机会。
summary: CSSAD established in March 2009, aiming to provide support to all Chinese students and scholars who are adjusting to the new environment as well as to the college life. CSSAD launches core values of selfless contribution, responsibility, and innovation.

name: Drexel University Construction Association
description:
The Drexel University Construction Association (DUCA) serves as the umbrella organization for Drexel’s student chapters of the Associated General Contractors, Associated Builders and Contractors, and the Construction Management Association of America.

DUCA is dedicated to advancing the professional growth of students pursuing studies in Construction Management, Real Estate, Engineering, and Architecture. Through networking events, guest lectures, seminars, site visits, and collaboration with Drexel faculty and industry professionals, DUCA connects students with leaders in the built environment.

The organization’s mission is to expand members’ exposure to the construction industry while showcasing the knowledge, talent, and capabilities of Drexel students to the professional community.
summary: The DUCA is dedicated to promoting and facilitating the professional development of Drexel University students concentrating in studies including Construction Management, Real Estate, Engineering, and Architecture.

name: Drexel Democrats
description: The purpose of the Drexel Democrats is to organize and mobilize support on campus for various local, state, and national Democratic Party candidates for political office, and expose students to various issues/views, and best civic engagement practices.
summary: The purpose of the Drexel Democrats is to organize and mobilize support on campus for various local, state, and national Democratic Party candidates for political office, and expose students to various issues/views, and best civic engagement practices.

name: Psi Chi Honor Society
description: Psi Chi is the International Honor Society in Psychology, founded in 1929 for the purposes of encouraging, stimulating, and maintaining excellence in scholarship and advancing the science of psychology. Membership is open to graduate and undergraduate students who meet the minimum qualifications. Psi Chi is a member of the Association of College Honor Societies and an affiliate of the American Psychological Association (APA) and the Association for Psychological Science (APS).
summary: Psi Chi is the International Honor Society in Psychology, founded in 1929 to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in scholarship and to advance the science of psychology. Membership is open to students who meet minimum qualifications.

name: Drexel Football Team (Improv Comedy)
description:
WE ARE AN AUDITION-BASED MEMBERSHIP.

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM: @dftimprov


Undefeated since 2005! The Drexel Football Team is a student-run improv group that performs free improvisational comedy shows for the Drexel community. All members are Drexel students - keep an eye out on social media outlets about shows. We perform both long-form and short-form improv.

Completely free. Completely made up on stage.

We hold auditions twice a year and post about it on social media! We do 1-2 shows per quarter, improv workshops, and even guest appearances!
summary: The Drexel Football Comedy Improv Team - Disappointing Sports Fans Since 2005 Love comedy? Love "Whose Line Is It Anyway?" Love "Make Some Noise"? Say no more! Come see The Drexel Football Team! Free long- and short-form improvised comedy at its best.

name: Weekend Warriors
description: Weekend Warriors is an Office of Campus Activities recognized group dedicated to providing the Drexel community with exciting opportunities to engage with the outdoor world. Everyone needs to take a break sometimes. Be it by relaxing on a leisurely hike or feeling the exhiliration of whitewater rafting, Weekend Warriors provides students with a change from the pressures of schoolwork. Our mission is to make the outdoors as accessible as possible for Drexel students and to train responsible and capable Trip Leaders to keep our trips safe, but fun. It is our mission to bring the outdoors to students that have never before realized the benefits that nature can bring.
summary: Weekend Warriors is an Office of Campus Activities recognized group dedicated to providing the Drexel community with fun opportunities to engage with the outdoors. Our mission is to make the outdoors accessible to all Drexel students.

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers
description: The American Institute of Chemical Engineers (AIChE) is a globally recognized organization for the chemical engineering profession. For over 100 years, AIChE has been working to distinguish and define chemical engineering as a profession and the student chapters hold the same goal. AIChE is open to all students with an interest in chemical engineering. We host social and professional events to further the interests of students interested in chemical engineering.
summary: The American Institute of Chemical Engineers is a globally recognized organization for the chemical engineering profession. For over 100 years AIChE has been working to distinguish and define chemical engineering as a profession.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
description:
Our mission: 

“To increase the number of culturally responsible black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community”

The purpose of DREXEL NSBE is:

  • To encourage the development of a technical and professional consciousness
  • To alleviate the problems of minority students that could deny them any opportunity to become acquainted and knowledgeable in areas of engineering and science; by representing the educational and social interests of minorities studying engineering and science at Drexel
  • To provide Academic support via tutoring and academic achievement programs
  • To provide resources and contact in Search for internships, co-ops, and full-time job opportunities offered by the top engineering companies in NSBE’s Job Placement Center
  • To offer access to various scholarships and grants (Many of our scholarships do not require a GPA)
  • To increase networking opportunities with other students and technical professionals in the disciplines of Engineering, Science, and Commerce & Engineering
  • To provide a vast number of programs that help prepare you for graduate school and your future career
  • To retain encouragement and increase the enrollment of minorities at Drexel
  • To promote a spirit of congeniality minority students
  • To provide a meaningful contact in their respective professions through corporate alumni affiliations
summary: Our mission: “To increase the number of culturally responsible black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community”

name: Global China Connection
description: Global China Connection is an international organization dedicated to promoting learning, communication, and growth among Chinese and non-Chinese students. A nonprofit student-run network of over 60 university chapters worldwide, GCC provides a valuable platform for cultural exchange and professional development for its members. For the most up-to-date information, please find us on Instagram @gccdrexel.
summary: Global China Connection (GCC) is a nonprofit and non-partisan organization that centers activations around cultural exchange for Drexel students interested in Chinese culture and the like. All students are welcome!

name: Panhellenic Council
description:
The Panhellenic Council (PHC) represents the five chapters of the National Panhellenic Conference that are chartered at Drexel University.

The mission of the governing council of the Drexel Panhellenic community is to cultivate genuine bonds and develop a positive environment where women are encouraged to excel academically, socially, and in leadership roles. We strive to promote the wellbeing, philanthropic endeavors, and civic engagement of women, and educational rights within our Drexel community, Philadelphia, and around the world.
summary: The Panhellenic Council (PHC) is the governing body of the five chapters of the National Panhellenic Conference that are chartered at Drexel University.

organizationType.name: Student Life Impact Organizations - CALENDAR YEAR

name: Drexel SIGGRAPH
description:
Organization focused on Digital Media and computer graphics.  We have student-run lectures on special topics within Digital Media, screenings of animated and effects-heavy movies, and are committed to a strong presence at the annual SIGGRAPH conference.

Connect with our community on Discord and Instagram and stay up to date on all our events!

Discord:https://discord.com/invite/6HMmSyK4FF

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/drexelsiggraph/
summary: Organization focused on Digital Media and computer graphics. We have student-run lectures on special topics within Digital Media, screenings of animated and effects-heavy movies, and are committed to a strong presence at the annual SIGGRAPH conference.

name: Drexel Sport Business Association
description:
The Drexel Sport Business Association (DSBA) was founded in 2018 and aims to be a unique opportunity for all Drexel students interested in working within the sports industry. With its foundation located within Drexel's Department of Sport Business, the DSBA believes it will find success reaching its primary goal: putting Drexel University on the map as having one of the top Sport Business programs in the country.

Each term, the DSBA plans to hold meetings and events giving membered students the opportunity to explore different facets of the sports industry and network with industry professionals.

The Drexel Sport Business Association is accepting to any students in the Drexel community interested in having a career in any area of business or the sports industry. Our primary goal is to help students prepare for a career in sports after graduating from Drexel, while continuously working to elevate the major at our university during that process.
summary: The Drexel Sport Business Association (DSBA) is an organization that strives to help its member students build their network within the sports industry through different professional development opportunities.

name: Alpha Pi Lambda
description:
Alpha Pi Lambda was founded on March 3, 1935 when a group of young men worked toward the creation of a new fraternity founded on their beliefs and principles. Such beliefs included our three core values; friendship, honor, and loyalty. Since 1935, Alpha Pi Lambda has remained a local fraternity.

Alpha Pi Lambda serves to promote fellowship, progress and leadership among the students of Drexel University. Through this experience an individual learns to adapt to the demands of society. The chapter involves itself in numerous activities. These include, philanthropy, scholarship, and social events. We are committed to the continuous interaction with the surrounding community.
summary: Local fraternity at Drexel University since 1935.

name: Delta Phi Omega Sorority, Incorporated
description:
Delta Phi Omega Sorority, Inc. was established on December 6, 1998 at the University of Houston by sixteen women from various backgrounds that came together with the common goal of uniting women among the South Asian community. We are one chapter affiliated with 3 schools: Drexel University, Temple University and Thomas Jefferson University. 

Our Founding Mothers envisioned a sorority based on five important pillars of loyalty, friendship, respect, sisterhood, and honesty. Our sisters continuously exemplify these values and we strive to instill them into everyone. We are unlike any other… we are truly a family and provide a home away from home. 

The Founders created the backbone of this sorority on the basis of the five pillars; Sisterhood, Respect, Loyalty, Honesty, and Friendship. They envisioned an organization that would highlight the diverse Indian culture through participation in community and social service projects.
summary: With over 2,600 members nationwide, Delta Phi Omega is the largest, strongest, and fastest growing sorority of its kind. As a sorority focused on service, social and academic endeavors, Delta Phi Omega touches base with each aspect of campus importance.

name: Iota Nu Delta Fraternity Inc., -- Gamma Chapter
description:
The Gamma Chapter of Iota Nu Delta was established on October 19, 1997 at Drexel University by four young men. Brothers strongly represent Drexel University.

An immeasurable strength of MIND, BODY, and SOUL came together to create a brotherhood that is the first of its kind. To this day, Iota Nu Delta has set the example for what a brotherhood should represent and has been often Imitated Never Duplicated. We work diligently to give back to our communities through various fundraisers and community services. We have built our legacy on unselfish devotion to others and unparalleled love and loyalty towards our brothers.

We started with 4 and today we stand over 100 strong. Brothers from all over the world, of all different backgrounds, religions and heritages make up the melting pot that is IND Philadelphia. No matter how different we are from one another we have one common goal in mind that we strive for each and everyday. That goal is to make Iota Nu Delta Fraternity, Inc. one of the most respected and most influential fraternities in the world.

Many men have mustered enough courage to travel down our path but only few have been able to prevail against the odds and become one of us. Quality over Quantity is our motto...Do you have the fire inside of you?

During the turbulent times of the early 90s, many minorities faced discrimination as well as ridicule for practicing their culture and essentially being different. South Asians specifically were often tormented by typical stereotypes that misrepresented their customs, culture, and their values. It was during this time that a group of eight dedicated men came together on the campus of Binghamton University in Binghamton, New York to start a movement. Their goal was to end the ignorant views upon the South Asians everywhere form an organization that brought South Asian men together.

The idea of ethnic fraternities first came about as a need to share and educate the community of the rich and diverse cultures that exist among us. These eight men came together with a vision to preserve and promote the rich and diverse heritage of the South Asian culture. They noticed however, that traditional on-campus student organizations were split among the lines of national origins, and at most provided a group of friends during college. The yearly election of new officers in these student organizations made it difficult to pursue any type of extended project – as each new executive board set its own agenda. Their solution to this problem was the creation of the first ever South Asian American interest fraternity.

Driven by a fire within each of them, on Monday, February 7th 1994, these eight men became the National Founding Fathers of Iota Nu Delta National Fraternity Incorporated and founded the FIRST South Asian Interest Fraternity in the world. By forming this organization, their goal was to bridge the gaps between people of different backgrounds, ethnicities, and cultures – all while promoting South Asian cultural awareness. Over time the Brothers deemed it necessary to have a central governing body with a solid infrastrucure which would take the Brotherhood to new heights. This ultimately lead to the formation of our first National Board. The efforts of all thse men to date have paved the way for an upcoming of one of the most prestigious organizations of its kind.
summary: Iota Nu Delta Fraternity, Inc. is the very first South Asian based fraternity of its kind, founded in 1994. We take great pride and honor knowing that through our vision and hard work we were able to pave the way for all South Asian Greeks Worldwide.

name: Pi Kappa Phi Fraternity
description: The Alpha Upsilon Chapter of Pi Kappa Phi, founded May 20th, 1933.
summary: The Alpha Upsilon Chapter of Pi Kappa Phi, founded May 20th, 1933.

name: Wilbur W. Oaks Physician Assistant Student Society
shortName: Wilbur W. Oaks Physician Assistant Student Society
description: This society serves as the official organization of the Drexel University Physician Assistant Program
summary: This society serves as the official organization of the Drexel University Physician Assistant Program.

name: Drexel Men's Club Basketball
description: The Basketball Club team competes in the National Club Basketball Association's North Atlantic (South) Region with local colleges and universities including Villanova, St. Joes, Penn, Delaware, Neumann, Rowan, and TCNJ. The team meets twice a week in the evening to practice and scrimmage. During the season, the team travels or remains at home to compete for the top spot in the league on Saturday mornings. The number one team in the league is invited to the national tournament at the end of the season. Additionally, there are tip-off tournaments prior to the seasons start.
summary: The Basketball Club competes in the NCBBA from November 1st - April 25th. We play hard, have fun, and form friendships

name: Ascend Drexel Student Chapter
description:
Our Student Chapters focus on fostering the next generation of undergraduate Asian American leaders. With events ranging from leadership training, ongoing resume and interview critiques to company visits as well as networking events, this volunteer-run chapter bring supplementary skills to students. Through joint events with other local chapters, Ascend provides students with additional ways to grow their career and network. 

 

Our growing number of Professional Chapters, with over 20,000 members from 200 companies represented, include membership from all levels: from corporate executive to managers and professionals. Each chapter presents a variety of local events, including professional development, mentorship, social mixers and community service - to enhance the skill set of Asian Americans in the workforce. 

 

Although our organization is focused on bringing a voice to Asian American professionals, we encourage any one, regardless of ethnic origin, to join the Ascend community.  

 

For more information, please visit our Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/ascend.drexel/

 

Feel free to contact us via email: ascend.drexel@gmail.com
summary: Since 2005, Ascend has been growing its presence throughout the US - with more than 45 student and 22 professional chapters in and around major business hubs. Our growing number of Professional Chapters, with over 60,000 members from 200 companies.

name: Chabad Student Group
description:
Constitution of Chabad Student Group

Article I: Name

This organization shall be named Chabad Student Group (CSG) providing vibrant Judaism at Drexel University.

Article II: Purpose

The primary purpose of the Chabad Student Group is to provide living Judaism to the students at Drexel University within the framework of traditional Judaism. Our objectives include but are not limited to; educational, ritual, and social activities, in addition to counseling and spiritual guidance to individual students. Our organization is based on Chassidic philosophy, as taught locally by the Chabad Jewish Center directed by Rabbi Chaim and Moussia Goldstein. Our philosophy states that every Jew deserves an equal opportunity to experience and enjoy their heritage. All programs and events of the Chabad Student Group are within the framework of Halachah, Jewish law as transmitted by the Rabbis throughout the generations dating back to Moses receiving the Torah at Mount Sinai. These goals shall be achieved through weekly Torah (Bible) classes, weekly women's programs, lectures on various Jewish issues, and speakers for religious and cultural events. We will also provide Shabbat and Holiday services, Glatt (the highest Kosher standards) Kosher dinners, and Holiday programs, and Shabbatons (unique weekends). This organization shall be not for profit or non-commercial in nature.

Article III: Affiliations

Full membership shall be limited to registered students of Drexel University, faculty, staff, the spouses of members, and other students may be associate members. Associate members may not hold office or vote on matters concerning this organization. Members are free to leave and disassociate without fear of retribution or harassment. Membership in Chabad Student Group is open to all students regardless of race, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, or ability. This organization is affiliated with Drexel University as a recognized student organization and as such, agrees to abide by the rules, policies, and procedures of Drexel University.

Article IV: Membership

Membership of this organization shall consist of undergraduate students enrolled at Drexel University.

Membership in this organization shall not discriminate against any individuals regardless of race, national origin, color, religion, sex, age, veteran status, sexual orientation, and/or ability.

Article V: Officers
Section 1: The officers shall be President, Vice President, Treasurer, and Event Coordinator and  Section 2: To be eligible to run for a position on the board, one must have participated in the CSG for at least one school year. Section 3: The organization President and Treasurer must be full-time, enrolled undergraduate students of Drexel University.
Section 4: Officers will hold office from summer term until summer term of the following year. Section 5: Roles of the officers. The President will act as a liaison between CSG and Drexel University in obtaining permission for the use of university facilities etc. He/she will also be responsible for presiding over meetings and act as the official representative of the Chabad Student Group. The Vice President shall be the liaison between CSG and the Drexel University
students through monitoring the various programs and events and relaying questions, concerns, and suggestions and will keep the minutes of the meetings and put in writing all records of Chabad student Group.. The Treasurer will handle the funds, whether collected or distributed for formal or informal events, as well as, balance and maintain accurate records of all financial transactions. The at large officers will assist with all of the above and all necessary tasks to accomplish the programs and services offered by the Chabad Student group.
Section 6: The impeachment of an officer can only occur when a special meeting is called by an active officer. He/she must put into writing the grounds for the impeachment request. Two-thirds of the officers must be present at the meeting and a two-third vote of the active officers is necessary for impeachment.
Elections of new officers will take place within one month of the beginning of summer term, every year. -Any student who participated in the CSG for one year can be nominated or run for any office. -Members who are running for office must deliver a speech prior to elections.
