-Members will be nominated in an announced meeting prior to elections. The officers of CSG will publicize the date and location of elections by word of mouth, e.mail list or print advertisement. The elections will be conducted by the previous board members.

Article VI: Advisor

Section 1: The organization advisor must be a full-time member of the faculty, administration, or professional staff of Drexel University.

Section 2: The duties and responsibilities of the advisor shall be to:

  1. Have basic knowledge as to the history, structure, and purpose of the organization
  2. Be aware of the group’s finances and budget, as your approval may be needed for financial transactions
  3. Provide useful guidance to help the student organization identify and attain its goals
  4. Be knowledgeable of University policies and regulations and civic ordinances, as well as state and federal laws that affect the activity of the organization
  5. Maintain ongoing contact with club officers

 

Article VII: Meetings

 

Section 1:        Regular meetings of the organization shall be held weekly at Chabad for an hour by the President.

 

Section 2:        Special meetings of the organization shall be held at Chabad by the President.

 

Section 3:        For the transaction of business and voting, a quorum must be obtained.  Quorum is defined as the minimum percentage of members required to be present.  The organization requires 30% for the transaction of business.

Article VIII: Hazing Statement

Membership should be a valuable and beneficial experience for all students. Leadership of this organization shall provide its new and current members with an experience that is positive, informative and consistent with federal, state, and local laws, and the policies and procedures of Drexel University. Activities which detract from the goal of fostering personal and intellectual development have no place in this organization. Hazing will not be tolerated by or of any member. It is the responsibility of the organization, its leadership and members to report hazing of any kind by members to the proper authority.

Article IX: Amendments

 

This constitution shall be amended by a vote of 2/3 majority of the membership during the time we elect a new board.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Date Revised: 4/26/2023

 

Bylaws of Chabad Student Group

 

Article I: Membership

 

Membership of this organization shall consist of undergraduate students enrolled at Drexel University and shall not discriminate against any individuals regardless of race, national origin, color, religion, sex, age, veteran status, sexual orientation, and/or ability.

 

Article II: Selection or Election of Officers

 

The selection/election of officers shall be done by the previous board annually.

 

 

Article III: Selection or Election of Advisor

 

The selection/election of the advisor shall be done by the current board annually.

 

 

Article IV: Committees

 

All permanent committees shall be formed by involved members of the group.

 

 

Article V: Rules of Order

 

This organization shall be governed by the current board.

 

 

Article VI: Amendment

 

These bylaws may be amended by a vote of majority vote of 2/3.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Date Revised: 4/26/2023
summary: Chabad Student Group (CSG) provides vibrant Judaism at Drexel University

name: Drexel University Pre-Veterinary Medical Association
description:
Anyone who is pursuing application to veterinary school, animal-related research, or simply has a passion for science and/or animals is invited to join Drexel’s pre-veterinary club.

Our mission is to strive to provide support and knowledge that will assist in our ultimate goal of practicing veterinary medicine, which can lead to a career in private practice, research, education, industry, or public health. We also hope to educate students on the variety and requirements of veterinary schools. We will accomplish our goals by providing exposure to various aspects of veterinary medicine, including hands-on experience, community service opportunities, and off-campus club trips, while aiming to create a supportive and friendly environment.
summary: Anyone who is pursuing application to veterinary school, animal-related research, or simply has a passion for science and/or animals is invited to join Drexel's pre-veterinary club.

name: Drexel Urban Growers
description:
Vision: DUG’s vision is of a West Philadelphia where all community members have consistent, convenient, and free access to produce, regardless of socioeconomic status, neighborhood of residence, race/ethnicity, age, or any other demographics. We also envision a close-knit community of Drexel students, community members, and local youth who work together to build sustainable, local food systems in West Philadelphia.

 

Mission: Drexel Urban Growers works to create a diverse network of students and community residents that grow and donate organic produce and food to our community, address and raise awareness about food justice and sovereignty, and connect people to resources to grow their food. 

Why Should I Join? 

  • Help be a part of the solution to hunger and food insecurity in your community 
  • Meet community members and individuals outside of Drexel through Harvest days, community dinners, and more
  • Reconnect with nature and spend time outside
  • Learn how to garden; form a new hobby!!
  • Be connected to volunteer opportunities
  • Meet Drexel students
  • Leadership opportunities (***see below if interested) 
  • Become a member of the West Philadelphia Food Justice Network
  • Break out of the Drexel bubble 

***Leadership

  • Hello! If you are interested in joining our organization, want to collaborate on an event, or are interested in pursuing a leadership opportunity with DUG, reach out our President, Mae Flaherty (mf3353@drexel.edu)
  • If you have questions about the garden, want to join the garden maintenance team, or want to host/co-host an event in the garden, contact our Garden Manager, Madi Rockett (mjr457@drexel.edu)

Join Drexel Urban Growers to learn how to raise and maintain vegetable beds, soil health, and sustain nutrition. We run Community Garden Harvest Days, volunteer activities at urban farms in Philadelphia, community picnics, and other food justice events! Email us at drexelgrows@gmail.com for meeting reminders and opportunities!

Follow us on instagram:  @DUGrowers

Join our discord: https://discord.gg/aXuswPRq 

Join our Facebook Page: https://www.facebook.com/groups/516246131780941/948866261852257/?notif_t=like
summary: Drexel Urban Growers (DUG) is an organization that maintains a community garden, raises awareness about and addresses food insecurity, and builds relationships between students and the West Philadelphia community. Join our Discord!

name: Drexel Socialists
description: Drexel Socialists aims to promote the understanding of the inequalities of modern capitalism, to educate the community on the ideals and philosophy of socialism, to educate the community on its rights and responsibilities as citizens of Drexel, Philadelphia, the United States and the world. We do this by doing projects that serve the people and build solidarity across racial, gendered, ethnic, and other divides.
summary: Drexel Socialists: serve the people, fight the power

name: Latinos for a United Campus
description:
The objective of our organization is to create Latiné cultural awareness and to unite the Latino community at Drexel. 

Membership is open to all full-time, part-time, undergraduate/graduate students, alumni, faculty, and staff of Drexel University.  No student shall be denied membership based on grounds of race, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, religion, age, or disability.
summary: The objective of our organization is to create Latiné cultural awareness and to unite the Latiné community at Drexel. Membership is open to all full-time, part-time, undergraduate/graduate students, alumni, faculty, and staff of Drexel.

name: Theme Park Engineering and Design Group
description:
The Drexel Theme Park Engineering and Design Group (Drexel TPED) is an officially recognized organization of the Themed Entertainment Association (TEA) that provides Drexel undergraduate students and the surrounding Philadelphia community with resources and knowledge pertaining to the themed entertainment industry!

 

In TPED you can learn about the behind-the-scenes work that goes into your favorite theme park attractions, discuss live entertainment like concerts or escape rooms, and even go on trips to some of your favorite themed experiences!

 

We achieve our mission by (1) learning about the various facets of themed entertainment at general body meetings; (2) facilitating hands-on learning, leadership, and interdisciplinary collaboration through industry-related projects; (3) volunteering our services at local Philly schools and Drexel events; and (4) providing opportunities for networking and for gaining industry-relevant experiences through competitions, conferences, guest speakers, behind-the-scenes tours, and trips to themed venues!

 

The themed entertainment industry encompasses all disciplines, which our activities reflect. All majors are welcomed and encouraged to attend our events!

 

For more information and to get involved in TPED, please join our Discord!
summary: The Drexel Theme Park Engineering and Design Group (Drexel TPED) is an undergraduate organization at Drexel University that celebrates the themed entertainment industry! For more information, please visit our website: www.drexeltped.org

name: Sigma Alpha Mu
description: The Mu Eta Chapter of Sigma Alpha Mu was founded on October 04, 1947 (Refounded October 24, 2009). The chapter has a long and storied history at Drexel University, and we strive every day to continue our legacy. As a social fraternity, we provide opportunities for lifelong social, moral, and intellectual development. Brotherhood is of utmost importance to us. Brothers will form close bonds and many lasting friendships throughout their time in college. A brother is a brother for life, and the memories and experiences shared are invaluable even after college. Our brotherhood is diverse with men of all different majors and backgrounds. We foster an environment where we can share cultural and social interests while still being an individual.
summary: The Mu Eta Chapter of Sigma Alpha Mu was founded on October 04, 1947 (Refounded October 24, 2009). As a social fraternity, we provide opportunities for lifelong social, moral, and intellectual development. Brotherhood is of utmost importance to us.

name: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc.
description:
Delta Sigma Theta Sorority Inc. was founded by 22 illustrious women on the campus of Howard University. The sorority was built on the principles of Sisterhood, Scholarship, and Service. Our founders' purpose was to acknowledge and promote the excellence of collegiate women along with educating our communities to strive for success. 

Delta Sigma Theta Sorority Inc. - Epsilon Phi Chapter is the Philadelphia Citywide Chapter. We were chartered on March 27, 1966. Our charter includes Drexel, La Salle, Jefferson, St. Joseph's University, and The University of the Arts. For over half a century, Epsilon Phi has been implementing programs and participating in community service throughout the Philadelphia region. 

Vist our website or e-mail us for more information on upcoming programs!
summary: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc. is an organization of college educated women committed to the constructive development of its members and to public service with a primary focus on the Black community.

name: Delta Sigma Phi Fraternity
description:
Mission Statement:

The Delta Sigma Phi Fraternity Gamma Chi Chapter Exists to build better men through the fraternal bond and holds high standards of excellence in chivalry, well-rounded values, supporting a respect-based culture, accountability, personal development, leadership, and organizational growth.
summary: "Better Men, Better Lives"

name: Kappa Phi Gamma, Sorority Inc.
description: Kappa Phi Gamma Sorority, Inc. was established on November 8th, 1998, as the Nation’s first ever South-Asian interest Sorority.  Her roots date back to the Jester basement at the University of Texas at Austin where 27 collegiate women from a myriad of backgrounds collaborated to form what would be the most diverse and strongest sorority of its kind. Although, South-Asian founded, she has expanded the warmth of her Sisterhood to any and all motivated young women, willing to make a difference at her University and community.Kappa Phi Gamma Sorority, Inc. was established on November 8th, 1998, as the Nation’s first ever South-Asian interest Sorority. Although, South-Asian founded, she has expanded the warmth of her Sisterhood to any and all motivated young women, willing to make a difference at her University and community.
summary: Kappa Phi Gamma Sorority, Inc. was established on November 8th, 1998, as the Nation’s first ever South-Asian interest Sorority. Although, South-Asian founded, she has expanded the warmth of her Sisterhood to any and all motivated young women.

name: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc.
description: The Delta Eta Chapter of Kappa Alpha Psi, Inc. was founded on the night of February 11, 1952, on the campus of the University of Pennsylvania. The charter includes the University of Pennsylvania, Drexel University, Villanova University, LaSalle University, and St. Joseph's University.
summary: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc. is a historically Black Greek-lettered fraternity. Since the fraternity's founding on January 5, 1911, at Indiana University, the fraternity has never limited membership based on color, creed, or national origin.

name: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc.
description: On December 4, 1906, seven undergraduate students at Cornell University, “The Seven Jewels,” organized Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, the first intercollegiate fraternity among African American men. With the Great Sphinx of Giza as its symbol, and the motto “First of All, Servants of All, We Shall Transcend All,” Alpha Phi Alpha dedicated itself to defend the rights and to promote the responsibilities of African Americans. The founders of Alpha Phi Alpha sought to combine social purpose with social action, to be more than a social organization. Throughout its history, Alpha Phi Alpha has promoted knowledge and achievement.
summary: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc. develops leaders, promotes brotherhood and academic excellence, while providing service and advocacy for our communities.

name: Chemistry Graduate Student Association
description: The Chemistry Graduate Student Association works on the behalf of chemistry graduate students in order to encourage a constructive relationship between the students, faculty, staff and administration of the department of Chemistry at Drexel University. By sharing our interests and our time, we can forge closer connections between ourselves, and the Drexel graduate student community.
summary: The Chemistry Graduate Student Association works on the behalf of chemistry graduate students in order to encourage a constructive relationship between the students, faculty, staff and administration of the department of Chemistry at Drexel University.

name: International Graduate Student Association
description: The mission of this organization shall be to advocate the interests, address the concerns, improve the communication channels, and augment the life of international graduate students at Drexel University with info sessions, fun-filled events and more.
summary: The mission of this organization shall be to advocate the interests, address the concerns, improve the communication channels, and augment the life of international graduate students at Drexel University.

name: Future Educators of Drexel
description: Future Educators of Drexel (FEDs) strives to further the involvement of its members in the field of education through community and social opportunities, as well as affiliation with relevant professional organizations and educational institutions.
summary: Future Educators of Drexel (FEDs) strives to further the involvement of its members in the field of education through community and social opportunities, as well as affiliation with relevant professional organizations and educational institutions.

name: The Paleontology and Geology Club of Drexel University
description: We are here to educate members of the greater Drexel community of the geologic processes and prehistoric life of the Earth. Our goal is to provide guest lectures, field trips, and other fun activities to both educate and entertain the Drexel Community.
summary: We are here to educate members of the greater Drexel community of the geologic processes and prehistoric life of the Earth. Our goal is to provide guest lectures, field trips, and other fun activities to both educate and entertain the Drexel Community.

name: Drexel University Ski/Snowboard Club
description: Whether you ski or ride, our organization is the home to mountain sport enthusiasts interested in bonding with others who share the same love for the mountain. We target all skill levels from first timers who are looking to learn the basics, to experts trying to challenge themselves. Our organization offers instruction, information sessions focused on building or improving skills, day trips, and weekend trips. We strongly focus on safety and learning while also having fun!
summary: Whether you ski or ride, our organization is the home to mountain sport enthusiasts interested in bonding with others who share the same love for the mountain. We target all skill levels from first timers to experts.

name: Drexel University Women in Physics Society
description:
The primary goal of the Women in Physics Society is to increase the retention rate of women in the Physics Department, resulting in more women graduating with a B.S. in physics from Drexel.

We recognize that our graduation rate is significantly below the national rate.

In 2005, the American Institute of Physics (AIP) found that women earned approximately 22% of bachelor’s degrees in physics. In June 2011, the Physics Department at Drexel University consisted of 10% women undergraduates. We think it is very important to work on recruiting women to physics, however, our main focus is on retaining those who start off as physics majors.

The Women in Physics Society works toward this goal by organizing activities that provide support, mentoring, education, and community involvement. We aim to be a support system for each other, both encouraging and motivating women throughout their college experience in the Physics Department. Our upper classmen know what it’s like to be a freshman and in the minority in all of your physics classes. We also know how discouraging that can be. WiPS strives to reach out to freshman women as early as possible in the academic year so that they immediately have a sense of community and belonging.

Some of the specific events that WiPS sponsors throughout the year includes:

1. Social events to get to know each other (tie-dye party, movie nights, restaurant trips, etc.)

2. Study groups – Since the majority of our class is men, we end up working on schoolwork with men. Sometimes women feel more comfortable asking questions when mentors or homework partners are also women. Women, more so than men, may doubt themselves in the classroom. Hearing other women talk about their feelings is a good way for us to learn that we’re not alone. Of course, one may participate in study groups that have both men and women, but this provides an option that does not currently exist.

3. Outreach – Since the Drexel Chapter of the Society of Physics Students is a well-established organization that already does many outreach events with the local community, we work closely with them, both helping to organize and attend their events. Through this partnership, we have been able to hold events at all-girls schools. We hope to serve as role models to these students and encourage their interest in science.

4.  Attending conferences – WiPS members have attended the Northeast Conference for Undergraduate Women in Physics (NCUWiP) the last three years and have always returned extremely inspired. The graduate student and career panesl are great ways to learn about options after graduating from Drexel. NCUWiP is also a perfect opportunity to present research and meet other women in your field.

5. Career Development/Mentoring – We regularly hold events that include our women graduate students and faculty. Hearing about their experiences is incredibly valuable!

We hope that providing such a support system will help increase our confidence, motivation, and encourage us to continue pursuing an undergraduate degree in the subject we all love.
summary: The objectives of this organization are to promote community among the undergraduate women in physics by organizing events, outreach activities, and providing support for one another.

name: Drexel University Pakistani Students Association
description: A reflection of Iqbal’s dream and Quaid’s vision. Celebrating the richness of Pakistan — its soulful music, vibrant colors, flavorful cuisine, and timeless traditions. Rooted in unity and service, Drexel PSA welcomes all to explore the beauty of our heritage and the strength of our community. As Quaid said, “With faith, discipline, and selfless devotion, there is nothing you cannot achieve.” Here, we honor that spirit — together.
summary: A mosaic of colors, stories, and aspirations - a living testament to the beauty and promise of Pakistan.

name: Sharing Excess, Drexel Chapter
description: Sharing Excess is a student-powered movement to fight food insecurity both on campus and in the surrounding community. We partner with local restaurants to deliver excess food to hunger relief organizations, and also operate Drexel's Guest Meal Swipe Donation program.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide students with opportunities to address food insecurity both on campus and in the surrounding community by sharing excess resources.

name: Active Minds at Drexel University
description: We are a peer-to-peer mental health advocacy and awareness group that is focused on changing the conversation and breaking the stigma around mental health. We educate students about mental health issues, how to support themselves, and how to support their peers.
summary: We are a peer-to-peer mental health advocacy and awareness group focused on changing the conversation and breaking the stigma around mental health. We educate students about mental health issues, how to support themselves, and how to support their peers.

name: Drexel Criminal Justice Honors Society
shortName: Alpha Phi Sigma, Eta Iota Chapter
description:
The purposes of Alpha Phi Sigma are to honor and  promote analytical thinking, rigorous scholarship and life long learning; to keep abreast of the advances in scientific research; to elevate the ethical standards of the criminal justice professions and to sustain in the public mind the benefit and necessity of education and professional training. Recognize distinguished and scholarly achievements and support appropriate projects by:

  1. Sponsoring scholarly and academic projects
  2. Honoring and promoting academic excellence
  3. Honoring and promoting leadership
  4. Promoting and supporting the interest of Alpha Phi Sigma National Chapter
  5. Organizing, promoting and establishing meetings
The qualifications to apply are: having at least a Sophomore status, a declared major or minor in Criminal Justice, taken at least four CJ courses, and a cumulative GPA of at least 3.2.
 
If interested, you'll need to send your unofficial transcript, APS application (which you can find and print out online), and a $70 fee (in form of a cashier's check) to the chapter advisor Dr. Ashley Dickinson. Just a quick note on the fee - it's a one time payment to the national chapter that puts you in the honor society for life, connecting you to chapters in graduate schools as well.
summary: The honor society is primarily geared towards assisting upperclassmen in obtaining employment post graduation through creating relationships with individuals and agencies in the criminal justice field.

name: Material Advantage
description: Material Advantage, Drexel Collegiate Chapter is a professional organization that welcomes anyone with an interest in the field of materials science and engineering. This student chapter is partnered with the following national organizations: The American Ceramic Society, The Association for Iron and Steel Technology, ASM International, and The Minerals, Metals & Materials Society (link). We strive to create a community that connects students with faculty, local professionals, and local collegiate chapters. Students will be able to strengthen their involvement and interest in materials science and engineering through conferences, competitions, and scholarship opportunities, all the while having fun with peers!
summary: The Drexel University Chapter of Material Advantage is a professional organization that celebrates the field of material science and engineering while offering the opportunity to network and socialize. This organization is open to students of all majors!

name: Minority Association of Pre-Medical Students
description:
Here at Drexel’s Minority Association of Pre-Medical Students (MAPS), we support and encourage underrepresented students who want to pursue careers in medicine. We’re part of the Student National Medical Association and we’re here to make sure you feel seen and supported on your path in healthcare.

We host events where you can network with other students pursuing medicine, and healthcare professionals. Events will help guide your pre-med journey, connect with mentors, give back to the community through volunteering opportunities, participate in hands-on clinical workshops, resume reviews, and build relations with people who will understand. Whether it’s a panel discussion or a fun interactive event there’s always something happening. This is a space to grow, ask questions, and stay motivated.

All undergraduate students interested in medicine are welcome to join us! To be a formal member, you just need to attend at least one general body meeting and one event (membership is not limited to pre-med students, students who are pre-dental, pre-PA, pre-nursing, and students looking into any other fields in medicine are welcome) Anyone can reach out and stop by to find out what we do at MAPS!
summary: The Minority Association of Pre-medical Students is an organization that seeks to support the endeavors of ambitious minority pre-med students by providing resources, academic guidance and mentorship.

name: Lindy Center for Civic Engagement
description:
Vision Statement:
The Lindy Center for Civic Engagement exists to fulfill Drexel University’s vision as a comprehensive, progressive university with a deep commitment to civic engagement. While we serve as a nexus to catalogue the university’s civic engagement activities as consistent with the strategic plan, our vision goes beyond to promote scholarship, engagement, and community. Our contributions to the university community and the greater public good will be recognized nationally.

Scholarship
The Lindy Center will collaborate with schools and colleges across the university to guide scholarship and research in the areas of civic engagement and community-based experiential learning. This includes providing support for faculty in courses conducting participatory and action-oriented activities. Overall, we will integrate the value of civic engagement more fully into campus education programs to enhance learning environments at the university.

Engagement
The Lindy Center will provide meaningful and high-quality civic engagement opportunities for every Drexel student. We support students as distinguished leaders in the area of public service. To this end we nurture a cadre of students at every level to enrich their education while serving the public good. Our focus is to provide experiences that leave every Drexel student believing that the ideals of social responsibility can be integrated into their careers.

 

Community
The Lindy Center is part of Drexel University’s role as a positive contributor to the quality of life in the region. Our consistent relationships within the wider community impact upon the pressing social issues affecting our region and are sources for engaged scholarship for faculty and enhanced learning for students. We develop community partnerships so that every Drexel student will recognize the benefits of public service to their education, their career, and to society.
summary:
Mission statement:
Drexel University’s Lindy Center for Civic Engagement promotes the ideals of social responsibility and public service by facilitating community based experiential learning for students, faculty, and staff. Through collaborations with t

name: The Good Idea Fund
description:
Our Mission

The Good Idea Fund exists as a way for individual students, faculty, staff, and recognized student organizations to apply and access student activity fee funds to engage the undergraduate student body in a broad spectrum of Student Life and campus-based initiatives.

    

New Funding Framework

The Good Idea is proud to release our new funding framework. The new framework adapts to some of the recent changes put in place by Campus Engagement regarding how student organizations are classified, and allows us to fund students, student organizations, and campus departments in ways we haven't been able to in the past. Although our funding types may look a bit different, we designed the framework with the same goal in mind - to help the undergraduate student body host amazing events!

Click Here to View Our Funding Framework

    

Apply For Funding!

Our application for funding is available on DragonLink! Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Below are a list of recommended application timelines, but please note we may deny an application or push back an event date if we do not believe there is adequate time to successfully execute the event. Please apply as early as possible!

   

Event Support - Please apply at least 4 weeks prior to your anticipated event date.
Co-Sponsorship - Please apply at least 2 months prior to your anticipated event date.
Supplemental Student Organization Funding - Please apply at least 2 weeks prior to your organization needing additional operational funds.

         

Have an idea for an event but don't know where to begin?

No problem, simply fill out an application for funding and select the "co-sponsorship" funding option. When prompted, select "undeveloped idea". You will then be asked a series of questions about your idea. The TGIF board will review your responses and decide whether to continue pursing the idea with you!

Contact Us

If you have any questions, reach out to us: 

tgifchair@drexel.edu
summary: The Good Idea Fund exists as a way for individual students, faculty, staff, and recognized student organizations to apply and access student activity fee funds to engage the undergraduate student body.

name: Student Center for Diversity & Inclusion
description:
The Student Center for Diversity and Inclusion (SCDI) supports and empowers students of all backgrounds as they build a sense of community and belonging. Our goal is to embrace, respect, and value all identities that reflect the diversity of Drexel University's students and community.

Our office engages Drexel's undergraduate and graduate students of African American, Latinx/e, Asian, Native American, and Indigenous descent, as well as LGBTQIA+, first generation, immigrants, and international student populations through direct advocacy, intentional identity-based community building activities, events, and courageous conversations. We are active advocates for student diversity, intersectional social justice, and education on campus. The SCDI is a safe/brave space where you can be who you are or explore who you want to be. 

We are student-centered but actively look for collaborations between academic departments and offices around campus.

  • Empower students to create a stimulating and diverse environment. Our goal is to build a sense of belonging by providing a safe/brave space where students can live their whole identity.
  • Facilitate intersectional cross-community connections and programs.
  • Start courageous conversations that explore different topics.
  • Provide educational training for the Drexel community.
  • Fight against period poverty and food insecurity.
  • Provide supplies to practice safe sex.
  • Support diversity-based student organizations and Drexel's faith communities.
  • Provide student-worker opportunities for our students who are passionate about DEI.

Come and visit our space on the second floor of the Rush Building!
summary: The Student Center for Diversity and Inclusion (SCDI) is a safe/brave space where you can be who you are or explore who you want to be.

name: Drexel Fencing Club
description:
The Fencing Club is open to all members of the Drexel University community who are interested in learning or refining fencing skills. Fencers of all disciplines and skill levels are encouraged to come out to practice.

 

Fall practice schedule is as follows:  

        Monday 9-10:30pm in the Rec Center Basketball Courts

        Tuesday 9-10:30pm in the Rec Center Basketball Courts

        Wednesday 9-10:30pm in the Rec Center Basketball Courts

 

 

In order to participate in practice you must have your paperwork completed and submitted by noon the day of practice, instructions for what paperwork needs to be submitted and where to find it is available in the documents tab below.

Follow us on Instagram at Drexel_Fencing, and make sure to join this Dragonlink page to receive more information about the upcoming season!
summary: The Fencing Club is open to all students attending Drexel University. Previous fencing experience is not required.

name: Drexel Finance and Investment Group
description:
A University is a unique institution that fosters life-shaping experiences, forges relationships, and establishes foundations for careers. Upon entering college, freshman students are inundated with new opportunities of all sorts. Throughout a four or five year collegiate tour, students explore those opportunities and define their interests with the main intention being a successful career. Our organization would like to cater specifically to that aspiration by providing a means for students to learn about the business world, engage with a diverse population of peers, professors, and professionals, and cultivate new proficiencies in order to maximize their chance for future personal success.

As a freshman, students enter college with a basic, flexible understanding of their future, which presents a seemingly endless array of opportunities. Our organization will offer high quality opportunities introducing students to the worlds of Finance, Entrepreneurship, and Investing, and show them the paths that lie within them. Through our programming initiatives, members will have the chance to engage in networking and educational events that will help them make connections with professionals and learn more about relevant business concepts in addition to their studies. In winter term, freshman students can apply to become a member of our Associate Board, an opportunity that provides students with a leadership positions early on in their academic careers and will help to maintain stability in the organization as a whole.

During the sophomore, pre- junior and junior years, members will have several opportunities to grow through the many branches of our organization. Our organization offers many areas for members of all interests to engage with programs such as the Weekly View newsletter, Programming, Marketing, and Membership development initiatives. By taking advantage of these events and programs, members will gain a competitive advantage while seeking Co-Op employment.

As seniors, members can leverage the relationships they have built throughout college going to some of our many networking events that bring Drexel Alumni, corporate recruiters, and potential employers onto our campus. At these events, members can learn about new career opportunities that are available at companies such as BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan Chase, and Susquehanna International Group.

After a member graduates, they will not be forgotten by our organization. We maintain strong connections with our alumni and recognize them as one of the greatest resources our organization holds. These graduated members will be encouraged to return to our events, not as attendees, but as guest speakers or hiring managers to help the next generation of bright-eyed members achieve their professional goals.

Overall, our organization will offer continuous opportunities for students to develop while enrolled in undergraduate studies at Drexel University. These opportunities are perpetuated by our organization's relationships with employers, member alumni and Drexel offices and administration. Together, we will plant the seeds of tomorrow’s business leaders.
summary: Formed in 2012 by a merger between the Drexel Finance Association, the Drexel Investment Group, and the Drexel Banker's Association, DFIG is the premier finance and investment student organization of Drexel University's Lebow College of Business.

name: Dragon Jedi
description: Dragon Jedi is a group of performance artists who use stunt saber choreography, character development, costuming, and stage combat to prepare its members for public performances and charity events as our way of giving back to the community. The goal is to create an inclusive environment in which all can participate in these activities, learn various skills, and enjoy the camaraderie of like-minded people.
summary: Dragon Jedi is a group of performing artists who use lightsaber choreography, character development, costuming, and stage combat to prepare our members for public performances and charity events as our way of giving back to the community.

name: The Cleftomaniacs
description: The Drexel Cleftomaniacs is an all-gender a cappella group established in 2012 featuring Drexel University undergraduate students who have a passion for singing and creating music. Our purpose is to provide a welcoming environment where Drexel students can appreciate and produce quality a cappella music while also fostering a diverse and tight-knit family. We aim to help each member develop as an individual musician and to constantly elevate the performance of the group as a whole. Auditions are held every fall and conditionally in the spring, all are welcome to audition. Check out our Instagram and website for more info. You can also see some of our past performances on YouTube!
summary: The Cleftomaniacs, Drexel's first all-gender a cappella group established in 2012!

name: Drexel Gaming Association
description:
Drexel Gaming Association (DGA) is Drexel University's home for gaming.

We hold regular events on campus where members of Drexel's expansive gaming community can come together and play their favorite titles. DGA strives to deliver the activities you care about with frequent LANs, tournaments, and other special opportunities. We focus on both the casual and competitive aspects of gaming. If you would like to stay up to date on all our events, follow us on social media and join our Discord.

 

https://discord.gg/wMfKTT9mvB
summary: We are Drexel University's home for gaming. In addition to hosting quarterly LANs, we have frequent community events - everything from board games to tournaments!

name: Korean Student Association
description: Korean Student Association (KSA) is a student organization primarily consisting of Korean international and Korean-American students, but always welcomes any students who have a strong interest in the Korean culture and issues - regardless of their ethnicity. KSA holds various cultural, educational, and social events to not only enrich all of its members' experiences at Drexel, but also to contribute further by supporting the Korean community around the Philadelphia area. KSA will also strive to offer its members a platform to network with Drexel Alumni residing both in and out of Korea. Being a resource to the student body is also another aspect we would like to offer as an organization--offering studying groups/sessions, tutoring, co-op interview/resume help, and more.
summary: The Korean Student Association is a student organization consists primarily of Korean international and Korean-American students committed to spreading Korean culture and raising awareness of Korean heritage throughout the Drexel community.

name: Flux
description: Flux aims to provide Drexel's student body with a variety of unique musical experiences by organizing and presenting concerts and events in various locations throughout campus, while being completely student-run. We give students the opportunity to put on live music events with their peers while also learning the skills its takes to put on shows. We also give local and Drexel artists/bands a chance to introduce themselves to the Drexel student body.
summary: Flux aims to provide Drexel's student body with a variety of unique musical experiences, organizing and presenting concerts in various locations throughout campus. We give students the opportunity to put on live music events with their peers.

name: Society of Security Analysts
description: The Society of Securities Analysts (SSA) aims to maintain a network of dedicated business students who are interested in careers in finance and who can actively contribute to the group. We provide members with exclusive resources, including speaker events with industry professionals, a weekly market news summary newsletter, and training sessions on various finance skills and technical topics. SSA alumni have successfully launched careers in investment banking, private equity, sales & trading, private wealth management, and more. Membership is selective. If you are interested, please submit a resume along with a brief paragraph of interest to our main contact.
summary: SSA maintains a network of dedicated business students interested in high finance and provides resources to support career paths such as investment banking, sales & trading, private wealth management, private equity, and more.

name: Fellowship of Christian Athletes
description: We are presenting a group for Christian Athletes to come together and be able to talk about the Lord and study His Word. We offer a safe haven from the stress of regular college life where you can just come and relax, sing, eat, and worship.
summary: To present to coaches and athletes, and all whom they influence, the challenge and adventure of receiving Jesus Christ as Savior and Lord, serving Him in their relationships and in the fellowship of the church.

name: Drexel Speaks
description:
Drexel Speaks is an organization that provides a mutually supportive open space and positive learning environment where every member has the opportunity to: (1) enhance public speaking skills (2) develop strong oral communication and (3) develop leadership skills, in order to foster self-confidence and personal growth. Dozens of members have reported a significant improvement in their overall public speaking skills after about a month of participating in the club. Drexel Speaks is a non-competitive, judgment-free zone where members are encouraged - but not pressured - to deliver short, impromptu speeches.

SPRING 2026: We meet at 6:00 pm on Thursdays in LeBow Business School, Room 033! If you would like to receive notifications on the details of each week's meeting (i.e., room number that week, if a meeting has been canceled, if the time of the meeting was changed, etc.) then please press "Join" so that we could add you to our mailing list!  

If you have any questions, please email to our President at ma3947@drexel.edu.
summary: We are a club that helps members improve their communication, public speaking, and leadership skills by providing a safe space for members to give impromptu speeches.

name: Communication, Culture, and Media Association of Doctoral Students (CCMADS)
description:
The Communication, Culture, and Media Association of Doctoral Students (CCMADS) serves to enhance the Communication, Culture, & Media program at Drexel University both academically and socially by supplementing it with learning and networking opportunities as well as extra-curricular activities. The association provides a forum for CCM graduate students to voice their opinions and have them represented by the CCMADS Board to the Graduate Student Association.

We seek to inspire leadership, collegiality, and departmental growth. As a group, we encourage and embrace the principles surrounding interdisciplinary thought and action. To do so, we facilitate exchange and cooperation among all doctoral students in the social sciences and humanities at Drexel. We believe that media and communication are fundamentally intertwined with culture and need to be studied in order to understand the impact they have in society. We believe in preparing CCM Doctoral students to be successful leaders in academia, politics, and corporate industry. We believe in advancing the study of Communication, Culture, & Media.
summary: CCMADS is a student organization for current Ph.D. students in the Department of Culture and Communication. The club will meets monthly to discuss upcoming events, member activity and departmental needs.

name: Drexel Student Centers
description:
Drexel Student Centers manages the daily operations of the Creese Student Center, the Rush building, the James E. Marks Intercultural Center (JEMIC), the Korman Center, the Perelman Center and Ross Commons, creating and managing work order flow and building projects for 135,000 square feet of space Drexel Student Centers collectively employs 250 students annually in roles that serve to support Drexel’s mission from customer service to technical event support to building management and security.

It is in the mission of Drexel Student Centers to enhance Drexel University and its academic objectives by offering programs, services and activities that meet the dynamic needs of our students, faculty, professional staff, alumni, and the Drexel community. Drexel Student Centers promotes and generates student involvement and development opportunities through employment and mentoring that promote learning beyond the classroom. Furthermore, it is in these facilities, programs, and retail spaces that we create an environment supportive of academic life and social interaction.

To learn more about Drexel Student Centers call the Creese Information Desk at 215-895-2515.
summary: Drexel Student Centers manages the daily operations of the Creese Student Center and Ross Commons, creating and managing work order flow and building projects for 135,000 square feet of space.

name: Drexel Chinese Graduate Christian Fellowship
description:
Drexel Chinese Graduate Christian Fellowship is to service Chinese graduate students and scholars at Drexel University through sharing love and life in Jesus Christ.

The mission of Drexel Chinese Graduate Christian Fellowship is to spread out the Gospel message -- to bring God's love to Drexel students (especially Chinese international graduate students), faculty, staff and neighbors. Everyone is more than welcome to our organization to know, learn and truly enjoy the holy grace from God.
summary: Drexel Chinese Graduate Christian Fellowship is to service Chinese graduate students and scholars at Drexel University through sharing love and life in Jesus Christ.

name: Cru
description: Cru is a caring community passionate about sharing the love of Jesus and creating an environment where He can transform the lives of Drexel students.
summary: We're the local chapter of Cru, an international, interdenominational Christian missions organization passionate about connecting people to Christ. There are no registration timeframes and students do not have to pay to enter our club. All are welcome!

name: Materials Research Society
description: The Materials Research Society (MRS) Drexel Chapter promotes interdisciplinary materials research on contemporary topics. Seminars, workshops, and discussions are held to understand and update our knowledge of cutting edge Material Science research. The Materials Research Society will build a dynamic, interactive, global community of materials researchers to advance technical excellence by providing a framework in which the materials disciplines can convene, collaborate, integrate and advocate.
summary: The Materials Research Society (MRS) Drexel Chapter promotes interdisciplinary materials research on contemporary topics. Seminars, workshops, and discussions are held to understand and update our knowledge of cutting edge Material Science research.

name: International Students and Scholars Services
description:
International Students and Scholars Services (ISSS) provides a variety of support services to approximately 3,500 international students, scholars, and employees at Drexel University. Our team is dedicated to supporting and advocating for the international community at Drexel.

We assist students from the moment they are admitted, through their visa applications and travel to the U.S., and continue to support them throughout their programs and up to 36 months after graduation. This includes providing support to students on Optional Practical Training (OPT), STEM OPT, and Academic Training (AT). ISSS Helps F-1 and J-1 students navigate complex immigration matters through holistic advising and various immigration and non-immigration programs. We also collaborate with other campus units to create programs that help students navigate their studies and adjust to life at Drexel and in the U.S., including new student orientation during Drexel's Welcome Week each September. Whether you are a newly admitted student, currently enrolled, or on OPT/STEM OPT/AT, we are here to support you throughout your journey.
summary: International Students and Scholar Services

name: Alpha Omega Epsilon
description:
Alpha Omega Epsilon is a sorority dedicated to engineers and technical science majors, promoting sisterhood, and the advancement of underrepresented genders in technical fields. A.O.E. promotes scholarship and academic achievement as well as integrity, character, and self-confidence. 

If you are interested in joining keep an eye out on our Instagram for our fall recruitment event days and times! DragonLink is currently for active members, to learn more about joining follow our Instagram @drexelaoe, or reach out to aoe.drexel.vp@gmail.com  to stay updated when recruitment events come out!
summary: A social and professional sorority that supports engineers and technical scientists. Our mission is promote friendship, leadership, and professionalism to all members of the organization, the community, and our professions.

name: The Drexel French Club
description: Drexel French Club is an organization focused on exploring French culture and language. It welcomes students of all backgrounds who share an interest in French culture, language learning, meeting other students, and promote global citizenship!
summary: French Club is about allowing Drexel students who are taking or having taken French language courses to immerse themselves in French-speaking cultures, the French language, and to meet other students sharing their passion while having fun.

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers: Drexel University Chapter
description:
FOR MORE INFORMATION, PLEASE VISIT OUR WEBSITE:

sase-drexel.carrd.co

ABOUT

SASE is dedicated to the advancement of Asian heritage scientists and engineers in education and employment so that they can achieve their full career potential. In addition to professional development, SASE also encourages members to contribute to the enhancement of the communities in which they live.

SASE’s mission is to:

  • Prepare Asian heritage scientists and engineers for success in the global business world.
  • Celebrate diversity on campuses and in the workplace.
  • Provide opportunities for members to make contributions to their local communities.
    ​

SASE membership is open to people of all ethnic backgrounds.

 

What We Do
SASE builds a bridge between the university and employers
• SASE has the support of Fortune 100 companies
• SASE provides networking possibilities and business connections for the student members
• SASE is another way for our school to increase its reputation among the business community

SASE will better prepare students for industry
• SASE will bring in recruiters and speakers to work with the student members
• SASE connects the student members to a professional network of support and resources
summary: The Society of Asian Scientists & Engineers (SASE) aims to help scientists, engineers, and technologists reach their full career potential through professional development, cultural awareness, and giving back to their community.

name: The Neuroscience Society
description: NSS shall serve as an organization dedicated to: promoting education in neuroscience throughout the Drexel and Philadelphia community, cultivating an academic and social environment for students to experience a multitude of topics in neuroscience, and promote the advancement of neuroscience in the Greater Philadelphia area.
summary: NSS strives to facilitate university-wide discussions of neuroscience through hosting discussions and events on a multitude of topics in the field of neuroscience that may interest our members and expand our knowledge.

name: American Medical Women's Association at Drexel
description:
The American Medical Women's Association is an organization which functions at the local, national, and international level to advance women in medicine and improve women's health. We achieve this by providing and developing leadership, advocacy, education, expertise, mentoring, and strategic alliances. 

AMWA at Drexel aims to raise awareness on women’s health concerns globally and locally through community service and guest speakers in the health fields. Drexel AMWA emphasizes building relationships and connections within the Drexel community and inside the medical world through mentoring programs and community service projects.
summary: AMWA at Drexel cultivates a sense of community and support among students interested in medical and healthcare professions. AMWA is dedicated to advancing women in medicine and improving women's health by empowering women to lead.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers - Drexel Chapter
description:
SHPE's mission is to promote the development of Hispanics in engineering, science and other technical professions within the STEAM fields (Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Math) to achieve educational excellence, economic opportunity, and social equity.  We fulfill this Mission by increasing educational opportunities, promoting professional and personal growth and reinforcing our reputation as a vital Hispanic organization.  We also carry out our social responsibility to be involved in education, business and enhancing pride within our organization.

The Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers (SHPE) was founded in Los Angeles, California, in 1974 by a group of engineers employed by the City of Los Angeles.  Their objective was to form a national organization of professional engineers to serve as role models in the Hispanic community.
The concept of networking was the essential basis for the organization.  SHPE quickly established two student chapters to begin the network that would grow to encompass the nation as well as reach countries outside the United States.  Today, SHPE enjoys a strong but independent network of professional and student chapters throughout the nation. By expanding at an aggressive pace and currently includes 170 student chapters with more than 5300 student members and 42 professional chapters with over 1500 professional members.
 
The Drexel University Chapter was founded in 1992, by a group of engineers who sought the need to expand SHPE to the university.  The student chapter was very active on campus and in the community until it became inactive in 2011. Fresh in 2014-15, we are growing our presence again and plan to re-establish the student chapter that was already in existence while continuing to expand SHPE throughout Drexel University.
summary: SHPE is the leading social-technical organization whose primary function is to enhance & achieve the potential of Hispanics in Science, Technology, Engineering,& Math. We plan to coordinate events that would introduce students to the professional world.

name: Supply Chain Management Association
description:
SCMA empowers Drexel's future business leaders. By enriching the knowledge and career development of its members, our association encourages the interest and promotes the growing importance of supply chain management (manufacturing and production planning, inventory control, ERP or SAP in the large consumer products companies, logistics, operations research, etc.). Various education and networking activities through LeBow C.o.B will be open to all members. 

  • Partnered with Philadelphia's Institute of Supply Management (ISM) - the largest supply management organization in the world with a network of over 50,000 members.
  • Representing the Drexel chapter of Association for Supply Chain Management (ASCM) - global leader in supply chain organizational transformation, innovation and leadership offering certifications and trainings.
summary: SCMA empowers Drexel's future Supply Chain leaders by enriching the knowledge and career development of our members. SCMA encourages interest and promotes the importance of supply chain management education and networking through LeBow C.o.B.

name: Drexel University Debate Union
shortName: DUDU, Drexel Parliamentary Debates, Drexel Debate Society
description:
All of our communications are sent through Discord: Invite link is https://discord.gg/PdxyC75

Spring Practices Tuesday6:30-8 PM and Thursdays 6:00-7:30 PM, Academic Building 217.

 

Welcome to Drexel's premier competitive debate team. We engage in American and British Parliamentary debate formats and practice both leisure and competitive debating. We compete in tournaments across PA, the US and abroad. Some of the most recent tournaments we have represented DUDU at are the BP In person (World University Debating Championships Vietnam '23, Yale IV 2023, Hart House IV 2023, US Universities Debate Championships 2022 (BP Nationals), North American University Debate Championships (NAUDC) 2023), BP Online (World University Debating Championships Belgrade '22, Korea '21, Tokyo IV 2023, Sofia Online 2023, DK Wien Invitational '23, Tabtastic Summer Open '23) and APDA ( Penn 2024, Rutgers 2024, Chicago 2023, North American Debate Championships (NorAms) 2023, Georgetown 2023, Chicago Pro-Ams 2023, JHU BIPOC 2023, APDA Nationals ).

 

We have also hosted two online tournaments in the past, sanctioned Drexel APDA '23 contributing to APDA Nats qual points and Drexel Open '23 which is a BP tournament. 

Drexel University Debate Union is now on Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/drexeldebates/), Snapchat (@drexeldebates), Instagram (@drexeldebates) and GroupMe (https://groupme.com/join_group/39801750/v4yDUQ ). Add us to keep hearing from us about events that will go on campus.
summary: Drexel's premier debate team offering American (APDA) and British Parliamentary (BP) style of debating to build persuasiveness and team-building skills. We compete in local/national APDA/BP tournaments and World University Debating Championships (WUDC)

name: Drexel University Club Sports
description:
NEW CLUB SPORT APPLICATIONS ARE NOW OPEN UNTIL FRIDAY, MAY 1 @ 11:59PM. SCROLL DOWN TO FORMS SECTION ON THIS PAGE AND CLICK ON "NEW CLUB SPORT APPLICATION FOR 2026-2027" TO BEGIN.

 

 

Information on how to sign-up to be eligible for Club Sports participation, scroll down to the "Documents" section of this page and download the "How to Join Drexel Club Sports" document.

 

Drexel University's Club Sports are student organizations created by the students. Clubs may be competitive, recreational, and/or instructional in nature. Depending on the nature of the sport, some Clubs have exclusive rosters that require try-outs to determine their season roster. These sport activities are aimed at developing the personal and athletic interests of students by providing a quality recreation, education, and leadership experience. Club Sports serve as an excellent way to learn technique, strategy, and safety with the goal of competing against other recognized undergraduate collegiate clubs in the region.

 

The Club Sports program features 36 different clubs, featured below:

Badminton

Golf

University Recreation serves as the Club Sports Program administration and is responsible for the development and implementation of the policies of the program.

summary: Drexel University's Club Sports are student organizations created by the students and may be competitive, recreational, and/or instructional in nature.

name: Phi Delta Epsilon (PhiDE)
description:
Phi Delta Epsilon International Medical Fraternity creates physicians of integrity with a life-long commitment to our guiding principles of philanthropy, deity, equity, and education through fellowship, service, mentoring, and formal training. Phi Delta Epsilon has grown from a small insular group to a diverse, all-inclusive organization. We have done this by living our motto: Facta Non Verba, Deeds Not Words. We will continue to prosper, grow, and spread the warmth of our fraternity into the future. We bring in two new classes a year, one each in the Spring and Fall terms. Our philanthropic organization is the Children's Miracle Network (CMN), with The Children's Hospital of Philadelphia (CHOP) as our local philanthropic affiliate. Throughout the year, we host various fundraising and awareness raising events for CHOP and CMN.   

 

International History:

In October of 1904, Aaron Brown and eight of his friends founded Phi Delta Epsilon at Cornell University Medical College. During the first decade of this century, there were many doors closed to Jewish medical students and physicians, doors which would not fully open until after World War II. In 1904, it was not uncommon for American medical schools to have quotas limiting admission of Jewish students, and medical fraternities. So Aaron Brown and his friends decided they would start their own fraternal organization, guided by the precepts of philanthropy, deity, and equity.

The success of the Cornell chapter soon led to the organization of other chapters in the East and Midwest. In 1918, Phi Delta Epsilon amalgamated with Alpha Phi Sigma, a medical fraternity organized in 1908, whose ideals and principles were similar to those of PhiDE. Its chapters were in the Midwest and West, making for an ideal union.

Following the amalgamation, the United States was divided into districts and, by 1930, enough members had graduated from medical schools allowing for the formation of graduate clubs. In 1926, an Endowment Fund was started giving the Fraternity long-term stability. In the 1940s the Aaron Brown Lectureship Program was begun and has remained a vital chapter event through the years.

In the late 1960s, the Fraternity opened its membership to women and encouraged recruitment of medical students of all races, nationalities and religious beliefs. The past 25 years have seen the Fraternity's membership become wonderfully diverse, reflecting the diversity of the medical profession today. The addition of the premedical affiliation in 1994 rounded out the Fraternity's membership, which now spans an entire lifetime of medical education and practice.

PhiDE celebrated its 100th anniversary in 2004 as a strong, vibrant, professionally directed organization headed for new growth and great excitement. The 21st century beckons us, and we continue operate under Aaron Brown's guiding principles of Philanthropy, Deity, and Equity.

 

Chapter History: 

Our chapter of PhiDE was founded by Shane Lewis in June of 2015.  Since our chartering, we have continued to grow and flourish on campus.  We hope to continue to increase our campus presence and offer events that are beneficial to all members of our campus community. For more information about our chapter, email drexel.phi.delta.epsilon@gmail.com or follow us on Instagram @drexel.phide
summary: Phi Delta Epsilon (PhiDE) is an international all-inclusive fraternity for individuals dedicated to the path of medicine. Our chapter, Pennsylvania Gamma, consists of undergraduate students committed to becoming physicians and attending medical school.

name: Club Powerlifting
description: Powerlifting is a strength sport where competitors try to put up the heaviest total amount of weight in the squat, bench press, and deadlift. Our mission is to support powerlifters of all skill levels, whether it's their first time picking up a barbell or they are competing in national-level competitions. We strive to create a positive and encouraging environment for all members to help them achieve their powerlifting goals however we can. We invite any student from Drexel community who wants to compete in powerlifting to join the team. No training experience is required. Just send us an e-mail at drexeluniversitypowerlifting@gmail.com or DM @drexelpowerlifting on Instagram if you are interested in joining!
summary: Our mission is to support powerlifters of all skill levels, whether it's their first time picking up a barbell or they are competing in national-level competitions.

name: College of Computing and Informatics Doctoral Student Association
description: Organization dedicated to developing and maintaining a social and academic community among CCI PhD students. Our goal is to stimulate intellectual, cultural, professional, and social growth among CCI students through academic, social, cultural and interdisciplinary events.
summary: Organization dedicated to developing and maintaining a social and academic community among CCI PhD students.

name: Industrial Designers Society of America at Drexel University
description: Drexel IDSA strives to facilitate opportunities for Drexel students to explore product design and build design skills in an informal setting through exposure to design professionals, workshops, and the broader industrial design community.
summary: Drexel IDSA strives to facilitate opportunities for Drexel students to explore product design and build design skills in an informal setting through exposure to design professionals, workshops, and the broader industrial design community

name: Van Rensselaer Hall
description:
Welcome to Van Rensselaer Hall!

We’re so excited to have you as part of our community. Van R is more than just a place to live—it’s a space to connect, learn, and grow during your time at Drexel.

At Van R., we value:

  • Community & Civic Engagement – building meaningful connections and giving back
  • Learning & Integration – growing inside and outside of the classroom
  • Sense of Self & Purpose – discovering who you are and what drives you

Our staff is here to support you every step of the way:

  • Area Coordinator (AC): Eric Jacobs
  • Residential Desk Coordinators (RDCs): Joy Campbell & Anthony Fowlkes
  • Resident Assistants (RAs): 2: Azmain 3/11: Nihar 4: Piya 5: Divine 6: Adekunmi 7: Albert 8-10: Annette

Check this page often to find upcoming events, ways to get involved, and opportunities to connect with your neighbors. We can’t wait to see you at programs and around the hall!
summary: Anything and everything Van R related. Catch up on news, social media, and events taking place within Van R.

name: American Institute of Architecture Students
description:
The AIAS is a national organization, completely non-profit and student run. Their mission is to promote excellence in architectural education, training, and practice. Its members are the voice of architectural education and the future of the profession across America. And their voices don’t go unnoticed, the AIAS sits in line with four other collateral organizations of architecture at the national level. They advocate for change in studio culture, intern titles, and even student loan forgiveness. They’ve proposed legislation to congress called the National Design Services Act (NDSA), a piece of legislation meant to enable architecture students to have their federal loans forgiven by doing pro-bono design work, much like doctors, lawyers, and veterinarians.

On the local level, our chapters build the leaders that will one day play major roles in architectural education and practice. Through a variety of events we provide members with opportunities to network, volunteer, design, build, and learn with other local students, faculty, and professionals. There are also opportunities to connect with students all across the country when attending either of our two annual conferences, Spring Quad or Forum. AIAS offers members the chance to gain valuable leadership and career development skills, and is a lifetime resource for those in the field of architecture.
summary: AIAS is a national, non-profit and completely student led organization, that focuses on the culture of architectural education and practice. The Drexel Chapter focuses on advancing leadership, design, and service among architecture students.

name: Special Olympics Club at Drexel University
description: Unique student organization offering individuals the opportunity to get involved with Special Olympics Pennsylvania through volunteering, fundraising, unified sports, and Special Olympics events.
summary: Unique student organization offering individuals the opportunity to get involved with Special Olympics Pennsylvania through volunteering, fundraising, unified sports, and Special Olympics events.

name: Women's Empowerment
description: Our goal for Women's Empowerment at Drexel is to empower ALL women. We aim to help women and individuals on campus empower themselves by empowering others. This organization works to create a safe space for women-identifying individuals of Drexel to express themselves and form tight-knit bonds. We invite men and non-binary individuals to join us as well in this movement. This organization would like to be a place for individuals to come together and empower each other. We also strive to find ways to help disadvantaged women in the Philadelphia area. Community engagement events, volunteer opportunities, fun general body meetings, networking opportunities, and social events are all ways that WE reach women-identifying individuals on and off campus.
summary: Women's Empowerment aims to uplift the student body at Drexel University through constructive dialogue, activism, and fun events. Our goal is to empower all women and our allies so they can strive for excellence in their daily lives.

name: Drexel CyberDragons
description:
This organization is for all students who want to learn more about cybersecurity and/or compete in collegiate competitions. With the assistance of security professionals, our goal is to educate members on securing a corporate environment. Members will participate in a wide range of exercises to reach this goal including hands-on labs, collegiate level competitions, and conversations with industry professionals. 

Joining our group here on DragonLink will get you access to get up to date information about club meetings, events, and competitions.

Club meetings usually occur every Tuesday and/or Thursday starting at 5 PM EST. They are also announced via email, so please make sure you select "Join" to stay in the loop. Also make sure to join our Discord server at https://discord.com/invite/GkjSeBd

Questions or concerns? Contact Ariyan Karim at cyberdragons@drexel.edu.
summary: This organization is for students interested in cybersecurity that want to learn more about the field and/or compete in collegiate competitions.

description:
An organization dedicated to integrating Drexel University students of all
disciplines with curiosity, enthusiasm, and motivation to explore aeronautics. The organization meets
with the objective to design, build, and fly model aircraft; and to compete annually in Aero SAE competitions. To this end Aero SAE will teach students the skills needed to design, build, and fly model aircraft of their own.

Flectimur, sed non Frangimur. Ad astra surgimus invicti.

Join us on Teams!
