name: The Accounting Society
description: The Accounting Society aims to prepare all accounting students to become leaders through the betterment of professional demeanor and foundational understandings. Additionally, we aid all Accounting students in understanding the accounting curriculum, CPA exam, and networking through workshops and events that mentor and connect students with hiring employers. We offer peer tutoring on campus and send out weekly e-mails reminding students of upcoming events, scholarship opportunities, other LeBow and Accounting news.
summary: The Accounting Society (TAS) of Drexel University aims to prepare all accounting students to become leaders through the betterment of professional demeanor and foundational understandings.

name: Queer People of Color
description: Queer People of Color (QPOC) is a student organization that aims to address the intersection of race, gender, and sexual identity. Formerly a student-run branch of the LGBTQA Student Center, this group serves as a way to candidly discuss what it means to be both queer and a person of color. The intersection of race and sexual identity will be openly addressed through dialogues, excursions, and informal conversations. QPOC ultimately serves as a resource for queer people of color to come together throughout each academic quarter and develop a strong community at Drexel.
summary: Queer People of Color (QPOC) is a student organization that aims to address the intersection of race, gender, and sexual identity.

name: NAMI on Campus Drexel University
description: NAMI on Campus at Drexel University is a student organization that strives to educate students and our community, raise awareness, and reduce the stigmas that surround mental illnesses. NAMI Drexel provides support to those with mental illness on campus by giving information on how to care for oneself in spite of the stresses of daily life. Our students advocate for accessible mental health services on a local, state, and national scale to enhance the quality of life of those in our community and beyond. On a national level, NAMI was founded as a grassroots organization for families and allies of those living with mental illnesses, and the fundamental building block of NAMI is support. On campus, NAMI helps students thrive in their environment as they adjust to college, receive peer support, and work to advocate for others.
summary: NAMI Drexel educates students and our community about mental illness, strives to reduce stigma, and most importantly, advocates for necessary changes to accommodate those with mental health issues at the university, local, state and national level.

name: Every Nation Campus
description:
Every Nation Campus (ENC) at Drexel University serves as the bridge between Freedom Church of Philadelphia—a local non-denominational church—and its global family, Every Nation. Check out our intro video to see who we are, what we do, and where you can find us!

ENC reflects the incredible diversity of Drexel’s campus. Our mission is to bring together students from every culture, ethnicity, and background to break down social, economic, and academic barriers—walking side by side, united in truth and love. We seek to connect people who might never otherwise meet, establish them in authentic Christian community, empower them to live Spirit-filled lives, and equip them to become influential leaders in the world.

ENC is a community where everyone is welcome to share their story, learn from others, and grow in a deeper relationship with Jesus Christ. We believe that lives transformed by Christ’s love lead to selfless service—making Drexel, Philadelphia, and beyond a better place for all.

Ultimately, ENC exists to raise up the next generation of Christ-centered students and send them out to serve their campus, their city, and the world.
summary: ENC is the Drexel chapter of an internationally established Christian organization that seeks to build up and connect students from various academic/cultural backgrounds through Bible studies and genuine fun, while grounding them in a local church.

name: Dornsife Undergraduate Student Government Organization (USGO)
description: DUSGO consists of an elected body of undergraduate student representatives who serve as liaisons with the administration to advocate for students’ needs. Additionally, DUSGO plans exciting academic, professional, service, and social community-based opportunities for public health students to connect and learn on campus and in Philadelphia. All undergraduate students of the Dornsife School of Public Health shall become members of the Student Government Organization upon enrollment and is open to the Drexel population for membership.
summary: Dornsife USGO is committed to engaging the Drexel community in meaningful opportunities related to public health initiatives, including the determinants of health and health outcomes. USGO values community outreach, collaboration, and innovation.

name: Drexel Pre-Dental Health Society
description:
The Drexel Pre-Dental Society is for students at Drexel University who are trying to pursue a future career in dentistry. The club will provide information on applying to dental schools and the process, which will make them more competitive applicants. Events will be held each quarter for this club. Events will include various speakers and representatives from various dental schools, such as dental students, dentists, or admission officers, who will speak about the admission process in more depth and their experiences in the field. In addition, more information will be given out to the members on shadowing and volunteering opportunities, the Dental Admission Test, and other dental related activities.    

 

Follow us on instagram: @drexelpdhs

 

Please join our groupme for updates and meeting reminders: 

https://web.groupme.com/join_group/93197778/4z2lIrgb?fbclid=PAAabLyyD6iZEwAQH5Bi8omdsSKWhcr8YG-6k2NSMa3Wfo9qcky6fXPg3G7iE_aem_AUW2I0urJrfEqoIydY1Km9jXHWlwmZjR9yJKXe9Ye9n0uNqRVHxhHIsURgCem5A2L8M
summary: Our mission is to assist all pre-dental students in their endeavors related to successful admittance to a prestigious dental school through informative meetings, peer and faculty support, guest speakers, dental school connections, and community service.

name: Dragon Concert Series
description:
DCS is fundamentally about immersing Drexel students into the music and live entertainment industries in multiple relevant capacities. We give students access to music industry career events, panels, and guest speakers, as well as create opportunities for our members to learn about and experience the details of planning concerts and other live performances.


DragonFly
Each year we collaborate with other student organizations to host the largest student-run event on Drexel's campus. With over 1000 attendees each year, DragonFly provides a great opportunity for our members to learn what goes on behind the scenes for coordinating concerts, large-scale events, and live entertainment. We are determined to grow DragonFly and the music scene at Drexel University by providing students with some of the world's most iconic names in music. Past acts include Yung Gravy, MKTO, Tinashe, Slayyyter, Chlöe Bailey, COIN, Flo Rida, Aminé, Kehlani, Lil Uzi Vert, Wiz Khalifa, Lil Wayne, Walk the Moon, and 2 Chainz!

Follow our Instagram @dragonconcerts to keep up with our events and learn about music events happening around Philly!
summary: A student organization dedicated to music industry career development and hands on experience in live music!

name: Delta Gamma – Eta Upsilon
description: Delta Gamma Fraternity is a Greek women's organization here to "Do Good". Delta Gamma fosters high ideals of friendship, promotes educational and cultural experiences, and creates a sense of social responsibility among the members of our sisterhood.
summary: Delta Gamma Fraternity is a Greek women's organization here to "Do Good". Delta Gamma fosters high ideals of friendship, promotes educational and cultural experiences, and creates a sense of social responsibility among the members of our sisterhood.

name: Theta Tau
description:
Theta Tau is the oldest, largest, and foremost Fraternity for Engineers. Since its founding at the University of Minnesota in 1904, over 35,000 have been initiated over the years. With emphasis on quality and a strong fraternal bond, the Fraternity has chapters only at ABET accredited schools and limits the number of student members in any one of its chapters across the nation.

Purpose

The purpose of Theta Tau is to develop and maintain a high standard of professional interest among its members, and to unite them in a strong bond of fraternal fellowship.

Core Values

From recruitment, through pledging, to lifelong brotherhood, our members are:

  • Honest & Ethical
  • Dependable & Trustworthy
  • Respectful of Ourselves and Each Other
  • Generous of Time, Talent, & Treasure for Each Other, Theta Tau, and our Community
  • Proud that we are the oldest, largest, and foremost Fraternity for Engineers

Open Motto

"Whatsoever thy hand findeth to do, do it with thy might;..." --Ecclesiastes 9:10

Important Symbols

Hammer and Tongs, Gear, Jacqueminot, Dark Red Garnet

Guiding Principles

  • Culture of brotherhood, lifelong relationships, and connection
  • Mutual respect and professionalism
  • Balance of social, service, and professional activities
  • Diversity of engineering disciplines and demographics

Our Goals:

For our Brotherhood

We forge lifelong bonds of fraternal friendship, a journey that develops and delivers a network of lasting personal and professional relationships. We foster an inviting, safe, and social environment in which our members become lifelong friends.

For our Profession

We develop and nurture engineers with strong communication, problem-solving, collaboration, and leadership skills that we demonstrate in our profession, our community, and in our lives.

For our Communities

We are known for our service to our college, university and the larger community. Our service projects create a unifying environment for learning and personal growth for our members.
summary: Theta Tau Co-ed Fraternity for Engineers strives to develop and maintain a high standard of professional interest among its members, and to unite them in a strong bond of fraternal fellowship.

name: Drexel University Counseling Center
description:
The Drexel University Counseling Center offers free, confidential, and culturally sensitive care to the Drexel student community. Part of our services include creating and partnering on events that focus on community approaches to mental health. We use this page to spotlight events and workshops that give students a chance to feel seen, heard, and connected to one another. We can't DU this without you but you don't have to DU it alone <3

 

*If you are a student organization or campus partner that would like to co-host an event with us, please reach out to Julie at jh4352@drexel.edu to talk through your idea!
summary: Workshops and events focused on making space for students to feel seen, heard, and connected to themselves and their community.

name: Omega Psi Phi
description: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. was founded November 17th, 1911 on the campus of Howard University by three students with the help of their faculty advisor. Manhood, Scholarship, Perseverance and Uplift are the 4 cardinal principles the organization is founded on. Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Incorporated is the first Black Greek Letter Organization (BGLO) founded on friendship and prides itself on a rich history of highly influential members who had a significant impact on the way the United States and world at large was shaped from it's inception in 1911 to the present day.
summary: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. was founded November 17th, 1911 on the campus of Howard University by three students with the help of their faculty advisor. Manhood, Scholarship, Perseverance and Uplift are the 4 cardinal principles the org is founded on.

name: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority Inc.
description:
Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated was founded January 16th, 1920 on the campus of Howard University. Founded by five undergraduate coeds, Zeta Phi Beta continues to raise awareness, encourage the highest standards of scholasticism, and foster a greater sense of unity and sisterhood among members. The Kappa Sigma chapter of Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc. was founded March 18th, 2005 on the campus of Drexel University by six determined women. Kappa Sigma continues to cultivate the ideals of piety, academic excellence, integrity, ingenuity, enlightenment and transcendence.

2023 Student Organization of the Year
summary: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc. was founded on the principles of scholarship, service, sisterhood and finer womanhood. The Kappa Sigma chapter works to positively impact Drexel University and the surrounding community.

name: Recreation Center
summary: Student Employment Application Hub for the Drexel Recreation Center

name: Drexel Criminal Justice Society
description: The mission of The Drexel Criminal Justice Society is to educate the members and the community about the professional criminal justice field through hands-on experiences while upholding integrity and respect to all.
summary: We will have a meeting once a week on Drexel campus to schedule, discuss, and participate in events that are in the criminal justice field. We will also have field trips and community events in Philadelphia.

name: Maternal and Child Health Student Organization at Drexel University
description: The mission of this organization is to explore a broad range of maternal and child health topics, including birth outcomes, family planning, the role of fathers, child and adolescent health, environmental conditions that impact the health of mothers and children through a collective effort involving guest speakers, article discussions, and community activities in order to disseminate maternal and infant/child health information to our peers and the community around us.
summary: The goal of Maternal and Child Student Organization (MCHSO) is to explore a broad range of topics that impact the health of mothers and children through presentations by guest speakers, article/current event discussions, and MCH promotional events.

name: Depict
description: Depict is a community for students who want to create art. Depict allows aspiring artists to expose themselves to different perspectives while creating their own styles. Students can collaborate on work, help each other improve, and bond. We also want to help those interested in art to explore their inner talent. We're just here to help you discover your talent.
summary: Depict is for the artist (you). This club is for anyone of any skill level. It's about honing your skills as an artist and finding other people who share the same interest.

name: Drexel University Real Estate Club
description:
FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM AND LINKEDIN: SEE BELOW

 

To be considered for membership in the Drexel University Real Estate Club, request to join on this page on DragonLink!

Our Story

We’ve been in your shoes – and we get how tough it is to break into the real estate industry. For that reason – our founders chose to assemble a professional real estate organization on campus for giving you – the students – a competitive advantage in this industry.

DUREC provides career development, educational events, mentorship, and networking opportunities in real estate for students at Drexel University. Our club extends beyond the classroom and provides numerous opportunities to interact with prominent industry executives.

Our Vision

  1. Build a tight-knit community between students, faculty, alumni, and industry experts passionate about the real estate industry.
  2. Actively invite real estate professionals to speak and interact with students on campus.
  3. Provide a variety of learning opportunities such as tours for students to meet prospective employers, project site visits, and opportunities to gain first-hand experience through case competitions and assignments directly from industry professionals.

Our Structure

Our members are full of passion and competitiveness; that is precisely what we wish to see in our prospective members. Our goal is to ensure that students will be relentless in maximizing their real estate potential. We would love to have you be a part of out networking/social events!
summary: Our club brings together and connects Drexel Students with the most prestigious professionals in Philadelphia Real Estate. We provide Drexel Students with educational sessions and networking events that enrich their personal and professional knowledge.

name: Business Analytics Club
description:
Welcome to the Business Analytics Club page!

Our mission is to provide students across all majors with accessible resources to enhance and reinforce their competency in data analysis. Our organization offers various educational workshops and presentations, as well as networking and career opportunities in the growing field of business analytics.

BAC strives to promote:

1. Educational Growth - through skill building workshops taught by volunteer students and professionals to assist members who seek to build new skills

2. Professional Growth - by encouraging members to branch out of their comfort zone to participate in competition outside of class

3. Social Growth - by looking for ways to collaborate with other student organizations on campus to develop and build new relationships

We strive to gather a community of students who are devoted, dedicated, and willing to invest their time in an organization that promotes these core values. To be granted membership, prospective members must register through the Business Analytics Club’s page on DragonLink. Current members do not need to re-register each academic year unless prompted by Campus Engagement.

Current members who wish to be recognized as Active Members must complete the Active Member Agreement Form, which can be found here: Active Member Agreement Form. This form confirms your commitment to meeting the event and participation requirements outlined by the club each quarter.

Join BAC and become part of a family of individuals who are diverse thinkers, advocates of teamwork, continuous in improving their community, and willing to learn and enhance their analytical skills.
summary: The Business Analytics Club is comprised of dedicated students who strive to improve academically, professionally, and socially through a collaboration and continuous improvements.

name: Pi Lambda Phi - PA Delta Iota
description: Founded at Yale University in 1895, Pi Lambda Phi (Pilam) was the first non-sectarian fraternity in the United States, accepting men of good character without regard to race or religion. Today, Pilam chapters at colleges and universities in the United States and Canada cultivate a brotherhood that promotes academics, community service, philanthropic engagement, leadership development, financial responsibility, social skills, and lifelong relationships. These values and the skills learned through the process help Pilam men succeed in their post-graduate endeavors. All of this is offered to undergraduate members in an environment free from hazing and drug & alcohol abuse.
summary: Founded at Yale University in 1895, Pi Lambda Phi (Pilam) was the first non-sectarian fraternity in the United States, accepting men of good character without regard to race or religion.

name: Neurodragons
description: Neurodragons is a dedicated body of neurodivergent and neurotypical students committed to raising awareness of the value of neurodiversity within the Drexel learning community and beyond. The purpose of this organization shall be to raise awareness of the value of neurodiversity within the Drexel learning community and beyond. Neurodragons shall share the following values and promote to all faculty and students at Drexel University: promote acceptance of social behaviors of all those who work, teach, and study at Drexel University; partake in initiatives for autism awareness month, and mental health week; lead campaigns to finally, create programs to aid in the elimination of the stigma surrounding mental health, particularly autism.
summary: Neurodragons is a dedicated body of neurodivergent and neurotypical students committed to raising awareness of the value of neurodiversity within the Drexel learning community and beyond.

name: Drexel K-Pop Club
description:
DKPC aims to create a welcoming environment that brings together people with a mutual passion for Korean pop music, K-Pop. Encouraging enthusiasm for K-Pop and providing a space for members to freely express their interests serve as motivation to learn more about the Korean culture and language, resulting in more globally-minded individuals. We center most events around general Korean pop culture and dance: 

 

DKPC GB 

Do you like K-pop and Korean pop culture?.....We do too :D  

Join DKPC and find your community! Photocard decorating, K-drama movie nights, and dance workshops (hosted by DKPC Prism), are just a few of the fun events you could enjoy if you joined our club.

 

DKPC Prism

Are you a dancer?!?.....You should audition for our dance team!!  

DKPC Prism is our growing family of dancers who all share a passion for dance covers and performing. Our team gets invited to perform both on and off-campus and we also host our Annual “DKPC Countdown” to showcase our talented dancers. 

Join Prism if you are…

  • passionate about (K-pop) dance
  • value communication and community
  • able to teach yourself choreo 
  • available to practice on weekends!!! 

 

* Dance Workshops

As mentioned above, DKPC Prism hosts dance workshops of your favorite K-pop songs! Don’t think you can dance? FALSE! Everyone can dance and our workshops are open to ALL levels. Follow our instagram (@drexelkpopclub) for updates on our next workshop!
summary: DKPC aims to develop students' interests in Korean music and culture by providing diverse resources to learn the dance, the pop culture, and the language, as well as to interact with people who share a common interest.

name: Rho Psi Eta, Pre-Health Sorority, Gamma Chapter
description: Rho Psi Eta is a pre-health academic sorority, the home for aspiring women in health and medical professionals. We work together to empower and encourage one another to fulfill our common goals of constantly upholding excellent standards of academics, honesty, and integrity while maintaining a commitment to excellence in our personal and professional lives.
summary: Rho Psi Eta is a pre-health academic sorority that thrives on the passion of sisterhood, service, academic excellence, and the health/medical field.

name: South Asian Students Association
description: The South Asian Students Association (SASA) looks to foster a community of South Asian students. Members of this organization will conduct social and cultural events to spread knowledge about South Asian culture as well as participate in service endeavors to make a difference on our campus and city. SASA will provide students with networking opportunities with peers and others and help students to find a sense of family within Drexel. It will be an active organization in hosting events and providing all students the opportunity to learn, give back, network, make friends, and have fun.
summary: Drexel University's South Asian Student Association (SASA) looks to foster an all-inclusive community and aims to increase the awareness of South Asian students by participating in and hosting social, professional, service, and cultural-driven events.

name: Retail and Design Club
description: Retail and Design Club is an organization that offers students the opportunity to immerse in the fashion industry through on campus events, speakers, and networking. We aim to create a space for students interested in fashion to have fun, network, and benefit from professional development. Some of our favorite events are Fashion Study Abroad and Co-op Panels, Mood Board Nights, and Clothing Swaps! We are open to all drexel students with an interest in fashion.
summary: Retail and Design Club is an organization that offers students the opportunity to immerse in the fashion industry through on campus events, speakers, and networking. Open to all Drexel Students.

name: Public Health Doctoral Students Advisory Group
description:
Public Health Doctoral Students Advisory Group (PHDSAG) Organizational Goals/Objectives

 

  1. Foster community for DSPH doctoral students through social events.
  2. Foster professional and skills development for DSPH doctoral students through events, networking, workshops, and co-working spaces.
  3. Foster additional support from Dornsife School of Public Health (DSPH) administrators and faculty members to enhance the development of leadership, research, and professional skills of doctoral students.
  4. Create opportunities and generate an environment for doctoral students for exchanging ideas and peer-review of ongoing and future research projects, papers, and any other academic pressing concerns.
  5. In partnership with administrators of DSPH, create channels of communication and platforms through which doctoral students can voice their concerns, share their ideas, and give feedback to administrators and anyone who has decision-making power.
  6. Create opportunities to communicate research, resources, and the connection of networks within DSPH and the community at large.
summary: Public Health Doctoral Students Advisory Group (PHDSAG) is comprised of doctoral students (PhD, DrPH) at the Drexel Dornsife School of Public Health. PHDSAG focuses on doctoral-specific activities and advocacy at Dornsife.

name: Drexel Consulting Group
description:
Welcome to the Drexel Consulting Group!

The Drexel Consulting Group serves members of the Drexel community who share a common interest in learning about consulting. We connect undergrads with resources to develop the skills, knowledge, and network to succeed in consulting. Throughout the year, we offer many exciting events such as, speaker panels, case competitions, and community events.

Join our DragonLink to stay updated on events and case competitions!

Questions? Email us at thedrexelconsultinggroup@gmail.com.
summary: The Drexel Consulting Group serves members of the Drexel community who share a common interest in learning about consulting. We connect undergrads with resources to develop the skills, knowledge, and network to succeed in consulting.

name: Dragon's First
description: Dragon’s First is the Drexel Chapter of a Philadelphia-based nonprofit, the Collective Success Network (CSN). CSN supports low-income, first-generation college students by achieving their academic and career aspirations through mentorship, professional development, networking, and leadership opportunities. Here at Drexel, we aim to build a supportive community for all FGLI students by providing professional resources, engaging workshops, and social events. We are here to assure that no one is alone in their academic journey.
summary: A student organization that supports first-generation college students and students from a low-Income background at Drexel University through mentorship, networking, workshops, and social events.

name: North Hall
description:
Welcome to North Hall!

We’re so excited to have you as part of our community. North Hall is more than just a place to live—it’s a space to connect, learn, and grow during your time at Drexel.

At North Hall, we value:

  • Community & Civic Engagement – building meaningful connections and giving back
  • Learning & Integration – growing inside and outside of the classroom
  • Sense of Self & Purpose – discovering who you are and what drives you

Our staff is here to support you every step of the way:

name: MEDLIFE
description:
Our mission is to help families achieve greater freedom from the constraints of poverty, empowering them to live healthier lives. Our patients did not choose to be poor, but they have chosen to strive toward a better life; MEDLIFE stands beside them in this pursuit.
 
We aim to achieve this goal through partnering with motivated individuals in poor communities working to improve their access to MEDs: Medicine, Education and community Development. MEDLIFE believes access to quality healthcare is a basic human right.
 
To this end, we commit our time, resources, knowledge and hope to bring Medicine, Education and Development to Low Income Families Everywhere. We wish to extend this mission to our own backyard, here in Philadelphia. By partnering with local community service organizations, students in our organization will help fight against poverty.
summary: MEDLIFE is an organization that partners with low-income communities in Latin America and Africa to improve their access to medicine, education, and community development initiatives through volunteer trips.

name: Kappa Delta Pi
description: Greetings!   Welcome to the homepage of the Alpha Theta Chi Chapter of Kappa Delta Pi. The Alpha Theta Chi Chapter is an international honor society for undergraduate and graduate students within the School of Education.  Students who are in the Teacher Education Program and Dragons Teach Program are eligible to be invited based on their GPA.  The motto of Kappa Delta Pi is based on three pillars:  Knowledge, Duty, and Power.  Kappa Delta Pi members are all over the world and notable members include Howard Gardner, John Dewey, Eleanor Roosevelt and Albert Einstein.
summary: National Society of Education Chartered at Drexel June 7th, 2017 Purpose: To foster excellence in education and promote fellowship among those dedicated to teaching.

name: International Council on Systems Engineering
description: The purpose of the Drexel INCOSE (International Council on Systems Engineering) Organization is to provide an enriched environment for systems engineering students to learn and engage with professionals in the field of systems engineering outside the classroom environment. This student chapter organization is connected with the Delaware Valley Chapter INCOSE professional society. The organization also provides network opportunities for students to engage with industry and professionals for potential employment opportunities in a systems engineering organization following graduation.
summary: The purpose Drexel INCOSE is to provide an enriched environment for systems engineering students to learn and engage with professionals in the field of systems engineering outside the classroom environment.

name: Students of Color in Psychology
description: Our mission is to prompt comprehensive educational, cultural and social discussions and events structured to combat bias, bigotry, and racism, while promoting intercultural dialogues, awareness, and respect for diversity. We strive to be positive and productive members of society in order to uplift and empower marginalized groups. We promote proactive leadership, scholarship, community outreach, and unity. SOCPSY is an inclusive group that welcomes and embraces all working towards our core values and goals.
summary: We are a group of undergraduate and graduate Drexel University psychology students that identify as people of color. Our drive is to promote the growth of our students in the field of psychology through event hosting, networking and sharing knowledge.

name: Center for Learning and Academic Success Services
description:
The Center for Learning and Academic Success Services (CLASS) empowers students to develop skills and strategies that foster academic success and personal growth through purposeful interactions with peer and professional staff.

CLASS supports Drexel University students' efforts to achieve their academic goals through a variety of services, including:

  • Academic Coaching
  • Tutoring
  • GSTD 100: Strategies for Academic Success
  • Workshops and Events

 

CLASS is a member of the Drexel University Learning Alliance.
summary: CLASS provides academic support to Drexel University students, including academic skills workshops and peer and professional academic coaching.

name: Drexel Motion Capture
description: The Drexel Motion Capture Club is a group of passionate students who want to learn more about motion capture and its multiple uses in the industry!
summary: To further the knowledge of motion capture technology through self guided and group oriented projects. We will engage our peers and provide an opportunity for discussions on special topics that utilize Drexel’s many resources in our fields of study.

name: DrExcel Health Undergraduate Association
description:
Develop your skills in another specialty! No experience required and all majors are welcome!

DrExcel Health is the first interdisciplinary organization and healthcare-specific incubator at Drexel University. The main purpose behind this organization is to combine the knowledge of medicine, business, entrepreneurship, engineering, among other specialties, from its members to facilitate progress on various projects and skills. The organization will be conducting a series of diverse workshops aimed at enhancing essential skills within the aforementioned fields. The organization also provides an opportunity for professional development in the life sciences career path through guest spotlights, faculty mentorship, and business model competitions.

Lab Projects: In addition to the workshops, members of the organization have the opportunity to take part in our lab projects. Before the pandemic, we have completed initial research and prototyping on a call sensor for patients with ALS Locked in Syndrome. In the future, we plan to embark on a new project targeting an unmet medical need including a research project collaboration with a Drexel professor. Lab meetings are student led and driven and students of all interests and skills are encouraged to join!
summary: DrExcel Health provides a platform to facilitate interdisciplinary collaboration between students to tackle unmet medical and humanitarian needs and provide an opportunity for professional skill development in the life sciences career path.

name: Friends of Doctor’s Without Borders
description:
Doctors Without Borders (MSF) is a non-profit, self-governed organization. Today, MSF is a worldwide movement of 24 associations, bound together as MSF International, based in Switzerland. Thousands of health professionals, logistical and administrative staff – most of whom are hired locally – work on programs in more than 60 countries.

MSF’s work is based on humanitarian principles. We are committed to bringing quality medical care to people caught in crisis, regardless of race, religion or political affiliation. MSF operates independently. We conduct our own evaluations on the ground to determine people’s needs. More than 90 percent of our overall funding comes from millions of private sources, not governments. MSF is neutral. We do not take sides in armed conflicts. We provide care on the basis of need. We push for independent access to victims of conflict as required under international humanitarian law.

Being part of a Student Chapter for Doctors Without Borders entails:

-Raising awareness and knowledge about MSF’s work in the field and about humanitarian issues.

-Encouraging students to consider working with non-governmental organizations, such as MSF, post-graduation.

-Supporting MSF in advocacy campaigns.

-Raising money for MSF, our work in the field, and specific campaigns.
summary: Doctors Without Borders / Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF) is an international, independent, medical humanitarian organization that delivers emergency aid to people affected by armed conflict, epidemics, natural disasters and exclusion from healthcare.

name: LeBow Student Leadership
summary: LeBow's Dean's Student Advisory Board and Peer Leaders will be working together to create a great impact on the LeBow College of Business.

name: Biomedical Science Graduate Student Association
description:
The Graduate School GSA represents over 800 students and provides a variety of services to enhance the Drexel experience. The GSA executive boards oversee but are not limited to:

  • Creating a community within their respective programs, divisions and the Graduate School as a whole.
  • Creating and supporting professional and career development opportunities.
  • Student representation and voting on the Graduate School Committees, including the Graduate Council.
  • Travel awards for the biomedical science programs.
  • Supporting annual events such as orientation, commencement and faculty awards.
  • Student mentoring program.
  • Networking and social events.
  • Oversee community outreach, initiate service projects and volunteer opportunities.

As stated in the GSA bylaws, the purpose of this group is to:

  • Operate in compliance with the by-laws of the Biomedical Graduate Student Association;
  • Advise the University Student Council on matters of mutual concern to the School of Biomedical Sciences and Professional Studies and the School of Medicine;
  • Provide a channel for communication among the graduate students, the University, and the community;
  • Serve the graduate students and represent their interests in the University and the community;
  • Promote understanding and intellectual stimulation among graduate students, the University, and the community;
  • Improve the position of graduate students in academic and extracurricular matters;
  • Elevate the quality and standards of graduate education;
  • Guarantee the rights and privileges of graduate students as members of the University and the community.
summary: Biomedical Science Graduate Student Association

name: Pre-Medical Pre-Health Graduate Student Association
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to assist students in the process of applying for admission to graduate healthcare-related institutions; including but not limited to MD, DO, PA, PT, OT, etc. This organization shall also facilitate a sense of community by providing students with events, outreach opportunities, and informal membership, ultimately encouraging a rewarding student experience.
summary: Pre-Medical Pre-Health GSA

name: Interdisciplinary and Career-Orientated Programs Graduate Student Association
description:
The Graduate Student Association (GSA) of the Graduate School of Biomedical Sciences and Professional Studies consists of three executive boards, one for each division in the Graduate School. 

The Graduate School GSA represents over 800 students and provides a variety of services to enhance the Drexel experience. The GSA executive boards oversee but are not limited to: Creating a community within their respective programs, divisions and the Graduate School as a whole. Creating and supporting professional and career development opportunities. Student representation and voting on the Graduate School Committees, including the Graduate Council. Travel awards for the biomedical science programs. Supporting annual events such as orientation, commencement and faculty awards. Student mentoring program. Networking and social events. Oversee community outreach, initiate service projects and volunteer opportunities.
summary: Interdisciplinary and Career-Orientated Programs GSA

name: Drexel Athletics
summary: #FearTheDragon

name: Middle Eastern and North African Club
description:
The Middle Eastern and North African Club aims to provide Drexel students with opportunities to explore, appreciate, and celebrate the diverse cultures, histories, and languages of the MENA region. Our mission is to foster a welcoming and inclusive community for all students—whether they identify with the region or are simply curious to learn more. Through cultural events, student-led discussions, and educational activities, we highlight the richness and complexity of the MENA region.

We hope to create a space where MENA-identifying students feel represented and supported, where international students from the region find community, and where students of all backgrounds can engage meaningfully with a part of the world that is often underrepresented on campus.
summary: This organization is for individuals who have an interest in or have a background within the Middle Eastern and North African region. We focus on sharing and celebrating MENA language, history, food, and culture with our members.

name: Drexel Society of Artificial Intelligence
description:
The Drexel Society of Artificial Intelligence (Drexel AI) seeks to help Dragons get familiar with Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning fields. AI is evolving quickly. People who manage to stay familiar with the cutting-edge technology of AI has simply more advantage in today's world. Drexel AI aims to promote the power of this field, and help Dragons have an advantage in this quickly changing world through hands-on software projects and academic research. 

If you're interested come join our Discord!
summary: Drexel AI promotes the field of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML), increases awareness, and helps students get familiar with the state-of-art technological applications of AI

name: Drexel University Coffee Club
description: DUCC encourages Drexel students to explore the extensive Philadelphia coffee scene on weekend mornings.  One of Philly's hidden cultural gems is it's many highly rated coffee shops.  DUCC also encourages students to explore life outside of campus and will help students form new friendships with other Drexel students over a cup of coffee.  Additionally, these coffee shops provide the perfect relaxed environment for students to not only socialize but also get some work done over the weekend.
summary: DUCC encourages Drexel students to taste and learn more about Philadelphia's cultural coffee scene, explore life outside of campus, and form new friendships over a warm cup of coffee.

name: Drexel Animation Group
description:
The Drexel Animation Group (DAnimate) aims to create a community of artists, animators, and animation enthusiasts to produce and share various forms of animation. DAnimate focuses on animation outside the focused area of 3D that is currently being taught at Drexel as well as integrating the animation knowledge with the curriculum to enhance the skill set of the student body.  Some of the types of animation explored are but are not limited to, stop motion, 2D, hand-drawn, motion graphics, projection mapping, and experimental animation. DAnimate also aims to enhance the skills and understanding of its members through its weekly meetings, and hold workshops to explore different animation media through screening and discussions.

Please join our Discord Server here: https://discord.gg/EKxNfRj for news and updates!
summary: The Drexel Animation Group focuses on creating and studying animation outside of the 3D program currently offered at Drexel. This includes, but is not limited to, stop motion, 2D/traditional, and experimental animation.

name: Drexel CHI User Experience Club
description:
Drexel CHI User Experience Club is a campus chapter of ACM SIGCHI. We are dedicated to providing students with interest in user experience, computer-human interaction, and design a place to share ideas, collaborate and develop professionally.

We aim to show creative students how they can translate their passion into a sustainable career by offering specialized workshops to promote good practices in the field of user experience and providing students with a community of like-minded individuals.
summary: Drexel CHI User Experience Club is a campus chapter of ACM SIGCHI. We are dedicated to providing students with interest in user experience, computer-human interaction, and design a place to share ideas, collaborate and develop professionally.

name: Drexel Career Cornerstone Group
description:
Drexel Career Cornerstone Group (Drexel CCG) is a graduate-level student organization focused on bridging the knowledge gap between students and early career stages. Drexel CCG strongly believes that along with academic knowledge and institutional experiences, it is equally important to learn about the challenges faced by recent graduates during their job application process and after they have secured a job. 
As such, we offer an innovative network platform for current graduates through interactive panel discussions, young professional speaker series, and hard skills-based workshops. Participants will receive valuable knowledge regarding different industries and discover available career paths and career advancement opportunities. Members are consistently engaged in the planning and execution of a variety of projects, honing team-working skills and gaining experience in an industry-like working environment.
summary: Drexel Career Cornerstone Group is a graduate student organization focused on bridging the knowledge gap between students and early career stages through skill-based workshops, professional events, and engaging membership and leadership experiences.

name: Persian Students Association
description:
• Our goal at PSA is to uplift the spirits of our students by organizing various Iranian events and celebrations, such as but not limited to:

 

  • We plan various events like potlucks, karaoke nights, movie nights (once a month), small meet and greet sessions with new students, and even host local music bands. Let's get started!
  • Create a thriving Persian community at Drexel by utilizing social networking platforms such as Twitter and Instagram.
  • Plan welcoming events for new arrivals.
  • It is possible for us to extend invitations to guest speakers.
  • You tell us what else can we do?

 

*** To stay updated on the latest PSA news and connect with the community, Please follow our Instagram page or kindly send us an email requesting to join our Telegram group chat.
summary: This organization tries to bring together the Persian community by celebrating cultural events and welcoming newcomers into Drexel for a smoother transition to American society.

name: School of Education Graduate Student Organization
description: The School of Education’s graduate student association, SoE GNOMEs, fosters community between all Masters, Ed.D. and Ph.D. students through professional development, networking, and service opportunities. GNOMEs is committed to addressing issues of social justice in P-20 education systems in Philadelphia, nationally, and worldwide. Events will be directed by the entire membership and reflect the current needs of the SoE graduate population.
summary: This organization aims to provide professional development and networking opportunities to the graduate students in the School of Education. The School of Education Graduate Student Association is for all Masters, Ed.D, and Ph.D students.

name: Sanitation Health Aid Relief Project
description: SHARP is an organization that aims to raise awareness about water, sanitation, and hygiene(WASH) issues faced by marginalized populations locally and globally. SHARP is a product of the Dornsife Global Development Scholars program. Therefore, SHARP will primarily serve countries that host Dornsife Scholars and the home of our university-Philadelphia. SHARP plans to host fundraising efforts regularly. Our efforts will focus on donating money and items such as pads, soap, and water purification tablets.  SHARP will provide a platform for students and faculty to brainstorm and design sustainable solutions for WASH-related problems.
summary: Sanitation Health Aid Relief Project (SHARP) is a student-run organization that aims to address water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) challenges both locally in Philadelphia and on an international scale.

name: Club Golf
description:
The Drexel Golf Team competes in regional tournaments with 16 other colleges and universities in the Philadelphia area. There are 6 regional tournaments a year that take place at country clubs within two hours of Philadelphia. There are also two national tournaments, which can only be attended by teams or individual players who have qualified in previous regional tournaments. The team aims to expand upon its current roster by introducing new members to the world of competitive golf through friendship and camaraderie.  

Check out the National Collegiate Club Golf Association web page here: https://nextgengolf.org/app/teams/drexel-university-club-golf

If you are interested in joining, please reach out to us via email or text. Requests to join our team via Dragonlink are not checked frequently, and you must contact us directly if you want to join the team!
summary: The focus of the Drexel Golf team is to promote the game of golf to those who would like to begin or continue to play the game at a competitive level. The team fills the gap between playing for Drexel's division one golf team and playing for fun.

name: Dornsife Center for Neighborhood Partnerships
description:
Who We Are

MISSION STATEMENT

The Dornsife Center brings Drexel’s academic and administrative expertise into the neighborhood, engaging students, faculty and administrators with community residents in creative collaboration to address matters of shared importance.

VISION STATEMENT

Mantua, Powelton Village and Drexel University are strong, vibrant and effective because they are doing shared public work to advance their individual and collective interests. Education and civic life are strengthened when all participate in this public work.

 

What We Do

The Dornsife Center convenes programming for a diverse community by weaving together Drexel’s knowledge resources with the expertise of community-based partners. We aim to address the needs and interests of our stakeholders in a way that strengthens, empowers, and educates while maintaining a strong commitment to economic and social justice. It is home to a number of services and programs that provide needed resources to the community. These include Drexel-led programming such as the Beachell Family Learning Center's open computer lab and career workshops, Andy and Gwen Stern Community Lawyering Clinic’s pro-bono legal assistance, and the Community Wellness HUB’s health and wellness services and programming. The Dornsife Center also host several youth, arts, and educational activities led by both university and community partners such as music lessons, dance classes, youth martial arts, qigong classes, and nutrition and cooking workshops.

 

We offer programming in the following general areas:

  • Adult Education and Workforce Access
  • Arts and Culture
  • Health and Wellness
  • Creating Knowledge Together
  • Kids and Families
  • Building Educational Opportunities
  • Individual and Family Supports

Sign up for our volunteer opportunities at Drexel's Galaxy Digital
summary: The Dornsife Center for Neighborhood Partnerships is located at 3509 Spring Garden St - north of main campus. It convenes programming for a diverse community by weaving together Drexel’s knowledge resources with the expertise of community-based partners.

name: Delta Sigma Pi
description:
We believe that the primary purpose of a business fraternity is to prepare students to be ethical leaders in their careers, but membership in Delta Sigma Pi extends far beyond the undergraduate experience. We are known as the foremost business fraternity because we equip our members throughout their lives to achieve maximum impact in their studies, careers, communities, civic involvement, and personal relationships.

As America's first co-ed professional business fraternity, Delta Sigma Pi takes our commitment to diversity seriously. We accept business majors from all backgrounds, regardless of race, gender, creed, or lifestyle, believing that everyone has a valuable perspective and that all of our members can benefit from learning different points of view and developing friendships based on mutual respect.

 

**PLEASE DO NOT REQUEST TO JOIN OUR ORGANIZATION ON DRAGONLINK UNLESS YOU HAVE BEEN EXTERNALLY APPROVED AND EXPLICITLY TOLD TO DO SO BY A MEMBER OF OUR CHAPTER'S EXECUTIVE BOARD
summary: Delta Sigma Pi is the nation’s leading co-ed professional business fraternity, with a commitment to equipping our members throughout their careers to make a greater impact in their business, civic, and personal lives.

name: Dragons for UNICEF
description: Dragons for UNICEF aims at creating opportunities for Drexel students to defend children's rights, raise awareness about issues concerning children's survival both locally and globally, and raise funds for UNICEF.
summary: Dragons for UNICEF aims at creating opportunities for Drexel students to defend children's rights, raise awareness about issues concerning children's survival both locally and globally, and raise funds for UNICEF.

name: Lambda Alpha Upsilon
description: The mission of Latino America Unida, Lambda Alpha Upsilon Fraternity, Inc. is to be a network of professionals that promotes brotherhood, scholarship and service to the community. The fraternity promotes brotherhood by providing its members personal and professional support on an undergraduate and alumni level. Our members pursue scholarship not only for professional advancement, but to attain personal growth. The brotherhood serves the community by sponsoring events which aide those in need, promoting cultural awareness, and enriching the collegiate environment. Every member must continue to uphold the mission and goals of the fraternity, as they are the common values that distinguish us as Hermanos of Lambda Alpha Upsilon.
summary: Latino America Unida, Lambda Alpha Upsilon Fraternity, Inc.

name: Kappa Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc.
description:
Kappa Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. is a nonprofit, Asian-interest sorority that was founded on March 9, 1995 at SUNY Binghamton in Binghamton, New York. Our sorority was conceived through the notion of an organization that would provide a culturally sound and educationally inspiring grounding for Asian-American women. 

 

We distinguish ourselves on our three pillars: Sisterhood, Service, and Cultural Diversity.

 

Although each chapter of Kappa Phi Lambda is unique in its approach to promoting these pillars, they are the foundation of our sorority and the virtues that bring us together in sisterhood. These values do not make us better than anyone; rather, they allow our sisters to become stronger women, provide service, and help advocate for cultural diversity within the greater community.
summary: Kappa Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. is a nonprofit, Asian-interest sorority with an emphasis on service and commitment to giving back to our members. Our three pillars are service, sisterhood, and cultural diversity.

name: Drexel University Club Ski and Snowboard Competitive Team
description: The Drexel University Club Ski and Snowboard Competitive Team competes against regional schools in various events, such as ski/snowboard racing and freestyle competitions.
summary: The Drexel University Club Ski and Snowboard Competitive Team competes against regional schools in various events, such as ski racing and free style competitions.

name: Special Events and Free In Philadelphia
description:
The Special Events team of Enrollment Management and Student Success is excited to bring you unique programming throughout the year!

Are you looking for some incredible things to do at Drexel and in and around Philadelphia but don't want to spend a lot of money? Check back this page for news articles and events with FREE TICKETS for students!
summary: EMSS Special Events and Free in Philadelphia - A listing of free events and programs for students!

name: Drexel Dragon Claws
description:
Who We Are:

Drexel Dragon Claws (DDC) is a graduate student-lead organization and a recognized chapter of the e-NABLE community. Through e-NABLE, we create 3D printed prosthetic devices for upper-limb amputation patients in the Greater Philadelphia area. Additionally, we 3D print medical models for the PMPH community here at Drexel.

 

What We Do:

Patients with amputations or limb abnormalities often experience barriers to accessing much needed assistive devices due to their high cost and limited insurance coverage. Pediatric patients, in particular, require frequent prosthetic fitting and resizing, which further exacerbates the cost barrier. Drexel Dragon Claws uses 3D-printing to provide free prosthetics to reduce the cost and accessibility barriers for these patients. We also use our 3D-printing capacity to create medical models for the PMPH community to aid students who are more visual and kinesthetic learners.
summary: Our mission, as an independent chapter of e-NABLE, is to design 3D-printed assistive prosthetic devices, enhancing the functionality and mobility of affected limbs for individuals with upper limb amputations or reductions, at no charge to our clients.

name: Drexel Dhvani
description: Dhvani is Drexel’s only co-ed South Asian a cappella group, dedicated to celebrating and sharing South Asian culture through music. We create and perform original arrangements that blend traditional South Asian melodies with modern pop, all using only our voices. Our fusion style offers a fresh and dynamic sound that reflects both cultural richness and musical innovation. By performing and competing on national stages, we aim to showcase the diversity of our music and connect with audiences of all backgrounds. Through every performance, we strive to inspire curiosity, appreciation, and love for this unique musical genre.
summary: Dhvani is Drexel's only South Asian A Capella group! We perform original arrangements, and are excited to join the collegiate network for A Capella. We strive to share our passion for music and culture with others looking to explore this innovative style

name: Drexel Dance Marathon
description: The purpose of the Drexel Dance Marathon organization is to raise awareness and funds for the families of patients at the Children's Hospital of Philadelphia through the Children's Miracle Network Program. Drexel Dance Marathon will support research and discovery, the patient-family experience, training and education, advocacy and outreach through fundraising and bringing understanding to this cause. Our goal is to unite Drexel students of all areas of interests and backgrounds through serving the Philadelphia community for a common cause. Looking to join our team or find out how to join as a participant? Email Anna Till, Membership Development Director at apt54@drexel.edu for more information!
summary: The purpose of the Drexel Dance Marathon organization is to raise awareness and funds for the families of patients at CHOP through the Children's Miracle Network Program. This is a great opportunity for leadership and development.

name: Pennoni Honors College
description: The College sponsors initiatives that serve all Drexel students interested in pursuing excellence. We encompass two units: our Academic Programs unit includes the Honors Program, the Custom-Designed Major, the interdisciplinary problem solving minor, and the theme-based Symposium; and Undergraduate Research and Enrichment Programs offers research through STAR and SuperNova; mentoring through the Aspire Program; and opportunities to prepare and apply for competitive grants and fellowships. Please check out the links for more information on these units and the events and programs associated with them.
summary: Pennoni Honors College

name: Drexel Esports Club Sports Team
description:
Drexel Esports - the student-run esports club under Club Sports, competing in collegiate esports leagues and tournaments. Over 9 game titles. Home of 4x National Champions. 

Feel free to check us out on Discord, or see our Linktree for all our socials!

https://discord.gg/fnf7tPvuFj | Drexel Esports | Twitter, Instagram, TikTok, Twitch | Linktree
summary: Drexel Esports Club Sports Team

name: Center for Black Culture
description:
The Center for Black Culture (CBC) at Drexel University serves as a hub of information, activity, and community for Black students, faculty, staff, and alumni. The CBC aims to increase knowledge, recognition, and respect of the people, histories, and cultures of the African diaspora and its many contributions to the world. All programs and services offered through the CBC are open to members of the Drexel community who want to gain a greater understanding of the Black experience and support a welcoming and wholly inclusive campus environment.

The Center for Black Culture is a university department that works closely with student organizations and other campus partners
summary: The Center for Black Culture (CBC) is a university department that serves as a hub of information, activity, and community for Black students, faculty, staff, and alumni.

name: Drexel's Association of Health Administrators
description: Our goal is to provide undergraduate and graduate students majoring or minoring Health Services Administration educational and networking opportunities and resources. This will be a space where people can find different resources and have a community outside of the classroom!
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to provide undergraduate and graduate Health Services Administration Students educational and networking opportunities.

name: Saving Mothers
description: Saving Mothers is a chapter of an organization dedicated to raising awareness and funds for underprivileged mothers and pregnant women in need of reproductive health care services. This chapter will also be highlighting women’s health issues across the globe including but not limited to the inequities of women in health care systems, maternal mortality rates, and pregnancy complications. Community outreach, volunteering opportunities, and speaker events will also contribute to the mission of this chapter.
summary: Saving Mothers is an organization dedicated to raising awareness and funds for underprivileged mothers and pregnant women in need of reproductive health care services.

name: Students Supporting Israel
description:
Our mission is to be a clear and confident pro-Israel voice on college campuses and to support students in grassroots pro-Israel advocacy

Our goals and activities include familiarizing students and the university community with current events in the Middle East, sharing Israeli culture on campus, and responding to bias anti-Israel propaganda when spread by other members of the academic community. We believe in sharing knowledge with students about Israel’s history, its diverse people, and its day-to-day reality, as Israel should not be looked at solely through the lens of conflict but as a nation with a legitimate and unique story, values, and aspirations.

We believe in the idea of Zionism, and as stated in the Declaration of the Establishment of the State of Israel that the Jewish people have the natural right "to be masters of their own fate, like all other nations, in their own sovereign state... a Jewish State."
summary: Students Supporting Israel is an independent, nonpartisan, grassroots campus movement that organizes a strong and united pro-Israel front on college campuses. We are made up of students from different political, religious, and ethnic backgrounds.

name: Drexel CHAARG
description:
Interest Form for additional info: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScYmBeFIIlzNYdL-9FPyGCEIu3MpngtGba6w4VgXRXJwwM3ow/viewform?usp=pp_url

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Oz8bv3TQdDw
summary: CHAARG is a health + fitness organization that liberates students from the elliptical + show them that fitness should be fun. We partner with local fitness studios every week and empower our members to become the best version of themselves.

name: I AM ME
description: • I am ME is a professional organization at Drexel focused on promoting equal partnership, equal opportunities, and in-creased visibility for female engineers in Mechanical Engineering. Through the creation of a safe space for female engineers, we strive to have an inclusive, respectful, and diverse space for all Drexel students. By connecting members with alumni and other women in ME, the organization helps build a base of support through networking, mentorship, and experiences that also educates members on how to succeed in the professional world. We will pursue our mission by advocating for women, educating them through workshops, seminar speakers, social activities, outreach, and other events.
summary: I am ME is a professional organization at Drexel focused on promoting equal partnership, equal opportunities, and increased visibility for female engineers in Mechanical Engineering. We will educate members through workshops, seminars, and more.

name: Hack4Impact Drexel
description: Building powerful nonprofit software as a tool for social good. We help nonprofits do the good they are meant to be doing more efficiently. By building them the custom software and web/mobile applications they need, they can focus on their important work.
summary: Building powerful nonprofit software as a tool for social good. We help nonprofits do the good they are meant to be doing more efficiently. By building them the custom software they need, they can focus on their important work.

name: Graduate Christian Fellowship at Drexel University
description:
Graduate Christian Fellowship’s mission is to cultivate Christian faith within the graduate student community. GCF was founded on the foundations of fellowship, worship, and empowerment to provide students perspective on addressing challenges and celebratory moments as graduate students. We offer sources of prayer and support, without the barriers of denominational traditions. Our gatherings serve as a tool for graduate students to utilize on their journey of remembering and becoming.

GCF exists for three purposes: 

To connect fellow graduate students across campus with other Christians.

- Dispersed around campus, the GCF focuses on connecting Drexel's graduate student Christian community and building a strong foundation in Jesus Christ among fellow graduate students.

- Because of the nature of the graduate student life, frequent gatherings are held to encourage the incorporation of faith into a daily routine.

- Because of the nature of professional development, quarterly gatherings are co-hosted to connect Drexel's graduate student Christian community with Drexel's Faculty Christian community.

To provide Drexel's graduate student Christian community a place to strengthen their Christian faith.

- GCF gatherings include but are not limited to Bible studies, Scripture readings, testimony sharing, social activities, prayer groups, and luncheons.

To equip Drexel's graduate student Christian community with the love of the gospel to empower their graduate careers.

- Every graduate student has areas in their professional development that they are actively working on to improve; GCF comes alongside them with the resources available through community and faith in Jesus Christ and equips them to accomplish positive change in their work environment.
summary: The Graduate Christian Fellowship (GCF) at Drexel University provides graduate students and post-doctoral fellows an environment in which the Christian faith is encouraged.

name: Drexel Society of Manufacturing Engineers
description:
Drexel SME is based in the Department of Engineering Technology. This organization focuses on the development of manufacturing engineering by promoting manufacturing technology. Through this process, we hope that our members acquire valuable skills that are applicable in the workforce, strong networking skills and contacts for future opportunities of greater development, and various certifications relating to mechanical engineering and manufacturing.

 

Additionally, we are recognized by the National Society for Manufacturing Engineers as Drexel Chapter S058.

 

Join us on Discord to stay in the loop on the latest meetings, events, and team engagement!
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to help manufacturers innovate, grow, and prosper by promoting manufacturing technology, developing a skilled workforce, and connecting the manufacturing industry.

name: Drexel Business Services
summary: To be updated

name: Perfect Pair - Drexel University Chapter
description: Perfect Pair fosters one-on-one, intergenerational connections between older adults and college students. We strive to reinforce a mutual sense of community and purpose for our pairs: a goal that underlies our efforts to combat loneliness and improve overall health and wellbeing. We achieve this by ensuring that our pair connections are personalized: based on shared background and interests. Simultaneously, we encourage pairs to re-engage with their passions by offering individualized and accessible programming. We hope for older adults and students to feel that their life perspective and quality of life have been meaningfully enhanced by their experience in our program.
summary: Individualized one-on-one through an extensive matching process that considers background, hobbies, and interests, college volunteers are paired with a senior in a local assisted living facility.

name: American Society for Engineering Management - Drexel Student Chapter
description: The American Society for Engineering Management - Drexel Student Chapter is an organization open to all persons looking to learn more about the field of engineering management and how to grow their managerial and leadership skills in engineering in their academic and professional careers via professional development, networking, and national community involvement.
summary: Drexel ASEM is an organization for all to learn more about engineering management as well as participate in mentorship, professional development, and personal growth events while learning more about the field.

name: Writers Room
description: Writers Room is Drexel's community literary arts program engaged in creative placemaking and art for social justice. We are a diverse, intergenerational collective of students/alumni, faculty/staff, and neighborhood residents whose work demonstrates a desire for collaborative opportunities in our joint communities.  Please join Writers Room for our free and open to all events such as Second Tuesdays, Thursday evening Creative Writing Kickbacks, monthly Open Mics, and much more.
summary: Writers Room is a university-community literary arts program engaged in creative placemaking and art for social justice. Our mission is to develop inclusive, intergenerational, co-creative places that foster connection and community.

name: Drexel Computational Design
description:
Drexel Computational Design (DCD) is a student-led organization that empowers students to apply programming, design, and creative problem-solving to real-world challenges. From AI to data tools, and from vibecoding to prototyping impactful builds, DCD offers a collaborative space where bold ideas come to life through code.

 

The organization serves as both a project incubator and a mentoring hub—bringing students together to prototype solutions across domains using tools like Python, MATLAB, and beyond. By following the engineering design process, members tackle open-ended problems that go beyond textbooks, challenging each other to think critically and build purposefully.

 

DCD also hosts learning community hours for students at all skill levels, offering peer-led sessions, tool deep-dives, and skill-building jams. Whether you're aiming to sharpen your technical edge, co-build something ambitious, or just vibe with code—you’ll find your place here.

 

Our goal is simple: Code smart. Build bold. Learn together.
summary: Drexel Computational Design (DCD) is an interdisciplinary student organization that brings together engineering, design, AI, and research to prototype bold, real-world solutions through code.

name: College of Medicine
summary: The Office of Student Affairs provides guidance, support and administrative assistance to all MD program students.

name: Philly Maza
description:
Philly Maza is Philadelphia's premier Coed Bollywood Fusion Dance team. We aim to integrate South Asian dance styles such as Bharatnatyam, Bhangra, and Bollywood with Western influences like Hip-hop and Contemporary dance. Our mission is to create a community where people from diverse backgrounds can come together to celebrate their passion for dance while also providing a meaningful way to stay connected to South Asian culture and roots. We pride ourselves on dancing, competing, and most importantly, having fun! We compete nationally in different cities and are constantly looking for new talent who want to continue their passion for dance.

 Follow us on @phillymaza on instagram tuned for tryouts coming in the Fall & Spring.
summary: Philly Maza is Philadelphia's premier Co-Ed Bollywood Fusion Dance team. We integrate South Asian dance styles such as Bharatnatyam, Bhangra, and Bollywood with hip-hop and contemporary dance.

name: Climate and Sustainability EcoReps
description:
Climate and Sustainability’s EcoReps Program is a group of student environmental leaders who are dedicated to increasing opportunities for student awareness of sustainability on campus. Drexel EcoReps hold on-campus events for students and staff to learn more about how we as an institution can decrease our carbon and pollution footprint. Students can get involved as either a casual volunteer or as a more active member of the events and planning teams.

Affiliated with Drexel's Office of Climate and Sustainability, EcoReps are students dedicated to increasing both opportunities for and student awareness of sustainability on campus

https://bit.ly/EcoRepsLinkTree

Instagram: sustainabledrexel

Meetings are Wednesday’s 2-4 at the PSA building!
summary:
Climate and Sustainability’s EcoReps Program is a group of student environmental leaders dedicated to increasing both opportunities for student awareness of sustainability on campus.
https://bit.ly/EcoRepsLinkTree
Insta:sustainabledrexel
Wednesdays 2-4

name: Drexel University Book Club
description: DUBC members gather on the first Sunday evening of each month to discuss an agreed-upon book. Each month's book is determined by vote at the prior meeting. Genre and length vary depending on member interest. Our goal is to promote reading and critical reflection while fostering a sense of community. Regular reading is proven to improve attention, memory, and vocabulary - not to mention, it broadens your perspective on the world around you. Avid readers, new readers, and anyone in between are welcome!
summary: DUBC members gather once a month to discuss an agreed-upon book. Avid readers, new readers, and anyone in between are welcome!

name: ASHRAE
description: Drexel University's ASHRAE Student Branch seeks to provide opportunities for learning and networking to college students considering pursuing careers in/related to heating, ventilation, air-conditioning and refrigeration.  As a student branch of ASHRAE, we aim to advance the arts and sciences of heating, ventilation, air conditioning and refrigeration to serve humanity and promote a sustainable world. They organize events such as facility and factory mechanical system tours, information sessions, and guest speakers from the HVAC community. DU ASHRAE members participate in Philadelphia Chapter ASHRAE events such as bowling nights and other networking events.
summary: Drexel University's ASHRAE Student Branch seeks to provide opportunities for learning and networking to college students considering pursuing careers in/related to heating, ventilation, air-conditioning and refrigeration.

name: Lit Lab
description: Lit Lab was created in an effort to bridge the gap between STEM and the humanities together through a student-run blog that publishes submissions quarterly. Our intention is to cultivate a creative space on campus that allows for interdisciplinary writing and sharing of ideas.
summary: Lit Lab was created in an effort to bridge the gap between STEM and the humanities together through a student-run blog that publishes submissions quarterly

name: Drexel International Business Student Association
description: The International Business Student Association has a mission centered around increasing cultural awareness in business settings. Additionally, we aim to provide the Lebow and Drexel community at large the opportunity to network and build professional relationships. Through this association, students will be invited to networking events, field trips around the Philadelphia Area to increase knowledge of the international business environment, and build relationships with students who hold similar interests. This association intends to promote Drexel University’s mission of inclusion and the creation of a diverse community by promoting events for all students, while also promoting the major and minor of International Business within Lebow College of Business.
summary: Our organization aims to create a community of culturally diverse members by arranging various speaker events and focusing on career-oriented programming. Members will increase their global awareness and grow professionally.

name: Sexual Exploration and Comfort in Restorative Educational Training
description:
S.E.C.R.E.T. reimagines sexual health and wellness at Drexel. We offer innovative approaches to sex education through workshops and collaborations with various organizations and professionals from diverse fields. We host interactive activities on campus that welcome dialogue surrounding sexuality, sexual violence, sexual policy, and so much more! Our group is inclusive of all ages, genders, and sexual identities, as we believe every person deserves a chance to participate in our reinvented Sex Talk!

Please email secretdrexel@gmail.com with any membership or e-board inquiries!
summary: We are the reinvention of sexual education at Drexel! We normalize healthy dialogue on the basis of sex through fun and interactive events, alongside open minded workshops and seminars regarding sexual health and policy.

name: Drexel Night Live
description: Drexel Night Live is a "Saturday Night Live" style sketch comedy show entirely written, produced, and performed by students. DNL performs a quarterly show in front of a live audience, consisting of live and pre-recorded sketches.
summary: Saturday Night Live style sketch comedy show entirely written, produced, and performed by students.

name: Drexel Business of Fashion
description: If you are interested in the business part of the fashion industry, want to learn more about fashion history, branding strategies, and opportunities to get a job in fashion, as well as meet like-minded people - DBOF is the perfect fit for you! Join us on DragonLink and follow us on instagram to stay in touch with all the news and updates.
summary: Drexel Business of Fashion is a community of like-minded people where business meets fashion. Our organization provides students with opportunities to learn about the business side of fashion, attend thematic events, and network.

name: Application First Aid
description: Remember applying to medical school? The personal statement, secondaries, interviews…the agonizing wait?? To get any help, you either had to know someone or spend hours scrolling through reddit. “Medical School Consultants” exist, but cost hundreds to thousands of dollars on top of an already exorbitantly priced application.
