Application First Aid (AFA) aims to fill this gap by providing free application assistance for medical school applicants. We provide one-on-one mentorship for students from a variety of undergraduate universities on personal statements, secondary essays, and interview preparation. We especially hope to reach individuals who belong to underrepresented groups in medicine. Be an AFA mentor, and help make this process a little better for someone whose shoes you were once in.
summary: Application First Aid

name: DUCOM Movember
description: The theme of Movember is changing the face of men's health, and the month's purpose is to raise awareness about three of the biggest health risks affecting men: prostate cancer, testicular cancer, and mental health issues. Our club will focus on raising funds and awareness for men’s health.
summary: Men's health awareness

name: Cardiothoracic Surgery Interest Group
description: Our group serves as an introduction to the exciting and evolving field of cardiothoracic (CT) surgery. We aim to inform students about the surgical treatment of all visceral organs residing in the thorax, including the heart, lungs, esophagus and major vessels in this region. We are fully devoted to providing interested students with guidance regarding a career in CT surgery, helping them to become ideal candidates for residency/fellowship.
summary: Our main goal is to introduce students to the field of cardiothoracic (CT) surgery. Additionally, we hope to provide guidance and connections to help those become ideal candidates for residency.

name: Sports Medicine Interest Group
description: The Sports Medicine Interest Group aims to offer medical students exposure to physicians, fellows, and residents in the field, as well as organize events with local sports teams that allow for hands-on exposure. Sports medicine is a broad branch of medicine that encompasses internal medicine, emergency medicine, family medicine, pediatrics, PM&R, and orthopedic surgery. We plan to provide medical students with experiences within these different specialties while also highlighting key differences.
summary: SMIG is an organization that helps medical students explore the field of sports medicine

name: Pediatric AIDS Benefit Concert
description: The Pediatric AIDS Benefit Concert is an annual gathering of Drexel University College of Medicine's most musically talented students and faculty with the goal of raising money to benefit patients of the Dorothy Mann Center for Pediatric and Adolescent HIV at St. Christopher's Hospital for Children. The Dorothy Mann Center provides hope and quality patient-centered care to children and families affected by HIV in the Philadelphia area. The money raised during the benefit concert helps to provide affected families with emergency funds for housing, utilities, food, rent, and other expenses. To date, PABC has raised over $650,000 to benefit children at St. Christopher's Hospital for Children.
summary: PABC is an organization committed to providing financial support for the children and families affected by HIV in the Philadelphia area.

name: Medical Interview & Assesment Club
description: Wanting to get more comfortable with the medical examination process? Feeling comfortable but wanting additional practice? Join the Medical Interview and Assessment Club! The Club is designed to increase DUCOM student's confidence in applying the skills learned in Foundations of Patient Care for future physician-patient interactions. We aim to provide a space for students for practice their interview and assessment skills with feedback and guidance from a clinician or mentor.  This is a great opportunity to hone your clinical skills during first and second year and feel confident by the time you get to clerkships.  We will have both student-led and physician-led sessions to workshop different parts of the interview, assessment, and oral presentation of your findings.
summary: The Club is designed to increase DUCOM student's confidence in applying the skills learned in Foundations of Patient Care for future physician-patient interactions.

name: Health Outreach Project West Reading
description:
Do you want an opportunity to get hands on experience in your clinical skills while serving the local community? Health Outreach Project (HOP) in West Reading aims to work in partnership with the Reading community to provide caring, nonjudgmental, and interdisciplinary health services to individuals who have limited or no access to care. Our HOP clinics and programs strive to create a respectful environment in which patients, students, community members, and health professionals work and learn together to address community health issues in innovative ways. HOP programs provide opportunities to address obstacles to health care, including language and cultural barriers, hours of operation, lack of insurance, homelessness, and drug and alcohol addiction.
 
We have clinic sites and projects for a variety of interests. Our Hope Rescue Mission Clinics include a Street Medicine Clinic, Telehealth Clinic, Screening Eye Exam Clinic, and Neuromuscular Screening Clinic. Our Greater Reading Clinics include FaithCare Clinic and Western Berks & Mary’s Shelter Clinic. For projects, we have opportunities to serve with the LGBT Center, Vaccine Hesitancy, and Naloxone Outreach. As a newer campus, we have many opportunities to grow, so bring your own ideas for us to help you bring to a reality!
summary: Health Outreach Project (HOP) in West Reading aims to work in partnership with the Reading community to provide caring, nonjudgmental, and interdisciplinary health services to individuals who have limited or no access to care.

name: DUCOM Journal Club
description: The mission of DUCOM Journal Club is to prepare students for a career rooted in evidence-based medicine. Journal Club focuses on developing students’ abilities to analyze and critique scientific literature, implement appropriate statistical methods, understand proper study design, and efficiently communicate scientific findings.
summary: Journal Club

name: Persian Student Association
description: Persian Student Association (PSA) is a cultural club focused on Persian culture and practices. Our goal is to create a community of students who are interested in the Persian culture whether they identify as Persian or not. DUCOM is a very diverse medical school filled with many students from different backgrounds. We aim to gather all students that are interested in different cultures in our club. We will educate, share, and promote our cultural events, creating a tight-knit community that shares similar values.
summary: Persian Student Association (PSA) is a cultural club focused on Persian culture and practices.

name: DUCOM Wellness
description:
Our mission is to foster a culture of holistic well-being at DUCOM by creating accessible, student-centered programs that support mental, physical, social, and emotional health. We aim to strengthen community, promote balance, and empower students to prioritize wellness throughout their medical education.

The DUCOM wellness organization is a collaborative, student-driven group dedicated to supporting the well-being of the entire DUCOM community. Wellness representatives from each society work collectively to identify student needs, develop programming, and host events that encourage healthy living and community connection. The group partners closely with the DUCOM Office of Student Affairs to provide a wide range of wellness initiatives—such as mindfulness workshops, fitness activities, stress-relief events, peer-support gatherings, resource awareness campaigns, and campus-wide wellness weeks.

In addition to event planning, the group advocates for student wellness within DUCOM by gathering feedback, promoting available resources, and integrating wellness into the culture of medical education. Membership is open to all elected wellness representatives, with opportunities for broader student involvement through event participation and volunteer support.
summary: This group unites wellness reps from each DUCOM society to design and promote student-centered wellness programs. In partnership with Student Affairs, we provide meaningful, inclusive, and engaging wellness opportunities throughout the year.

name: Hematology/Oncology Interest Group
description:
The aim of the DUCoM Hematology/Oncology interest group is to help educate students on Heme/Onc and expose them to different elements of the field so that they can make informed professional and career decisions. Join our GroupMe for more info! 

https://groupme.com/join_group/96365092/uYp7CuV7
summary: The aim of the DUCOM Hematology/Oncology interest group is to help educate students on Heme/Onc and expose them to different elements of the field so that they can make informed professional and career decisions. Join our GroupMe with the link below!

name: Attending Musicians
description: Attending Musicians is an organization that is dedicated to bringing together the diverse members of the DUCOM community, including students, teachers, clinicians, healthcare workers, and staff, through music. We aim to utilize our musical talents to engage with each other and the surrounding community through initiatives such as hosting mini-concerts and fundraising events at DUCOM and other local community facilities. All medical students are welcome to join regardless of their musical instrument, skill level, or genre of interest!
summary: Attending Musicians is an organization that is dedicated to bringing together the diverse members of the DUCOM community, including students, teachers, clinicians, healthcare workers, and staff, through music.

name: Reproductive Endocrinology Interest Group
description: The Drexel University College of Medicine Reproductive Endocrinology and Infertility Interest Group aims to increase exposure of medical students to the field of reproductive medicine. We hope to increase awareness and understanding of hormonal function as it pertains to reproduction as well as the issue of infertility. Networking events will also be available for members in order to form relationships and pursue research opportunities with reproductive endocrinology and infertility specialists. All medical students are welcome to join the group regardless of their level of interest or knowledge.
summary: The Drexel University College of Medicine Reproductive Endocrinology and Infertility Interest Group aims to increase exposure of medical students to the field of reproductive medicine.

name: DUCOM Black Doctor Network
description: DUCOM Black Doctor Network (DBDN) is a student-led, professional development organization created to provide opportunities for Drexel medical students who identify as Black/African American to become proactive in networking and building meaningful relationships with mentors. The goal of DBDN is to serve as a conduit for medical students to find shadowing opportunities, strategic counseling/guidance, and research opportunities as they prepare for a career in medicine. DBDN is a collaborative organization dedicated to improving the pipeline of Black students interested in medicine.
summary: DBDN is a student-led, professional development organization created to provide opportunities for Drexel medical students who identify as Black/African American to become proactive in networking and building meaningful relationships with mentors.

name: Health Outreach Project
description: Drexel University College of Medicine Health Outreach Project (HOP) aims to work in partnership with the community to provide caring, nonjudgmental, and interdisciplinary health services to individuals who have limited or no access to care.
summary: Drexel University College of Medicine Health Outreach Project (HOP) aims to work in partnership with the community to provide caring, nonjudgmental, and interdisciplinary health services to individuals who have limited or no access to care.

name: Stress and Anxiety Management for Medical Professionals
description: Stress and Anxiety Management for Medical Professionals (SAMMP) at DUCOM is an organization designed with the goal of shedding light on the importance of mental health management, both as a medical students and as practicing healthcare providers. Considering the high rates of burnout, anxiety, and stress that many physicians endure in silence, it is a topic that merits some acknowledgment and discussion. With that being said, DUCOM’s SAMMP will work to enlighten medical students on stress, mental health and preventing burnout in the field. SAMMP will work closely with DUCOM Psych/Soc advisors in order to conduct informative sessions on medical school stress and physician burnout, delving into the risks and management techniques. Through sessions designed for communicating with other medical student peers, knowledgeable faculty, and even guest speakers, SAMMP hopes to guide DUCOM students into the professional field with mental health self-awareness!
summary: Supporting students and clinicians and shedding light on the importance of mental health management as medical professionals.

name: Anesthesiology Interest Group
description: Anesthesiology Interest Group (AIG) aims to educate and immerse students in the field of anesthesiology. AIG strives to help students understand and experience what being an anesthesiologist entails. This is achieved through a variety of ways. AIG hosts numerous workshops, such as an intubation workshop, where students can interact with anesthesiology attending physicians and residents to learn basic skills. Monthly meetings also include information sessions, where students can learn about anesthesiology, as well as about any new developments in the field. Additionally, AIG serves as a bridge to help Drexel students connect with students and physicians at other institutions and Anesthesiology events that take place off campus both inside and outside the Philadelphia community.
summary: Anesthesiology Interest Group (AIG) aims to educate and immerse students in the field of anesthesiology.

name: Student National Medical Association
description:
The Student National Medical Association (SNMA) is committed to supporting current and future underrepresented minority medical students, addressing the needs of underserved communities, and increasing the number of clinically excellent, culturally competent and socially conscious physicians. Our mission is fulfilled through community service/outreach in poverty-stricken communities, providing exposure to medical specialties by physician discussion panels, and providing support to our current SNMA student body members.
 
Our vision is to be inclusive of all and providing support to the future generations of physicians that have a passion for equitable access to medical care and mentoring those that will come after us to promote educational and cultural growth.
summary: SNMA is committed to supporting current and future underrepresented minority medical students, addressing the needs of underserved communities, and increasing the number of clinically excellent, culturally competent and socially conscious physicians.

name: Drexel African Medical Student Association
description: The Drexel African Medical Student Association’s mission is to share African culture on our campus, and to engage individuals in dialogues that will create a deeper appreciation and understanding of the diverse cultures within the continent. We will strive to provide a network of support for all members, linking them to other students, alumni, physicians, and leaders in the community as needed. We welcome not only African students but all students interested in understanding Africa and what it has to offer. We aim to create a home away from home.
summary: DAMSA's mission is to share African culture on our campus, and to engage individuals in dialogues that will create a deeper appreciation and understanding of the diverse cultures within the continent.

name: DrExcel Health
description: We are DrExcel Health, an interest group at Drexel University College of Medicine that facilitates collaboration between medical, biomedical engineering, business, and entrepreneurship students to address pressing questions that face the medical industry today. We aim to do this by forming interdisciplinary student teams that closely work with a physician mentor and experienced researchers. In the year-long project, teams identify a current problem, design a solution, and ultimately share their work with others. Past projects include developing novel approaches to orthopedic surgery techniques, apps to support those with debilitating illnesses, and assessing transplant organ viability! In addition, we aim to host a longitudinal seminar series featuring talks from entrepreneurs from the biotech industry.
summary: DrExcel Health facilitates collaboration between medical, biomedical engineering, business, and entrepreneurship students to address pressing questions that face the medical industry today.

name: LGBTQIA+ Professionals in Medicine
description: LGBTQIA+ Professionals in Medicine seeks to enhance the health of the LGBTQIA+ community through community social support, advocating for curricular and policy change in the field of medicine, and educating students and staff in the greater DUCOM community on issues pertaining to sexual and gender minorities.
summary: LGBTQIA+ Professionals in Medicine seeks to enhance the health of the LGBTQIA+ community

name: East Asian Students and Scholars Association
description: The East Asian Students and Scholars Association (EASSA) is a multicultural group which seeks to provide a social network to the scholars and scientists at DUCOM and to help them develop their careers and promote their academic success. We hope to build a community of individuals with an interest in and respect for East Asian cultures. EASSA fosters friendship among students through social interaction, cultural activities, and educational exchanges. We hope to serve as a bridge for intercultural communication between the Philadelphia communities and student groups at DUCOM.
summary: EASSA is a multicultural group which seeks to provide a social network to the scholars and scientists at DUCOM and to help them develop their careers and promote their academic success.

name: Asian Pacific American Medical Student Association
description: The Asian Pacific American Medical Student Association (APAMSA) is a national organization of medical and pre-medical students committed to addressing the unique health challenges of Asian and Pacific Islander American (APIA) communities. APAMSA serves as a forum for student leaders to engage these health issues and develop initiatives and projects addressing those needs. The local, regional, and national activities of APAMSA aim to promote the health of the AANHPI community and help healthcare workers understand how to care for AANHPI patients in a culturally sensitive manner. Finally, APAMSA provides an important venue for medical students to meet, exchange experiences, and develop personally and professionally through leadership and service.
summary: APAMSA is a national organization of medical and pre-medical students committed to addressing the unique health challenges of Asian and Pacific Islander American (APIA) communities.

name: Students for a National Health Program
description: Students for a National Health Program (SNaHP) is a national organization that advocates for a single-payer healthcare in the United States, as opposed to the current for-profit system. We believe that a single-payer system will significantly reduce the cost of healthcare for Americans, expand access to care, and make progress toward medical equity among people of all ethnic, racial, and class backgrounds. We strive to accomplish our mission through education of healthcare systems at large, tabling events, and encouraging healthy discourse. We are closely affiliated with Physicians for a National Health Program (PNHP).
summary: Students for a National Health Program (SNaHP) is a national organization that advocates for a single-payer healthcare in the United States, as opposed to the current for-profit system, via educational series.

name: Maimonides
description: DUCOM Maimonides is an interest group for Jewish students at Drexel University College of Medicine. It is our goal to create a welcoming environment for our Jewish students and provide programming and enrichment events to foster a sustainable Jewish community within our campus.
summary: Maimonides goal is to create a welcoming environment for our Jewish students and provide programming and enrichment events to foster a sustainable Jewish community within our campus.

name: DUCOM Muslim Students Association
description: The MSA aims to create an inclusive community for Muslim students at DUCOM. We hope to provide a safe space to discuss our common faith, hold social events and religious gatherings, and bring awareness about Muslims in the healthcare field at large.
summary: The MSA aims to create an inclusive community for Muslim students at DUCOM.

name: RISE PHILADELPHIA
description: RISE Philadelphia is a virtual tutoring program that was founded by a group of medical students across Philadelphia at the start of the COVID-19 pandemic in March 2020. Millions of students across the world were forced to adapt to an uncertain in-home online curriculum and so graduate school students came together to provide one-on-one virtual tutoring sessions to support Philadelphia and South Jersey students’ transition to this new remote learning environment. Now, hundreds of students in the Philadelphia school systems continue to use and benefit from this non-profit organization and the free tutoring it offers.
summary: RISE Philadelphia is a virtual tutoring program that was founded by a group of medical students across Philadelphia at the start of the COVID-19 pandemic in March 2020.

name: Family Medicine Interest Group
description: The DUCOM Family Medicine Interest Group is the on-campus group dedicated to helping students obtain more information about the Family Medicine specialty. Specifically, we aim to provide students with information about what they can expect throughout residency and post-residency, as well information about the different ways that they can tailor the specialty to fit their interests. To accomplish this goal, the group organizes a mentorship program, in which students are matched with current residents. We also host several talks that allow current FM residents to share their stories about their experiences with the specialty and/or subspecialties. We also provide students with hands on opportunities to learn clinical skills performed by FM physicians by hosting clinical skills workshops with partnering residency programs.
summary: The DUCOM Family Medicine Interest Group is the on-campus group dedicated to helping students obtain more information about the Family Medicine specialty.

name: Research Interest Group
description: The mission of the Research Interest Group is to (1) increase research opportunities for Drexel medical students, (2) provide guidance to students interested in pursuing research, and (3) improve the student research experience in partnership with the Office of Student Affairs and Career Advising.
summary: The mission of the Research Interest Group is to increase research opportunities, provide guidance to students interested in pursuing research, and improve the student research experience in partnership with the OSACA.

name: Women In Surgery
description: Our mission is to provide a networking platform for female medical students to gain exposure to the surgical field by meeting female surgeons in the region. With this platform, students can learn more about opportunities in the surgical field. Through professional networking opportunities, clinical skills workshops and panels, the interest group will provide female students with the resources to succeed in their career choice and engage with other aspiring female surgeons in the area. By fostering an environment of opportunity and inclusion, we aim to encourage greater female representation in the surgical field.
summary: Our mission is to provide a networking platform for female medical students to gain exposure to the surgical field by meeting female surgeons in the region.

name: American Association of Neurological Surgeons Medical Student Chapter
description: The purpose of Drexel University College of Medicine’s American Association of Neurological Surgeons (AANS) Medical Student Chapter is to foster professional growth and career development of members of DUCOM who are interested in the field of neurosurgery by providing them with early support and leadership training. This organization will provide a forum for students to organize professional, educational, and research activities. The national recognition of AANS allows our members to have a voice in the field, even as a medical student. AANS also provides many other advantages to its medical student chapters including access to educational materials, networking events across the country, grants available for educational and research programs, service and volunteering opportunities, as well as opportunities to collaborate with other AANS Medical Student Chapters. AANS organizes speaker series, case presentations, and neurosurgical demonstrations for student exposure to the field. This organization also hosts regular programming through journal clubs, mentorship opportunities, and social functions.
summary: AANS Medical Student Chapter is to foster professional growth and career development of members of DUCOM who are interested in the field of neurosurgery by providing them with early support and leadership training.

name: Cutting Edge Anatomy
description: Cutting Edge Anatomy seeks to introduce basic anatomy and physiology to the youth of our community, while serving as mentors for students interested in a career in healthcare. Working with students from local schools, we will utilize engaging activities such as Kahoot, anatomical models, interactive presentations, possible field-trip(s) to DUCOM, basic case-based learning activities, crafts, and scavenger hunts. Our group will meet and prepare hands-on curriculum, while forming relationships with local organizations and interacting with their students. By exposing younger members of our community to these concepts early on in their education, we can promote a growing interest and passion for science within medicine.
summary: Cutting Edge Anatomy seeks to introduce basic anatomy and physiology to the youth of our community, while serving as mentors for students interested in a career in healthcare.

name: DUCOM Med-Peds Interest Group
description: The Med-Peds Interest Group aims to educate DUCOM students on the combined training program that ends with a dual certification in both Internal Medicine and Pediatrics. We hope to provide information sessions and professional contacts so that students can explore Med-Peds and learn about the career flexibility that this path has to offer. Over the course of the year, the group will host informational panels about the specialty, a mentorship program, and an opportunity to refine clinical decision making through solving resident-facilitated case studies. Med-Peds is a great residency option for students seeking a versatile and engaging career in a supportive environment!
summary: The Med-Peds Interest Group aims to educate DUCOM students on the combined internal medicine and pediatrics residency in order to help them decide if this route of training aligns with their career goals after medical school.

name: OB/GYN Interest Group
description: The Ob/Gyn Interest Group aims to provide opportunities for medical students to explore their interest in the specialty through the organization of speaking events, panel discussions, skills workshops, and shadowing. It is our goal to act as a resource for students who are thinking about entering the field of obstetrics and gynecology by providing them with options to connect with current physicians, explore the various disciplines encompassed in Ob/Gyn, and practice skills associated with the specialty.
summary: The Ob/Gyn Interest Group aims to provide opportunities for medical students to explore their interest in the specialty through the organization of speaking events, panel discussions, skills workshops, and shadowing.

name: DUCOM Women in Lifting
description:
Drexel University College of Medicine Women in Lifting is an organization to help create a safe and welcoming environment in the Drexel gym for women who are interested in lifting. In today’s society, the gym is typically more male-dominated in the weights section, leaving women reluctant to traverse the area. We hope to empower women and instill confidence in the gym, and give women the proper and strong foundation in starting their fitness journey. Even though Women in Lifting is catered towards women, we are open to anyone who is interested in learning how to lift and begin their fitness journey.

This organization is open to medical students only.
summary: DUCOM Women in Lifting is an organization to help create a safe and welcoming environment in the Drexel gym for women who are interested in lifting.

name: Drexel Running Group (West Reading)
description: DRG is for those who love running, want to be better at running, or just purely want to look cool and run. The DRG strives to foster camaraderie and inclusion through running in the great outdoors of Reading, Pennsylvania. There are many running friendly areas to explore, such as Gring's Mill, Wyomissing Park, and Blue Marsh. Whether new to running or an ex-collegiate runner, all are welcome to participate. DRG provides a chance to spend time with friends while taking care of our physical and mental wellness through running. Along with meeting weekly for casual runs, we participate in local races and hold club meals. Come run and eat a bagel with us!
summary: DRG is for those who love running or for those that want to start.

name: Impulse
description: Drexel Impulse was formed as a way for students to learn and practice different dance styles to both de-stress and foster a dance community at DUCOM. Impulse aims to hold workshops for students interested in learning different dance styles. We have regular dance practices on campus to exercise and take much needed study breaks! Members have the opportunity to choreograph and lead classes. All styles of dance are welcome. Impulse members also have the opportunity to perform various pieces together at different events throughout the school year, such as PABC, the classical concert or the multicultural show.
summary: Drexel Impulse was formed as a way for students to learn and practice different dance styles to de-stress and foster a dance community at DUCOM.

name: DUCOM Christian Disciples
description: DUCOM Christian Disciples is an interdenominational Christian club based on the West Reading campus that meets on a weekly basis (usually over a free meal) to study scripture, grow in friendship with one another, and support each other in living as Christian medical students and future physicians! We are open to people of all backgrounds and Christian faiths or anyone who is interested in exploring the Christian faith for the first time. In addition to our weekly meetings, we are looking to offer fun social events (bonfires, concerts, service, etc.) to grow our community in relationship with one another and dive deeper into our Christian faith. We have a network of Christian physicians, medical professionals, and religious leaders in the area that come to speak to our group regularly, offer opportunities for service, and invite us to local events. We hope you will join us this semester to grow deeper in your life as a Christian disciple!
summary: An interdenominational Christian club based on the West Reading campus that meets on a weekly basis to study scripture, grow in friendship with one another, and support each other in living as Christian medical students and future physicians!

name: Addiction Medicine Interest Geared Organization for Students
description: Addiction affects approximately 20 million people. Substance use disorders (SUDs) are commonly encountered in all specialties of medicine and are instrumental in the development of chronic diseases. It is vital to provide preventative care and early intervention to reduce the risk of addiction and substance-related health conditions. Medical students do not receive enough exposure in medical education about addictive diseases and in trainings for addiction as part of the regular curriculum. The goal of our interest group is to provide our peers the opportunity to increase their knowledge, awareness, and skills to assess for SUDs, provide early intervention and make appropriate referrals for treatment of the disease.
summary: Addiction Medicine Interest Geared Organization for Students

name: Surgery Interest Group
description: Our mission is to help students further their interest and knowledge in the field of surgery. Throughout the year we will host a variety of surgeons representing different subspecialties to talk about their careers, patient populations, procedures, and lifestyle. We also will be hosting other fun events like a suture clinic to teach students the critical surgical skill of placing and tying sutures!
summary: Surgery Interest Group

name: ENT Interest Group
description: The DUCOM ENT Interest Group serves to assist medical students in exploring Otorhinolaryngology. An ENT surgeon provides comprehensive medical and surgical care for patients of all ages with diseases and disorders that affect the ears, nose, throat, the respiratory and upper alimentary systems, and related structures of the head and neck. The specialty includes cosmetic facial reconstruction, head and neck cancer, and the diagnosis and management of allergic, sinus, laryngeal, and otologic pathologies. Our group hopes to expose students to this vast and dynamic field and provide opportunities to better understand it.
summary: ENT Interest Group

name: Ophthalmology Interest Group
description:
The DUCOM Ophthalmology Interest Group is a student-run organization committed to exploring the field of ophthalmology and creating opportunities for learning, engagement, and discussion. Group leaders will plan and host talks from professionals in the field, highlight opportunities for engagement in the community, and provide resources for education. Through these events and others, we aim to connect interested students, create a platform for discussion, and facilitate the interaction between ophthalmologists and students for research, shadowing, and mentorship.

Link to our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/100764073/QILIloLB
summary: Ophthalmology Interest Group

name: Pathology Interest Group
description: The Pathology Interest Group provides exposure to the medical specialty of pathology beyond what students learn in the classroom. We offer resources to shadow pathologists and also sponsor meetings where different aspects of the field are discussed. We also go over what it's like to be a pathology resident and attending.
summary: Pathology Interest Group

name: Arts Club
description:
Philadelphia is a city that prides itself in the arts, especially for its murals. Seeking out art experiences is difficult for DUCOM students with our busy schedules, and so the arts club aims to bridge art and DUCOM. The arts club offers the students on the Philadelphia campus a way to connect with the city through art, whether that be through art museums, spaces, or activities. Some other events the club is hoping to host include a lecture on the intersection between art and medicine and community service events that involve art. We also promote creating art through various mediums with our events. With these experiences, the club hopes to bridge the gap between DUCOM students and the arts.

 

Join our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/110211715/qRwRnflP
summary: DUCOM Arts Club

name: Mothers and Baby Dragons
description:
The goal of Drexel University College of Medicine's Mothers & Baby Dragons (MBD) is to promote healthy pregnancies through one-on-one partnerships between low-income pregnant women and medical student companions who can help facilitate a healthy prenatal journey. Medical students provide support to expecting mothers by accompanying them to prenatal appointments at the Women's Care Center in Philadelphia, and regularly communicating with the mothers to discuss their unique challenges and successes throughout the pregnancy and during the baby's first few months.
We believe that every woman deserves easily accessible resources to deal with the realities and challenges of pregnancy, and extra guidance and a listening ear can give these women the help they need to start a happy and healthy family. Our goal for medical students involved in this effort is to develop empathy, skills for effective communication with patients, and the drive to go above and beyond for patient care. We hope to develop champions for comprehensive and high quality care for underserved patients through advocacy, good listening, effective teaching, and providing inspiration and hope for patients to achieve their goals for health.
summary: Mothers and Baby Dragons

name: Internal Medicine Interest Group
description: The Internal Medicine Interest Group is interested in expanding our knowledge as medical students on primary care and the sub-specialities within the umbrella of Internal Medicine. To that end, we have speaker presentations hosting physicians from various sub-specialties to highlight interesting clinical cases, talk about their roads to specialty and what brought them into the field. In the past, the group has also hosted patient advocacy groups (Amyloidosis) to raise awareness and talk about medicine from a patient’s perspective.
summary: The Internal Medicine Interest Group is interested in expanding our knowledge as medical students on primary care and the sub-specialities within the umbrella of Internal Medicine.

name: DUCOM Ultrasound Club
description:
The DUCOM Ultrasound Club strives to teach, share, and build a community of medical students interested in ultrasound. We aim to inspire students to share their ultrasound knowledge beyond the classroom and foster a lifelong appreciation. We are aiming to establish equal opportunities in both the Philadelphia and West Reading campuses.

Please look into our Notion site (https://drexelultrasound.notion.site) for the latest event updates and details, US resources, or ways to get more involved. If you want to contribute to our database on Notion, please email Justin (jay54@drexel.edu) to get edit access. Thanks! 

 

📚 Education

To build a database of free online ultrasound sources for any DUCOM student with a structured guide for learning

📔 Workshops

To host workshops between US-trained physicians and DUCOM students to learn and appreciate hands-on ultrasound skills applicable to daily practice in triage or diagnostic confirmation

🧠 Anatomy

To collaborate with faculty to promote US in anatomical education, hands-on learning, and greater abstraction in anatomical reasoning

🧪 Research

To foster discussion of current ultrasound research such that DUCOM students are prepared to enter any field with a basic evidence-based understanding of clinical ultrasound
summary: Ultrasound Club

name: American Medical Association
description: The AMA has a robust House of Delegates consisting of representation from every state and medical society, a solid base of physician members, a thriving advocacy influence, the most revered journals and resources in medicine, and respected practice tools. The Drexel University AMA chapter aims to spread knowledge, promote collaborative learning and build networks between medical students and experts in the field.
summary: American Medical Association

name: South Asian Medical Students Association
description: SAMSA is an organization with the goal of spreading awareness of and sharing South Asian culture with the students of DUCOM community. We are a student-led group that represents the countries of Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Maldives, India, Pakistan, and Sri Lanka. Our mission is to create an environment for both faculty and students of South Asian descent, and those interested in South Asian culture to celebrate diversity and encounter others of similar interests and backgrounds. It is also our hope for medical students and faculty to learn about the traditions, food, and culture of the region, and participate in ethnic events that allow exploration and experience of the rich culture of South Asia. Through our various traditional events, discussions, talks/presentations, and performances, we hope to foster cultural understanding and diversity.
summary: South Asian Medical Students Association

name: DUCOM Volleyball Club
description: DUCOM Volleyball is a student organization that offers a fun and inclusive space for students to play volleyball. We welcome all skill levels to join and participate in pick-up style games and tournaments to stay active, unwind, and build relationships.
summary: DUCOM Volleyball is a student organization that offers a fun and inclusive space for students to play volleyball. We welcome all skill levels to join and participate in pick-up style games and tournaments to stay active, unwind, and build relationships.

name: Diagnostic Radiology Interest Group
description: Our purpose is to share information about Radiology the wider campus. We hope to provide sessions explaining how to build a radiology application, what life is like in residence, as an attending, and offer special topic sessions on imaging modalities, women in radiology, and radiology sub-specialties.
summary: Diagnostic Radiology Interest Group

name: Pediatrics Interest Group
description: The Pediatric Interest Group is an organization within DUCOM intended to help students learn more about the field of Pediatrics and the care of children. Medical students considering Pediatrics as a specialty, interested in the role of a pediatrician, and looking to learn about pediatric care are encouraged to participate in activities sponsored by the Pediatric Interest Group. Along with furthering your education, there are numerous opportunities to serve children and families in our neighborhoods through community-oriented projects. We are looking forward to providing students with opportunities to shadow, seek mentorship, and participate in panel discussions to engage with physicians involved in the field of pediatrics!
summary: Pediatrics Interest Group

name: Interventional Radiology Interest Group
description: DUCOM Interventional Radiology Interest Group is a student organization, which facilitates exploration of the field of Interventional Radiology for medical students, especially during the preclinical phases of education. The focus of the radiology interest group is to provide opportunities and information for students who are curious about the field of interventional radiology and to connect those students with shadowing opportunities. The group leadership will plan and host educational events throughout the school year, such as clinical skills simulation workshops, specialty-specific discussions and seminars, and networking mixers. In addition to these offerings, the radiology interest group will foster vertical collegiality by connecting first- and second-year students with students who are further along on the radiology pathway.
summary: DUCOM Interventional Radiology Interest Group is a student organization, which facilitates exploration of the field of Interventional Radiology for medical students, especially during the preclinical phases of education.

name: DUCOM Soccer
description:
***Medical students only***

The purpose of DUCOM's soccer club is to help connect medical students through our love for soccer, while also getting exercise throughout our medical school experience. There will be a fall league from September to November where we play once a week and try to provide anyone with an interest in the sport a place to safely enjoy the game with no judgement. We aim for everyone to have fun and enjoy themselves while they get a break from the rigor of medical school. The fall league is an organized, competitive league with other medical schools in the Philadelphia and South New Jersey area. Additional opportunities to play outside of this league will be organized in the form of weekly or bi-weekly pick-up games. These games will have no referees and will be casual in style.
summary: DUCOM Soccer

name: DUCOM Running Club (Philly)
description: Our running group serves to establish a community of runners, of all levels, at the College of Medicine. We will hold weekly runs of three-five miles in length and also participate in races around Philadelphia. Our goal is to provide a casual, non-intimidating environment for all types of runners in order to get students familiar with and excited about running around Philadelphia.
summary: DUCOM Running Club

name: DUCOM Friends of Frankie's World
description:
Frankie’s World is a non-profit daycare center for young children with special medical needs here in Philly. Inspired by the owner’s daughter, Frankie’s World blends healthcare and preschool education in a unique environment that allows all kids to meet their full potential both physically and socially. More information on their mission can be found at https://www.frankiesworld.com/
As extra helping hands, volunteers will be engaging in lessons, crafts, and other activities with preschool-aged kids as well as providing support for the care of children with a unique range of needs. Volunteers may also help with playtime, meals, etc. for both groups (0-2 and 2+). Being a medical daycare, there are ample opportunities to observe clinical procedures in a pediatric setting as well.
summary: DUCOM Friends of Frankie's World

name: Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation Interest Group
description: Our group aims to help medical students learn more about the specialty of PM&R, also known as physiatry. We hold meetings, conference information sessions, and educational workshops to deepen our understanding of the field. Guest speakers such as current PM&R residents and attending physicians will also provide students with an overview of their experiences and will offer insight regarding how to match into the specialty. Additionally, we plan to promote professional relationships between medical students and practicing physiatrists through shadowing, clinical rotations, and national physical medicine & rehabilitation conferences.
summary:
Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation Interest Group

https://forms.gle/o6XULdVY4Dwn9z8f7

name: Emergency Medicine Interest Group
description: The Emergency Medicine Interest Group aims to provide opportunities for medical students to explore their interest in the specialty through the organization of speaking events, panel discussions, skills workshops, and shadowing. It is our goal to act as a resource for students who are thinking about entering the field of emergency medicine by providing them with options to connect with current EM physicians, explore the various disciplines encompassed by EM, and practice skills associated with the specialty.
summary: Emergency Medicine Interest Group

name: Story Fellowship
description:
Story Fellowship is a student organization that creates a welcoming space for students of all backgrounds to explore faith, community, and purpose. While we’re connected to Story College Church, our doors are open to anyone—whether you’re curious about Christianity, wrestling with life’s big questions, or simply looking for a close-knit community.

Our mission is to build a community on campus that helps students grow in character, community, and faith. We do this through regular hangouts, small-group discussions, community service, and fun events like game nights and trips.

No matter your beliefs or background, you’ll find Story Fellowship to be a supportive and inclusive space to ask questions, explore faith, and build meaningful connections that last beyond college.
summary: Story Fellowship is a welcoming student group at Drexel where all backgrounds can explore faith, community, and purpose through hangouts, small groups, service, and fun events—a welcoming space to ask questions, build friendships, and grow together.

name: DUCOM Military Medicine
description: We work to assist HPSP students in all branches navigate the complicated world of military medicine. However, it is not required for you to be a military scholarship student to join this group! We will have events to introduce all interested students to military medicine including an annual military residency panel, practicing branch-specific fitness tests, organizing an M3/M4 military student panel, and hosting military physician speakers.
summary: Military Medicine

name: DUCOM Classical
description: DUCOM Classical is a student-led organization that opens the world of classical music to all medical students, faculty, staff, and alumni at DUCOM. Events include free or discounted concerts and the annual winter and spring recitals. We work closely with the Alumni Association and the Office of Student Affairs to organize our recitals, as they are a wonderful opportunity to bring the DUCOM community together. The recitals endeavor to provide a platform for creative expression for students/faculty/staff/alumni.
summary: DUCOM Classical

name: Beating Hearts
description: The mission of DUCOM Beating Hearts is to educate middle school students regarding the basic anatomy and physiology of the cardiovascular system, to teach them the importance of maintaining heart health, and to equip them with the basic knowledge and skills to perform Hands-Only CPR and AED use. DUCOM Beating Hearts will teach these skills to middle-school students through a series of 3 heart-health education sessions. The first two sessions will take place on the middle school campuses, while the final session will take place at DUCOM and include a tour of the SIM center and anatomy labs. The success of this program relies upon dual partnership between the administrators, teachers and students of nearby middle schools and DUCOM medical students. This program will partner with nearby middle schools and be offered to students in the 7th or 8th grade. DUCOM Beating Hearts will open doors for medical students to teach important CPR skills to local students and generate true community engagement in the process.
summary: Beating Hearts

name: DUCOM Stories in Medicine
description:
The original mission of our club was inspired by Narrative Medicine, which aims to “address the relational and psychological dimensions that occur in tandem with physical illness, with an attempt to deal with the individual stories of patients.” We meet as a group to discuss books, movies, poems, and other media sources of patient stories to better understand the experiences of both the patient and the physician. We hope that by examining these perspectives, we are able to better relate to our future patients and colleagues from an informed and empathetic position.

Primarily, this is a book club to discuss stories written by physicians sharing personal experiences and patient interactions such as When Breath Becomes Air by Paul Kalanithi, Being Mortal by Atul Gawande, The Beauty in Breaking by Michele Harper and An Elegant Defense by Matt Richtel.
summary: DUCOM Stories in Medicine

name: Orthopaedic Surgery Interest Group
description: The Orthopaedic Surgery Interest Group aims to help Drexel medical students explore the field of orthopaedic surgery through guest speakers, clinical skills workshops, and shadowing opportunities.
summary: Orthopaedic Surgery Interest Group

name: DUCOM Golf Club
description: The purpose of the DUCOM Golf Club is to provide opportunities to DUCOM students at all skill levels, with the goal of promoting student wellness, fostering sportsmanship, and developing peer relationships. WE will accomplish these goals through events such as going to the driving range, mini golf, and 9 and 18 hole rounds. More experienced golfers in the club are able to provide pointers. Golf is a sport of life-long learning, and this club will provide students with the opportunity to pursue a new hobby or connect with other students who enjoy the game of golf.
summary: DUCOM Golf Club

name: DUCOM Psychiatry Interest Group
description: The Psychiatry Interest Group organizes events and opportunities for students to explore the field of psychiatry. We invite guest speakers from Drexel and outside institutions to talk about clinical techniques and the realities of clinical practice. We also host events in a Q&A format to allow medical students to ask residents anything they are curious about the specialty. We also have relationships with various faculty members to allow students to take advantage of shadowing opportunities as a way to gain experience in their perspective career. Lastly, we host various events for those who are interested in pursuing a career in psychiatry.
summary: DUCOM Psychiatry Interest Group

name: DUCOM American Medical Women's Association
description: The American Medical Women’s Association (AMWA) strives to advance women in healthcare by advocating for equality in the workforce and empowering women to not only be contributors to the field but also leaders. Our chapter focuses on enhancing the network of future women physicians by hosting panels and mixers with physicians and creating mentorship opportunities. We also value philanthropy by organizing fundraisers for local women’s rights organizations, including Planned Parenthood. With every event, our ultimate mission is to bring awareness to the disparities of women physicians in medicine and provide them the tools to change this narrative.
summary: American Medical Women's Association

name: Primary Care Progress at Drexel
description: Primary Care Progress (PCP) is a national non-profit that is dedicated to bringing all who are interested in the future of primary care together to promote and transform primary care as we know it. We seek to draw on the experience and knowledge of healthcare professionals to educate students in order to provide them with the change-making tools they need to both improve primary care delivery and revitalize the primary care community. Additionally, we at Primary Care Progress wish to work alongside other groups and serve as advocates for patients affected by issues such as substance abuse, current healthcare reform, and misconceptions about healthcare.
summary: Primary Care Progress at Drexel

name: Gastroenterology Interest Group
description: The Gastroenterology Interest Group is dedicated to introducing students to the exciting aspects of the field of gastroenterology. We are here to expose students to the broad specialty that is GI and connect them with unique opportunities and hear from experts in the field.
summary: Gastroenterology Interest Group

name: MUsculoskeletal Skills and CLinical Education (MUSCLE) Club
description: The DUCOM MUSCLE Club aims to give medical students the opportunity to learn about and gain hands-on experience with musculoskeletal clinical skills with an emphasis on those involved in rehabilitation and sports medicine.
summary: MUsculoskeletal Skills and CLinical Education (MUSCLE) Club

name: Clinical Skills Interest Group
description: The Clinical Skills Interest Group’s purpose is to expose students to useful clinical skills and information and provide hands on experience before starting our clinical rotations. Some of our events include Suturing Clinic, First Aid/CPR Training, Phlebotomy Workshop, Intubation Workshop, and Ultrasound Workshop.
summary: Clinical Skills Interest Group

name: Nutrition in Medicine Club
description: Nutrition is critical in the prevention and management of various chronic diseases. However, nutrition education is not a focus of the medical school curriculum. Our goal as a club is to hold nutrition-related events and guest speakers to better prepare us to integrate the fields of nutrition and medicine. In doing so, we will be better prepared to serve our patients through not only medical care, but better health promotion.
summary: Nutrition in Medicine Club

name: Cardiology Interest Group
description:
The Drexel University College of Medicine Cardiology Interest Group aims to spark medical students’ interest in the field of cardiology, and to foster an academic community at Drexel. We hope to provide students with opportunities to explore every aspect of the field, from general cardiology to cardiothoracic surgery.
 
Networking events will be held for members to form relationships and pursue research opportunities with cardiologists and other medical students in the Philadelphia area. Shadowing will also be available for students to explore many subspecialties within the field. Clinical skill seminars and lessons will be offered throughout the academic year, with doctors and professors leading EKG workshops, cath labs, and more.
 
We welcome all medical students to join our group, regardless of their current level of interest or knowledge in cardiology. Our inclusive community values every member's unique journey and goals.
 
If you are passionate about cardiology or just curious to learn more, we invite you to get in touch and attend our next event!
summary: Cardiology Interest Group

name: Dermatology Interest Group
description: The Dermatology Interest Group (DIG) at Drexel University College of Medicine (DUCOM) aims to educate students at DUCOM about the field of dermatology. Our organization seeks to provide opportunities for dermatology-related shadowing, research, and volunteering, as well as opportunities for community engagement such as Melanoma Awareness Month in May and the National Psoriasis Foundation Walk in September. Furthermore, students in the DIG are encouraged to connect with DUCOM alumni and residents in dermatology during networking events and panels that will be hosted by the DIG.
summary: Dermatology Interest Group

name: LabakCare Student Organization
description: The purpose of the LabakCare Student Organization is to improve the health of underserved communities by providing no cost preventive health care services in Philadelphia and local surrounding areas, in addition to providing international clinics in Haiti and Nigeria. LabakCare offers health screenings, flu shots, and referrals to uninsured individuals through partnerships with local nonprofits, shelters, and religious institutions. This organization is a unique opportunity to work with fellow medical students in organizing fundraising efforts for this non-profit organization while also having the opportunity to work together with physicians, nurses, pharmacists, and other health care workers and students in order to screen patients and help them understand their diagnosed health issues. Students who are passionate about sharing in LabakCare’s mission of serving medically underserved communities, working to decrease the incidence of preventable chronic diseases, working with interdisciplinary aspects of healthcare, and organizing efforts to expand the reach of LabakCare’s service to underserved communities are encouraged to join.
summary: LabakCare Student Organization

name: DUCOM Christian Fellowship
description: DUCOM Christian Fellowship (DCF) aims to foster a welcoming Christian community on the Philly campus for current believers as well as those interested in Christianity. We understand that beginning medical school is often a significant event in peoples’ lives, and encourage the exploration of the intersection between faith and medicine. DCF holds weekly facilitated Bible studies and Daily Devotionals for anyone interested in attending. We also have guest speakers and social events, approx. 1-2 per semester.
summary: DUCOM Christian Fellowship

name: DUCOM Climbing Club
description: The DUCOM Climbing Club seeks to bring new and old climbers together for the purpose of building a climbing community and educating and exposing new climbers to the sport.
summary: DUCOM Climbing Club

name: CofTea
description: DUCOM CofTea club is a student organization that aims to provide a space for students to bond over their mutual love for caffeinated beverages, like coffee and tea. We seek to provide services to the student body in the form of campus-wide events, fundraisers, and informative sessions about these drinks in various contexts, including medicine. We, at CofTea, believe that coffee/tea in all of its various forms deserves to be celebrated so we would love to highlight the different cultural profiles of these drinks. We are self-proclaimed beverage connoisseurs, because who knows coffee and tea better than sleep-deprived medical students? Whether you like your drinks hot, iced, or even at room temperature, we hope that you enjoy your time with us!
summary: CofTea

name: DUCOM Tennis
description: DUCOM Club Tennis aims to provide a community for students who enjoy playing tennis or are interested in learning how to play. We welcome players of all skill levels and provide racquets if you need one. We host practices that consist of games, drills, and lessons for beginners.
summary: DUCOM Tennis

name: Hiking Club
description: Hiking Club aims to promote mental wellness, stress reduction, and provide experiences in nature while building community among DUCOM students. Hiking is a great way to take a break from studying and clear your mind. Come and join us!
summary: DUCOM Hiking Club

name: DUCOM Doctor's Note
summary: Doctor’s Note is Drexel University College of Medicine’s a cappella group made entirely of medical students, formed to promote musical abilities and passions for students as they navigate medical school!

name: Latino Medical Student Association
description: The LMSA chapter here at DUCOM exists to serve as a safe haven for present medical students and an outlet for community engagement in the Latinx population. We aim to serve the diverse patient population that the great state of Pennsylvania has to offer, while simultaneously allowing our members to gain exposure to the vulnerable populations they will be serving as clinicians. Through mentorship, service, and education we aim to empower our members to be the force that corrects the health inequities that presently exist in medicine to date.
summary: Latino Medical Student Organization Chapter at Drexel University College of Medicine aims to provide Latinx/e/a community engagement for medical students.

name: Plastic Surgery Interest Group
description:
The DUCOM Plastic Surgery Interest Group (PSIG) is an organization for medical students who are interested in learning more about plastic surgery as a specialty. Our group seeks to facilitate opportunities for medical students to learn about the field of plastic surgery through physician talks, skills workshops, journal clubs, shadowing, and research. PSIG aims to facilitate connection between DUCOM students and plastic surgeons or trainees for the purpose of increasing exposure to what a career in plastic surgery entails.

If plastic surgery excites you, please join us in exploring more of what this dynamic specialty has to offer!
summary: Plastic Surgery Interest Group

name: Wilderness Medicine Interest Group
description:
The Wilderness Medicine Interest Group exists to provide opportunities to DUCOM students to explore the field of wilderness medicine. We aim to provide learning experiences regarding the various subsets of wilderness medicine, hands-on experience in first aid, how time outside can impact health, and overall an appreciation for the practice of medicine in a resource limited environment.

Wilderness medicine is a growing field with clinical rotations and fellowship opportunities now available. With more and more people venturing into the outdoors it is important for physicians to be trained in this unique environment and for students to be able to explore their interest.
summary: Wilderness Medicine Interest Group

name: Drexel University's Club for Students with Visible and Invisible Disabilities)
description: The Drexel University's Club for Students with Invisible and Visible Disabilities provides the Drexel community with a safe space for all students who identify as having a disability and allies to discuss pertinent ADA legislation, accommodations, advocacy and more. This organization gives students, faculty, and staff a platform to educate the the Drexel University community on disability rights and highlight the disability experience. Finally, the organization host fun social and volunteering events to engage the greater Philadelphia community.
summary: Drexel University's Club for Students with Invisible and Visible Disabilities (DUCSVID) will provide the Drexel community a safe space for all students who identify as having a disability and allies.

name: Drexel Fiber Arts Club
description: Fiber Arts Club is an organization for Drexel students to destress and relax while learning a new skill whether its knitting, crocheting, or both. We help students channel their stress and anxiety into a productive hobby that brings joy to themselves and others.
summary: Fiber Arts Club is an organization welcoming all students interested in learning how to crochet or knit or those with all levels of experience. We want to provide a safe space for students to grow and de-stress from their academic and personal lives.

name: Multimedia Audio for Westphal
summary: Our mission is to facilitate community and collaborations between Westphal students with interests or projects relating to audio for games and extended media.

name: Groove Phi Groove
description: Founded on October 12th, 1962 during the civil rights era by fourteen young Black men who wanted an afro-centric alternative to the fraternities on their campus. The Phi in our name is its original kemetic version stemming from the ancient indigenous language of Egypt. This chapter hopes to further their ideals by giving back to communities both on and off campus. Having 62,357 members worldwide the connection made through joining Groove can further not only your social life but also your professional future post graduation.
summary: Groove Phi Groove

name: Office of Community Engagement
description:
The mission of the Office of Community Engagement (OCE) is to integrate meaningful community service and reflective learning to prepare medical students to address socioeconomic determinants of disease and become community-responsive physicians.

Curricular

Health Advocacy Practicum

Health Advocacy 2

Fourth-Year Elective: Providing Health Care for Vulnerable Populations

 

Non-Curricular

Bridging the Gaps Community Health Internship Program

Health Outreach Project

Medical Humanities Program
summary:
The Office of Community Engagement supports volunteer, curricular, and co-curricular experiences centered around meaningful community service and reflective learning for CoM students.

HAP, HA2, BTG & HOP

name: Dragon Bhangra Crew
summary: Dragon Bhangra Crew is an all-inclusive Bhangra Dance Team at DUCOM! We hold workshops throughout each semester, focusing on teaching choreography pieces created by the DBC board. Whether you have no experience or a lot, we encourage everyone to come out

name: DUCOM PHL Table Tennis
description: DUCOM Table Tennis is a club for everyone at all levels of playing. We want to provide a space for students who are interested in playing or who want to learn how to play table tennis. We plan to hold practices where students can learn from one another and play against each other. On some occasions we also hope to host tournaments for students to compete and win prizes.
summary: DUCOM Table Tennis is a club for everyone at all levels of playing. We want to provide a space for students who are interested in playing or who want to learn how to play table tennis.

name: DUCOM French Club
description: French Club is a community for French learners and French-speaking individuals at DUCOM to come together for conversation and cultural appreciation. Activities include informal meetings for conversational practice; book clubs; culinary events; sharing recommendations for French learners such as educational resources, TV shows and movies, and music; and learning together about the histories and traditions of various French-speaking cultures across the world.
summary: French Club is a community for French learners and French-speaking individuals at DUCOM to come together for conversation and cultural appreciation.

name: Pain Medicine Club
description: Our mission is to bring more awareness to pain medicine and to reduce the stigma associated with pain. This club consists of the different pain medicine branches (Anesthesiology, Psychiatry, Orthopedics, Neurology, PM&R) collaborating to educate students and the public of the ins and outs of the specialty. You will also learn from Addiction Medicine & Psychiatry how pain management specialists can discern between people who are experiencing pain and those who are simply seeking opioids because of their substance use disorder. As for the patients who have had substance use disorders in the past who are experiencing pain, you will learn and understand how to best manage their pain and prevent relapse. Through this club you will also discover the different alternatives to opioids. In addition, we will partner up with different clinicians and organizations to provide hands-on and shadowing opportunities as well as to be speakers in the events we will host. We will also provide various opportunities to network with others in pain medicine outside of DUCOM.
summary: Our mission is to bring more awareness to pain medicine and to reduce the stigma associated with pain. This club consists of the different pain med branches (Anesth,Psych,Neuro,PM&R) collaborating to educate students on the ins and outs of the specialty.

name: Reading Latinx Club
description: REAL is a student organization focused on embracing and celebrating the Latino/a community. Our club focuses on cultural education, medical Spanish workshops, community involvement, and teaching and practicing common Spanish phrases that help establish trust among Patients and Physicians.
summary: REAL is a student organization focused on embracing and celebrating the Latinx community.

name: DUCOM Pickleball Club
summary: DUCOM Pickleball Club is an organization for everyone from die-hard picklers to those who are new to the game and want to learn more. All skill levels are welcome!

name: Drexel Coalition for Disability Advocacy
description: DCDA is an organization dedicated to generating a culture of disability inclusion and justice at DUCOM that promotes the improvement of disability education in medical training and practice, works towards dismantling ableism in healthcare and beyond, and provides a support network and advocacy platform for students and faculty with disabilities. We aim to be a central hub for disability-related initiatives, working in conjunction with DUCOM offices, student groups, and community programs to support our common goals.
summary: DCDA seeks to advance disability rights and health equity through education and advocacy, and improve accessibility and support for medical students with disabilities.

name: WR Golf Club
description: The purpose of the WR Golf Club is to provide golf opportunities to DUCOM students at all skill levels, with the goals of promoting student wellness, fostering sportsmanship, and developing peer relationships. We will accomplish these goals through events such as going to the driving range, putt putt, and 9/18 hole rounds. More experienced golfers are able to provide pointers. Golf is a sport of life-long learning, paralleling a career in medicine, and this club will provide students with the opportunity to pursue a new hobby or connect students who enjoy the game of golf.
summary: WR Golf Club strives to provide golf opportunities to DUCOM students at all skill levels, with the goals of promoting student wellness, fostering sportsmanship, and developing peer relationships.

name: DUCOM Student Interest Group in Neurology
description:
The Student Interest Group in Neurology is a chapter of the American Academy of Neurology, whose purpose is to increase knowledge and foster interest in Neurology by providing opportunities to participate in clinical, research, and service activities in Neurology.
 
As the DUCOM Specialty Interest Group in Neurology (SIGN) we aim to expand students’ clinical skills training in neurology through practice sessions during the Fall 2023 semester. 
 
Second, we aim to facilitate student involvement in neurology-related clinical, translational, and basic research to better prepare students for their Scholar’s program applications, their MSSRF applications, and their overall summer goals following the completion of their M1 year.
 
This all fits with our mission of expanding upon the overarching goal of SIGN, explaining the possibilities available and helping those interesting in the field gain knowledge, experience/skills and connections that can help further ones passions, career and the field as a whole.
summary: The Student Interest Group in Neurology is a chapter of the American Academy of Neurology, whose purpose is to increase knowledge and foster interest in Neurology by providing opportunities to participate in clinical, research, and service activities.

name: Global Health Interest Group
description: The mission of the Global Health Interest Group at Drexel University College of Medicine is to provide educational, networking, and practical opportunities for students to explore ways in which global and public health work can be integrated into careers in medicine. The group aims to hold panel discussions and workshops, as well as interactive and didactic sessions featuring experts in the field, to help interested students learn more about global and public health disciplines.
summary: The Global Health Interest Group at the Drexel University College of Medicine aims to provide educational, networking, and practical opportunities for students to explore the disciplines of global and public health.

name: Archery Club
description: Join our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/97323443/5eoqmuAL
summary: A fun and safe space to learn and practice Archery. No prior experience required! Come shoot some arrows with your peers. Be like Legolas, Katniss, or Robin Hood in no time!

name: Middle Eastern/North African in Medicine
description: MENA in Medicine serves as an organization, to spread awareness about cultures and events in the Middle East North African Region. We hope to will a sense of community among students who identify with MENA and fundraise for different causes while also providing health education opportunities for MENA folks in the greater Philadelphia area.
summary:
As an organization, we hope to fundraise and bring awareness to the MENA region.
This will include providing mentorship for undergraduate interested in medicine. In addition, we will encourage a sense of community among students who identify with MENA.

name: DUCOM Student Government Association
summary: SGA serves as the official representative body for the medical student community. The SGA's primary role is to advocate for student interests, facilitate communication between students and the administration, and enhance the overall student experience.

name: DUCOM Physick House Society
summary: Student organization consisting of Drexel Medical students within the same learning society.

name: DUCOM Rocky Statue Society
summary: Rocky Statue Society is one of the seven learning societies to which students belong. It aims to provide opportunities for socialization, wellness, community service, and student support.

name: DUCOM Athenaeum Society
summary: Learning Society, part of the Student Government, hosts events to bring together students of the Athenaeum Society

name: DUCOM Liberty Bell Society
summary: Student government organization for drexel medical students in the same learning society

name: DUCOM Pagoda Society
summary: DUCOM's Pagoda society

name: DUCOM Reading Terminal Society
summary: Learning society dedicated to improving student lives through events, accommodating communication between students and staff, and overall efforts in improving student wellbeing

name: DUCOM Eakins House Society
description: DUCOM Eakins House Society is a medical student learning community that fosters peer connections through wellness initiatives and social events, helping students build relationships and maintain a healthy work-life balance.
summary: DUCOM Eakins House Society is a medical student learning community that fosters peer connections through wellness initiatives and social events, helping students build relationships and maintain a healthy work-life balance.

name: DUCOM Clinical Site - Allegheny Health Network
summary: DUCOM Clinical Site - Allegheny Health Network

summary: DUCOM Clinical Site - Crozer-Chester

summary: DUCOM Clinical Site - Kaiser Permanente Bay Area

summary: DUCOM Clinical Site - UPMC Pinnacle

summary: DUCOM Clinical Site - Reading Hospital

summary: DUCOM Clinical Site - Rotators
