name: Office of Community Health and Inclusive Excellence
description:
The Office of Community Health & Inclusive Excellence at Drexel University College of Medicine provides a platform for inter-professional, multidisciplinary community engagement and health equity activities and collaboration across Drexel University.

 

For more information, please contact:

Annette Gadegbeku, MD
Senior Associate Dean of Community Health & Inclusive Excellence;
Associate Professor, Department of Family, Community & Preventive Medicine
abg36@drexel.edu
summary: The Office of Community Health & Inclusive Excellence at Drexel University College of Medicine provides a platform for inter-professional, multidisciplinary community engagement and health equity activities and collaboration across Drexel University.

name: Public Health Interest Group
description: The mission of the Public Health Interest Group at Drexel University College of Medicine is to expose students to the multitude of ways in which public health intersects with medicine. We will highlight issues that are pervasive in the United States, especially in Philadelphia and Reading, PA, focusing on how social determinants of health contribute to complexities and disparities seen at the national level. We will host skills-building workshops, panel discussions with experts across the nation, and promote community engagement opportunities, with the ultimate goal of preparing students to provide holistic and humanistic care to future patients.
summary: The mission of the Public Health Interest Group at Drexel University College of Medicine is to expose students to the multitude of ways in which public health intersects with medicine.

name: DUCOM WR CofTea
description: DUCOM at WR CofTea club is a student organization that aims to provide a space for students to bond over their mutual love for caffeinated beverages, like coffee and tea. We seek to provide services to the student body in the form of campus-wide events, fundraisers, and informative sessions about these drinks in various contexts, including medicine. We, at CofTea, believe that coffee/tea in all of its various forms deserves to be celebrated so we would love to highlight the different cultural profiles of these drinks. We are self-proclaimed beverage connoisseurs, because who knows coffee and tea better than sleep-deprived medical students? Whether you like your drinks hot, iced, or even at room temperature, we hope that you enjoy your time with us!
summary: DUCOM WR CofTea club is a student organization that aims to provide a space for students to bond over their mutual love for caffeinated beverages, like coffee and tea.

name: Drexel Climbing Team
description:
The Drexel Climbing Team is the premier group for climbing training for both graduate and undergraduate students attending Drexel University. Our primary goal for our students is to foster a collaborative training atmosphere to regularly develop climbing skills and strengths, while also fostering meaningful relationships with students sharing a common love for climbing. To allow students to fully experience their progression, we also attend USA Climbing Collegiate competitions locally and in the surrounding area including NJ, DE, or MD. The culmination of the season will occur in the Spring with the USA Climbing Divisional Championships, which doubles as the qualifying competition for the National Championships. The Drexel Climbing Team will make every effort to pool resources and discount entry to these competitions and to training facilities (i.e. Tufas Boulder Lounge or other gyms) through strategic partnerships and Team Dues. On-season dues are $35/quarter, while off-season dues are $25/quarter. Participation on the team also grants access to a discounted Tufas Boulder Lounge membership of $40/month, down from $60/month. Practices will be held weekly at Tufas Boulder Lounge on Monday and Wednesday (7:00pm-9:00pm). During the season, we will also make an effort to travel to additional gyms and bring outside experts in to assist in the development of students as climbing athletes.

Please note: You must email us at drexelclimbingteam@gmail.com to make us aware of your interest. Also, if you have not completed your onboarding with the Club Sports Office and Trainer, you will not have full access to the site or practices. You will receive a "Practice Eligible" role on Dragonlink once all materials have been approved. 

Dues Link: https://secure.touchnet.com/C20688_ustores/web/product_detail.jsp?PRODUCTID=4246&SINGLESTORE=true
summary: The perfect group on campus to find your own way to become more structured in pursuing climbing of any kind! Whether you are interested in becoming a serious competitor or just want a group to learn new climbing skills with, we are the group for you!

name: DUCOM West Reading Ice Skating Club
summary: DUCOM West Reading Ice Skating Club strives to unite students with a passion for ice skating. Our hope is to give students the opportunity to explore a new hobby, re-visit an old interest, or learn a new skill, all while having fun.

name: DUCOM Swim Club
description: Dive into a refreshing break from the rigors of medical school with the DUCOM Swim Club! We offer a welcoming space for students to unwind, stay active, and build a supportive community through swimming.
summary: Dive into a refreshing break from the rigors of medical school with the DUCOM Swim Club! We offer a welcoming space for students to unwind, stay active, and build a supportive community through swimming.

name: DUCOM Squash Club
description:
Club Facilities: The DUCOM Squash Club typically meets at a well-equipped and conveniently located squash facility at the Kline and Specter Squash Center (Drexel Recreation Center). This state-of-the-art facility provides members with access to multiple squash courts, locker rooms, and viewing areas, ensuring that everyone has the opportunity to practice and play in a comfortable and safe setting.

Membership: Membership in the DUCOM Squash Club is open to medical students, faculty, and staff members who share a passion for squash or a desire to learn and improve their skills. The club prides itself on being an inclusive community, welcoming players of all skill levels, from beginners to seasoned athletes.

Activities and Offerings:

  1. Regular Play and Practice: The club schedules regular practice sessions and open play times to accommodate a wide range of schedules. Members can enjoy casual games or engage in more competitive play based on their preferences.

  2. Coaching and Skill Development: The club often provides coaching sessions led by experienced players to help members enhance their squash skills. These sessions can be tailored to various levels, ensuring that everyone can benefit from personalized guidance.

  3. Intramural and Intercollegiate Tournaments: For those who enjoy competition, the club organizes intramural tournaments among its members. Additionally, we will seek to participate in intercollegiate squash tournaments, representing the medical school on a larger stage.
summary: The DUCOM Squash Club caters to squash players of all levels of experience. We organize tournaments, introductory how-to-play sessions, viewing parties for the US Squash Open, and provide equipment to play the sport.

name: WR Climbing Club
description: Are you an experienced climber? Have you never climber but wanted to try a new sport? WR Climbing club is open to all experience levels and involves trips to both indoor gyms and the great outdoors in the local area and beyond! Newcomers can learn the basics of different disciplines including bouldering and top rope climbing with sport climbing also available for experienced climbers. Climbing is a relaxing way to take a break from studies and get some exercise while being a part of a fun and supportive athletic community:)
summary: Climbing club is an indoor/outdoor recreational club open to new and experienced climbers. Different disciplines of climbing will be available to members depending on their interests and experience levels where applicable.

name: West Reading Board Game Club
description: The purpose of this club is to offer students at West Reading an outlet to meat and socialize in a non-academic manner and improve/create interpersonal bonds with peers through fun and social activities. By playing board games with peers, we hope to offer a place where students can enjoy some free time in between studying in school and help reduce stress. We also wish to include games like Dungeons and Dragons and other table top games to help offer students the opportunity to be creative and expand upon their imagination.
summary: The purpose of this group is to offer a stress reducing environment and allow peers to bond over board games, table top games, and card games.

name: DUCOM MedConnect
description: Drexel MedConnect (Formerly Drexel MTC) is committed to fostering the next generation of healthcare leaders, addressing the need for greater diversity and inclusion in the medical field, and promoting interprofessional collaboration. Our mission is to mentor underserved students in our community through engaging brief case studies of intriguing medical cases, and to kindle their passion for STEM and the medical sciences. We also aim to expand our reach to other health professions at Drexel, including Nursing, Physical Therapy, Physician Assistants and other related programs, to further their education, interprofessional collaboration, and navigating them through their interest in the Medical Field.
summary: DUCOM MedConnect focuses on providing guidance and mentorship to middle and high school students in our community interested in the medical field.

name: Frisbee and Spikeball club
description: The DUCOM Frisbee & Spikeball club’s mission is to provide an outlet and place for DUCOM students to come together and enjoy time outside playing games and having fun with friends. Frisbee and spikeball are incredibly inclusive sports and anyone can play. Anyone is welcome to join and play at any time. We hope to provide a space of recreation and fun in sports that are for everyone!
summary: DUCOM Frisbee & Spikeball is for friends to come together, relax, and throw some plastic and play spikeball together!

name: Drexel Women’s Wrestling Club
description:
FALL 2025-2026 Practice Schedule: Monday/Thursday 6:30-8pm

 

The Drexel Women's Wrestling Club is a dynamic and empowering community where female athletes come together to train, compete, and support each other in the sport of wrestling. This club provides a welcoming and inclusive environment for women of all skill levels, from beginners to experienced wrestlers, fostering a sense of camaraderie and teamwork. Members engage in various wrestling techniques, drills, and sparring sessions, to encourage growth both on and off the mat. The club not only focuses on physical strength and technique but also emphasizes mental resilience, confidence, and discipline. It serves as a platform for women to challenge themselves, build self-confidence, and celebrate the strength and skill of female athletes in the sport of wrestling.
summary: The Drexel Women’s Wrestling Club is a welcoming community for female athletes of all levels to learn and grow in the sport of wrestling.

name: DUCOM Academic Support
description: Drexel University College of Medicine Office of Academic Support
summary: Drexel University College of Medicine Office of Academic Support

name: Student Life Leadership Curriculum
description:
The purpose of this curriculum is to ensure that all students who choose to be a part of a student organization understand the purpose of their role and the campus impact that they can have. The skills learned by taking on a student leadership position is invaluable. With this curriculum, we hope that you are empowered to lead authentically, make connections, grow, and create change. ​

 

This curriculum was created to ensure that all students who choose to be a part of a student organization understand the purpose of their role and the campus impact that they can have. This curriculum is designed be an extension of Recognition, and will be a year-long process organizations will participate in. ​

 

View the "Student Life Leadership" file in the documents section below for more details about the curriculum and requirements for organizations. 

 

Click this link to track your organization's progress for 2025-2026.
summary: The Student Life Leadership Curriculum is managed by the Student Organization Resource Center. Dragon, Gold, FSL, and Student Life Impact organizations are required to participate in the Curriculum.

name: Knotty Talk
description: The mission of Knotty Talk is to educate medical students on surgical knot tying and suturing. This will be accomplished through hosting frequent hand-knot tying and suturing workshops, led by peers and experts. We want to ensure that members have enough hands-on practice, thus we will hold workshops several times a month, allowing everyone the experience to participate. Our goal is to provide a foundation for students to excel in surgical rotations and hone skills necessary for procedure-focused medical specialties.
summary: Knot tying and suturing are as much an art as they are valuable procedural skills. Join us to gain hands-on experience tying knots and suturing from peers and experts.

name: DUCOM WR Women's Barbell Club
description: The club creates a safe and welcoming environment for women to learn and lift together. We will have weekly workout sessions at the school gym where members can come for a quick lift or join in a class-like workout. In addition to strength training, we recognize the importance of flexibility and mobility for overall fitness. Sessions incorporate dedicated time for stretching and mobility exercises, allowing time to improve range of motion, prevent muscle imbalances, and enhance recovery.
summary: DUCOM WR WBC offers a supportive and motivating environment for women of all fitness levels to come together, learn, and thrive through the power of lifting. The club focuses on consistency, community, and safety to maximize mental and physical health.

name: DUCOM Students for Gun Violence Control, Awareness, Responsible Education, and Safety
description:
We hope to foster a community at DUCOM for students to learn about gun violence and its impact on the communities we serve. In 2022, Philadelphia alone had over 2000 reported victims of gun violence.

With this in mind, our goals are listed below:

  • 1) Foster skills for delivery of trauma-informed care, gun violence safety, and violence prevention
  • 2) Discuss gun violence and the impact it has on our communities and the communities we serve
  • 3) Discuss the role healthcare providers can play in addressing the gun violence epidemic
  • 4) Collaborate with community members passionate about ending the gun violence epidemic to cultivate a collaborative educational environment 

We plan to host an annual Gun Violence Safety and Prevention week, and collaborate with community members and physicians, cultivating a collaborative environment where we can all learn from each other and address the pressing epidemic of gun violence.
summary: We hope to foster skills for the delivery of trauma informed care for victims of violence, educate our students on gun violence safety, and collaborate with community to cultivate a collaborative environment.

name: Dragons Against Food Insecurity
description:
Dragons Against Food Insecurity, or DAFI, is a DUCOM student organization for those who wish to contribute in their school and community to fight food insecurity. Food insecurity affects not only underserved populations in Philadelphia area, but also members of our student population, who are often unsure of what resources they are eligible for.

 

As such, our mission is twofold: provide education for our student body on how they can access food resources, as well as serve Philadelphia and Reading communities in improving food accessibility. We achieve this through workshops where we teach our students about how they can access food assistance, as well as setting up volunteering opportunities with larger locally-based food banks such as Philabundance, Sharing Excess, and Helping Harvest for those who wish to serve their community. 

 

This is a Drexel University College of Medicine Organization. For a similar undergraduate organization, check out Sharing Excess Drexel.
summary: DAFI's main goal is to educate the DUCOM student population about food insecurity both on campus and in the community, and provide information and opportunities on how they can combat this critical issue.

name: Academic Medicine Interest Group
description: The Academic Medicine Interest Group (AMIG) aims to bring awareness to DUCOM students about what a career in academic medicine entails. This includes what academic medicine is, the different careers in academia, and the pathway to becoming an academic physician. We also hope to bring in academic physicians to talk about their careers and for networking with professionals working at academic institutions.
summary: The Academic Medicine Interest Group (AMIG) aims to bring awareness to DUCOM students about what a career in academic medicine entails. This includes what academic medicine is, the different careers in academia, and the path to academic medicine.

name: National Organization of Minority Architecture Students
description: The National Organization of Minority Architecture Students (NOMAS) at Drexel University promotes and advocates for the voices of the marginalized and disenfranchised in the architectural field. Our goal is to uplift, highlight, and empower minority architecture students through professional development, education, and community engagement. NOMAS provides a community that understands and discusses the struggles, obstacles, and hardships minority architecture students face in academia and the workforce; and educates the student body (and those interested in this topic) on the importance of allyship and advocacy. Furthermore, this organization will prioritize supporting students emerging in the design field, provide networking opportunities with other NOMAS organizations, and most importantly, speak against bigotry, intolerance, and ignorance.
summary: Drexel NOMAS promotes and advocates the voices of the marginalized and disenfranchised in architecture. The goal is to highlight and empower minority design students through professional development, education, and volunteerism regionally and nationally.

name: Civil, Architectural, & Environmental Engineering Graduate Student Association
description: The purpose of this organization is to create a student-led community that fosters department connectivity and promotes academic, professional, and personal development, as well as overall wellness. Our mission is to provide a supportive and inclusive platform for CAEE graduate students to collaborate, share knowledge, and engage in meaningful discussions. Through a diverse range of activities, workshops, and events, we aim to enhance students' academic skills, nurture their professional growth, and empower them to take charge of their personal development. By fostering a strong sense of community, we aspire to create a positive and enriching environment that encourages lifelong learning and well-being for all members.
summary: This organization is the Graduate Student Association at the Civil, Architectural, & Environmental Engineering department.

name: Drexel WEH
summary: WEH is a Christ-centered community for women on campus (or anyone who identifies with the struggles coming along with being a woman!). With every heart (WEH!), God's Kingdom grows, and we hope to be a part of transformation on our campus in Jesus' name!

name: Law in Medicine
description: As a dynamic student organization, we unite medical and law students to explore vital intersections of healthcare ethics, policy, and practice, promoting interdisciplinary learning and innovative solutions for complex healthcare challenges. We'll host monthly seminars in partnership with the Thomas R. Kline School of Law, aimed at creating a more holistic and equitable healthcare system.
summary: We unite medical and law students to explore vital intersections of healthcare ethics, policy, and practice by promoting an interdisciplinary learning and innovative solutions for complex healthcare challenges.

name: Vascular Surgery Interest Group
description:
The Vascular Surgery Interest Group (VSIG) at DUCOM is dedicated to helping our students learn more about the field of vascular surgery and its critical role in patient care. Our mission is to expose DUCOM students to vascular disease management, including both surgical and endovascular interventions, while fostering early interest in a potential career in this surgical specialty.

Through educational programming, clinical exposure, and mentorship opportunities, we aim to increase awareness of vascular surgery as a career path and enhance students’ knowledge of vascular health. Our initiatives include hosting lectures/Q&A panels with vascular surgeons, organizing skills workshops, and connecting students with shadowing and research opportunities in the field.
summary: The goal of the Vascular Surgery Interest Group is to help Drexel medical students explore a potential career in vascular surgery. We hope to increase awareness about careers in vascular surgery and improve students' vascular health clinical knowledge.

name: DUCOM Cookie Appreciation Club
description: Indulge your taste buds and join our sweet community dedicated to the love of all things cookie. Whether you're a chocolate chip enthusiast, a sugar cookie connoisseur, or a die-hard fan of oatmeal raisin, our club is the perfect place for you. Come experience the joy that a warm cookie can bring you.
summary: The goal of the Cookie Appreciation Club is to spread joy to students through the wonderful circular baked good of cookies.

name: Artificial Intelligence in Medicine
description:
The AI in Medicine Club at Drexel University College of Medicine represents a pioneering initiative aimed at integrating the rapidly evolving field of artificial intelligence with the ever-advancing domain of medicine. This club is designed to serve as a dynamic platform for students, researchers, and faculty members to explore the transformative impact of AI in healthcare.

Mission and Vision: The club's primary objective is to foster a deep understanding of how AI technologies can be leveraged to improve patient care, streamline healthcare operations, and facilitate groundbreaking medical research. It seeks to cultivate a community of forward-thinking individuals who are enthusiastic about shaping the future of medicine through technological innovation.

Key Activities:

Guest Speaker Series: The club regularly invites esteemed professionals from the fields of AI and healthcare. These guest speakers share insights on current trends, breakthroughs, and ethical considerations in AI applications in medicine, providing members with a real-world perspective.

Collaborative Research Opportunities: Members are encouraged to engage in collaborative research projects. These projects could involve developing AI algorithms for disease diagnosis, predictive analytics for patient outcomes, or exploring new AI-driven treatment methodologies. The club facilitates connections between students and faculty for mentorship and support.

Workshops and Seminars: The club organizes hands-on workshops and educational seminars. These sessions cover a range of topics, from the basics of AI and machine learning to advanced concepts in computational biology and personalized medicine. The aim is to equip members with both theoretical knowledge and practical skills.

Ethical and Policy Discussions: Recognizing the importance of ethics in AI, the club hosts discussions and debates on the ethical implications of AI in medicine. This includes topics like patient data privacy, algorithmic bias, and the impact of AI on healthcare employment.

Membership and Meetings: The club is open to all members of the Drexel community with an interest in AI and healthcare, regardless of their academic background. Regular meetings are held to discuss recent advancements, share research findings, and plan upcoming events.

In summary, the AI in Medicine Club at Drexel University College of Medicine is not just a club; it's a hub for innovation, learning, and collaboration at the intersection of AI and healthcare. It aims to inspire and empower the next generation of healthcare professionals and researchers to drive positive change in the world of medicine through the application of artificial intelligence.
summary: Explore the future of healthcare with us! Dive into the fascinating intersection of artificial intelligence and medicine through engaging guest speakers, collaborative research opportunities, and informative teaching sessions.

name: Drexel Asian Baptist Student Koinonia
description:
Asian Baptist Student Koinonia (ABSK) is a student group at Drexel committed to studying the Word of God and building Christ-centered relationships as a family of God. We strive to learn more about God through the Bible and share God’s love with all in the Drexel community.  All are welcome to join us on Friday nights for thought-provoking Bible studies, food, fun, and fellowship.

We meet for Friday Bible study and Sunday worship every week. There are many opportunities to join! Please head over to our Discord or Instagram for more details.
summary: ABSK is a student group committed to studying the Word of God and building Christ-centered relationships as a family. We strive to learn more about God through the Bible and share God’s love with all in the Drexel community.

name: Film in Color
description: Film In Color is an extension of the classroom, fostering collaboration and connecting students with peers and others who share a strong interest in film. We encourage the exploration of valuable course offerings while promoting mentorship across all areas of production. Through monthly film screenings and numerous collaborative efforts, we create a space for growth and learning. Our community-driven events aim to build a strong sense of belonging within both the Film Basement and the broader Drexel community, even spanning outside of Westphal. Ultimately, Film In Color strives to inspire, guide, and celebrate the next generation of diverse filmmakers.
summary: We provide a space where film students of underrepresented identities can get to know one another to collaborate on projects in and out of the club, along with providing networking opportunities with those currently in the industry.

name: Student Engagement, Involvement, and Leadership
description:
Student Engagement, Involvement, and Leadership seeks to enhance the campus community by creating opportunities for Dragons to engage, grow, and develop a sense of community. Our efforts are rooted in the following pillars:


Development of the whole leader

Through various programs, such as leadership workshops and panels, we strive to help you learn critical personal and professional development skills.


Build a sense of campus pride

Whether it is a small crafting session or our annual Homecoming bonfire, we seek to curate programs where students can find their place and community on campus.


Sharing Resources (Build Stability)

Through events such as the Involvement Fair, Open Houses, and weekly tabling, we aim to introduce you to all the campus offers so you can feel supported throughout your journey.
summary: Student Engagement, Involvement, and Leadership seeks to enhance the campus community by creating opportunities for Dragons to engage, grow, and develop a sense of community.

name: Drexel Sociology Club
summary: The Sociology Club's mission is to help students from all disciplines explore the field of sociology by promoting sociological thinking through civic engagement opportunities, social events, volunteering, guest speakers, group discussions, etc.

name: Drexel Turkish Students Association
description: We are the Turkish Students Association, dedicated to connecting with the Turkish community and promoting collaboration with various organizations. Our mission includes supporting Turkish student societies abroad, addressing common concerns, and assisting Turkish students transitioning to Drexel University. We provide guidance on enrollment, orientation, and registration for students from different states or countries. Additionally, we focus on promoting modern Turkish language, culture, and Co-Op opportunities in Turkiye.  Join us as we actively engage with the Turkish community and enhance the university experience for Turkish students.
summary: The Turkish Students Association connects with the Turkish community, supports Turkish students abroad, assists with Drexel University enrollment, and promotes Turkish language and culture, along with Co-Op opportunities in Turkiye and Europe.

name: National Council of Negro Women, Inc. Drexel Section
description:
NCNW was founded in 1935 by Dr. Mary McLeod Bethune, an influential educator and activist, and civil rights advocate, and for more than fifty years, the iconic Dr. Dorothy Height was president of NCNW. Beginning March 2023 Rev. Shavon Arline-Bradley was appointed as the organization's first President & CEO.

Today, NCNW’s programs are grounded on a foundation of critical concerns that are now "NCNW Priorities." Our organization promotes education; encourages entrepreneurship, financial literacy, and economic stability; educates women about good health and promotes healthcare access, civic engagement and advocates for sound public policy and social justice.
summary: The National Council of Negro Women is an “organization of organizations” comprised of 330 campus and community-based sections and 33 national women’s organizations that enlightens, inspires, and connects more than 2,000,000 women and men.

name: Harmony Healers
description:
Join us in our mission to bridge the generational gap, forging bonds through the universal language of music. Harmony Healers perform live music for nursing facilities, assisted-living facilities, and hospitals in Philadelphia and beyond. We aim to deliver the therapeutic effects of music to brighten the spirits of the residents we encounter.

Harmony Healers enriches the lives of our elderly neighbors not only through music but also through personal interactions to foster meaningful connections between students and residents. Our members have a unique opportunity to blend their medical studies with the joy of creating a positive impact in the community.

If you have a passion for medicine, music, and putting a smile on another’s face, we invite you to join us as we spread musical warmth and compassion throughout Philadelphia. Whether you have a musical background or not, or even if you have a non-music-centered talent, you can make an impact with us. Let's make a difference — one note at a time!
summary: Welcome to Harmony Healers, where the healing power of music meets compassionate care! Our club gathers a symphony of talents dedicated to bringing joy and comfort to the elderly residents of local nursing and assisted-living facilities in Philadelphia.

name: Volleyball Club at West Reading
summary: Volleyball Club at West Reading welcomes students of any skill levels to play in weekly open gym games to de-stress and form long-lasting friendships.

name: Performing Arts & Medicine
description: DUCOM Performing Arts & Medicine seeks to engage the student community through performing and visual arts (e.g., voice, music, dance, theatre, painting, drawing, etc), as an element of their identity as a healthcare professional and explore the intersection of art with clinical practice.
summary: DUCOM Performing Arts & Medicine seeks to engage the student community through performing and visual arts (e.g., voice, music, dance, theatre, painting, drawing, etc), as an element of their identity as a healthcare professional!

name: Radial Groove
description: A group designed to bring in musicians and followers of music of all genres to discuss and appreciate music regularly, host private and public jam sessions, and inform the DUCOM community about local music events.
summary: A group designed to bring in musicians of all genres to discuss and appreciate music regularly, host private and public jam sessions, and inform the DUCOM community about local concerts.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Pre-Law Fraternity
description:
Phi Alpha Delta is the world’s preeminent Pre-law fraternity because of our dedication to service: service to the student, the school, the profession, and the community.

It is through devotion to the ideals of compassion, courage, diversity, innovation, integrity, professionalism, and service that we better both our profession and our community. Through accepting our differences we become not just a law fraternity, but a family.

Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International strives to be the premier legal professional organization with a diverse membership that cultivates leaders and serves as a voice for the role of legal professionalism in our society.

The purpose of this Fraternity shall be to form a strong bond uniting students and teachers of the law with members of the Bench and Bar in a fraternal fellowship designed to advance the ideals of liberty and equal justice under law; to stimulate excellence in scholarship; to inspire the virtues of compassion and courage; to foster integrity and professional competence; to promote the welfare of its members; and to encourage their moral, intellectual, and cultural advancement; so that each member may enjoy a lifetime of honorable professional and public service.
summary: Phi Alpha Delta is the world’s preeminent Pre-law fraternity because of our dedication to service: service to the student, the school, the profession, and the community.

name: Sun Run
description: Sun Run is a dedicated group of medical students committed to advocating for skin health awareness. Our primary goal is to ensure the safety of marathon runners and spectators by offering complimentary sunscreen during local Philadelphia marathon like the Drexel 5K. As we all know, prolonged exposure to the sun can have detrimental effects on the skin, and our goal is to promote awareness and protection against harmful UV rays. By distributing sunscreen in an organized manner, we aim to contribute to the overall well-being of the participants and help prevent skin cancer. Through our efforts, we aim to educate individuals about the significance of sun protection in preventing skin damage and reducing the risk of skin cancer. We are excited about the event and believe that our initiative can contribute significantly to the well-being of the community.
summary: A club for med students passionate about skin health. We provide free sunscreen to marathon runners and spectators at the Drexel 5K to promote sun safety and prevent skin damage to those involved!

name: Drexel Student Health Center
description:
Whether you need allergy shots or immunizations, treatment for a sports injury or a woman's health issue, Drexel's Student Health Center is here to provide you with a range of services in a confidential environment. Staffed by doctors and nurse practitioners from the Drexel University College of Medicine, our goal is to assist you in developing and maintaining a healthy lifestyle to benefit you throughout college and beyond.

  • Monday: 8:30 a.m.– 5 p.m.
  • Tuesday: 10:30 a.m.– 6:30 p.m.
  • Wednesday: 8:30 a.m.– 5 p.m.
  • Thursday: 10:30 a.m.– 6:30 p.m.
  • Friday: 8:30 a.m. – 5 p.m.
summary: The Drexel Student Health Center offers comprehensive health services to support the academic and social well-being of our students.

name: DUCOM Pool Association
description: A fun and safe place to learn and play Pool. No prior experience is required! Come socialize and play against classmates at HSB and various locations in Philly!
summary: A fun and safe place to learn and play Pool. No prior experience is required! Come socialize and play against classmates at HSB and various locations in Philly!

name: Sun Protection Outreach Teaching by Students
description: The mission of SPOTS is to educate students from preschool through high school about the importance of early detection and prevention of skin cancer. Through community outreach, DUCOM SPOTS aims to promote sun-safe habits, educate kids on the harmful effects of the use of artificial tanning beds, and teach students how to perform skin self-exams. This organization uniquely serves the Drexel community by engaging medical students in meaningful public health education and outreach, while providing school aged children education on skin cancer and prevention.
summary: SPOTS aims to educate K-12 students about sun-safe habits. SPOTS uniquely serves the DUCOM community by engaging medical students in meaningful public health education and outreach, while providing children with education on skin cancer and prevention.

name: Women in Music
description:
The goal of Women in Music at Drexel University is to advance the awareness, equality, diversity, heritage, opportunities, and cultural aspects of women and other underrepresented genders in the musical arts through education, support, empowerment, and recognition. 

We host jam sessions, panels, networking events, workshops, and more! This is an incredible opportunity to be part of an organization and a network of amazing individuals who are passionate about making the music industry a better place for all. Anyone interested in participating is welcome to join!
summary: WIM exists to advance the awareness, equality, diversity, heritage, opportunities, and cultural aspects of women and other underrepresented genders in the musical arts through education, support, empowerment, and recognition.

name: American Academy of Environmental Engineers and Scientists - Drexel University Chapter
description: The American Academy of Environmental Engineers and Scientists (AAEES) is a professional organization that aims to support and connect undergraduate and graduate environmental engineering and science students as they navigate their degrees and careers.
summary: The American Academy of Environmental Engineers and Scientists (AAEES) is a professional organization that aims to support and connect undergraduate and graduate environmental engineering and science students as they navigate their degrees and careers.

name: Basketball Club at WR
summary: Basketball Club at West Reading welcomes students of any experience level to play in pickup basketball games to de-stress and hone their skills.

name: DUCOM Book Wyrms
description: Welcome to DUCOM Book Wyrms—a cozy space where medical students can unwind and connect over literature without the pressure of extensive reading commitments. We meet monthly for relaxed, silent book club gatherings- complete with snacks and a welcoming atmosphere. Our goal is to read 2-3 books a year chosen together ranging anywhere from short stories to novels. We also embrace audiobooks to make enjoying literature during commutes or downtime more accessible. Join us to explore diverse genres, share recommendations, and find joy in reading, all while balancing the demands of medical school. Whether you’re a casual reader or an avid book lover, there’s a spot for you in our book club!
summary: Whether you are a casual or avid reader, this is the place for you! Join us for casual monthly silent book club gatherings, complete with snacks and fun giveaways. We'll read books, discuss our personal favorites and find the joy in casual reading!

name: DUCOM Badminton Club At West Reading
description: Badminton club aims to be an open and communicative organization that will help allow students to enjoy and learn about the sport that is badminton. Badminton is often difficult to just pick up an play on a more serious level due to the needing of equipment and indoor gym space. Ideally we will use funds to help mitigate some of those initial barriers for people. Badminton, although globally popular, does not recieve much attention or participation in the US, but can be a very fun and safe sport that would be a positive for the student population here.
summary: DUCOM badminton club at West Reading aims to bring together a community of badminton players both new and returning. We will aim to host regular practices, potentially some scrimmages, and also participate and get engaged with fitness days.

name: DUCOM Bowling Club at West Reading
description:
Are you excited to knock down some pins? At WR Bowling, we’re all about hitting the lanes and having a blast, whether you’re a seasoned bowler or just looking to unwind. This is your chance to relax, laugh, and connect with friends.

Join your classmates for an unforgettable time! Whether you’re chasing that perfect game or simply enjoying the atmosphere, everyone is welcome. WR Bowling is the ideal escape for medical students seeking to alleviate stress while embracing a bit of friendly competition.

Bowling offers the perfect break from your studies, allowing you to meet new people, sharpen your skills, and enjoy some good old-fashioned fun. So grab your bowling shoes and let’s make some strikes, spares, and memories together!
summary: Our mission is to create a relaxed, inclusive space where students can unwind and enjoy bowling. We foster community through casual games and friendly competition, offering a chance to improve skills while promoting a healthy work-life balance.

name: DUCOM Yarn Dragons
description:
Come join us in several arts and crafts workshops to learn how to crochet, embroider, needle felt, etc.

Our creations will be donated to the community! Zero prior experience is required :)
summary:
Yarn Dragons is an organization designed to spread our love of crafts and to make a positive impact on the community. 

Our focus is primarily on the Philadelphia campus.

name: DUCOM Foodies Club
description: Join us and connect with fellow foodies who share your love for great food, good company, and the joy of eating! We are planning on hosting bi-weekly trips and events in the Philly area. Join our page to stay in the loop!
summary: Welcome to DUCOM Foodie Club, a community for passionate food lovers! Whether you're a seasoned home chef, an adventurous eater, or simply someone who enjoys discovering new flavors, our club is the perfect place to indulge your culinary curiosity.

name: Peer Counselors
description:
Peer Counselors (PCs) are students trained to provide empathic, active listening session to support fellow students who want to talk with another students about some of the stressful aspects of the student experience (e.g., anxiety, sadness, relationship concerns, academic struggles, etc.). PCs also provide fun and educational outreach events on campus to promote a culture of mental wellness at Drexel.  Located in the Rush Building, PCs provide in-person or tele-appointments.  Students looking to speak to a PC can look up the PCs bios and schedule with someone who feels like a good fit.

 

Visit our Website: https://drexel.edu/counselingandhealth/counseling-center/peer-counseling
summary: Peer Counselors are students who provide supportive sessions to other students looking to talk with someone who understands some of the daily stressors students face. PCs also provide outreach to desgtimatize and promote mental health.

name: Drexel National Associationfor the Advancement of Colored People
description:
The Drexel NAACP Chapter is a dynamic student-led organization dedicated to advancing civil rights, social justice, and equality for all. We empower students to become advocates for change by addressing issues like racial injustice, voter suppression, and educational equity. Our chapter fosters leadership development, community service, and civic engagement through impactful initiatives, educational events, and advocacy efforts. Together, we strive to create a more inclusive and equitable campus, while building a strong foundation for future leaders in the fight for justice.

For more information on the NAACP's founding, watch this video.
summary: Drexel NAACP was created to reestablish a beacon of solidarity and amplify conversations on race and equality amongst the student body. Our goal is to develop leaders who are informed, socially conscious individuals who are committed to positive change.

name: Women in Orthopaedics
description:
Women in Orthopaedics is an orthopaedic society which promotes mentorship and empowerment of all those who identify as female and are interested in/pursuing a career in the field of orthopedics.  

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/109436503/vIfF5YBf
summary: Women in Orthopaedics is an orthopaedic society which promotes mentorship and empowerment of all those who identify as female and are interested in/pursuing a career in the field of orthopaedics.

name: Infectious Disease Interest Group at DUCOM
description:
The ID Interest Group is a collaborative and educational forum for budding medical students, of all years, to learn more about common pathogens in the healthcare setting. Meetings include presentations and discussions on such topics.

https://groupme.com/join_group/104163974/L2wq0Sj6
summary:
The IDIG is a collaborative and educational forum for budding medical students, of all years, to learn more about common pathogens in the healthcare setting. Meetings include presentations on such topics.
https://groupme.com/join_group/104163974/L2wq0Sj6

name: Allergy and Immunology Interest Group
description: The Allergy and Immunology Interest Group aims to educate students about the Allergy and Immunology specialty. The group connects students with professionals in the field, and provides opportunities to find research and understand what the specialty entails. Our group will explore topics spanning both pediatric and adult allergy medicine, new breakthroughs in the field, and much more.
summary: The Allergy and Immunology Interest Group aims to educate students about the Allergy and Immunology specialty of internal medicine.

name: Healthcare Sustainability
description:
Mission 

We want to empower future practices with the tools to champion sustainability in healthcare. We think addressing sustainability and environmental stewardship is an important aspect to public health, from providing green spaces within the hospital for staff and patients, to advocating for green infrastructure locally and reducing the cost of healthcare.  

 

Objectives 

  1. Explore the intersection of healthcare and environmental sustainability 
  2. Develop and implement actionable strategies for sustainable practices in hospital systems 
  3. Collaborate with hospital administration and environmental organizations to drive meaningful change 
  4. Promote research and innovation in healthcare sustainability 

 

Key Focus Areas:  

  1. Energy Efficiency and Renewables 
  2. Waste Reduction and Management 
  3. Green Transportation 
  4. Sustainable Procurement 
  5. Water Conservation
summary: We want to empower future practices with the tools to champion sustainability in healthcare. We think addressing sustainability and environmental stewardship is an important aspect of public health and can improve infrastructure locally and reduce costs.

name: DUCOM Floofs
description: DUCOM Floofs hopes to establish a close-knit and supportive pet community at DUCOM. In addition to creating a welcoming space where pet enthusiasts can connect, we will host educational workshops focused on responsible pet ownership (e.g. basic care, dog/cat cpr), develop programs to decrease the costs of pet ownership (e.g. intra-club pet sitting, free/reduced cost vaccines), and support local shelters through fundraising and volunteering initiatives and pet fostering programs. We will also organize community bonding events such as goat yoga, doggy socialization, and pet-themed movie nights, amongst others.
summary:
Whether you’re a proud pet owner or simply an animal enthusiast, DUCOM Floofs invites you to join our growing pet community! 

🐶🐱🐷🐁🐀🐹🐰🐸🐠🪲🐌🦋🦂🕷

name: DUCOM Powerlifting Club
description:
The DUCOM Powerlifting Club is Drexel’s premier club for medical students passionate about powerlifting and strength training. Focused on the core lifts—squat, bench press, and deadlift—we provide a community where members can push their limits, achieve personal records (PRs), and advance their lifting techniques. Our mission is to create an environment where students can balance the demands of medical school while dedicating time to their strength goals.

We plan to participate in official powerlifting competitions, giving members the opportunity to represent Drexel on a larger stage. For those looking for a more casual experience, we also host unofficial intra-school competitions, fostering a sense of friendly competition and camaraderie among our members.

With a focus on continuous improvement and progression, our club encourages lifters of all experience levels to set and achieve new goals, push past plateaus, and celebrate their progress together.
summary: The DUCOM Powerlifting Club is Drexel’s club for medical students dedicated to strength and powerlifting. We aim to provide a supportive environment where members can challenge themselves to hit PRs on the main weightlifting and Olympic lifts.

name: National Pan-Hellenic Council
description: The purpose of the DUNPHC is to foster cooperation and a positive and courteous discourse among its member organizations, focusing on matters of mutual concern, working collaboratively on projects to improve the community, and supporting academic excellence.
summary: The purpose of the DUNPHC is to foster cooperation and a positive and courteous discourse among its member organizations.

organizationType.name: Student Life Impact Organizations - CALENDAR YEAR

name: DUCOM Sex Positivity Week
summary: DUCOM Sex Positivity Week

name: Crosswalk Christian Fellowship
description:
Crosswalk Christian Fellowship is a vibrant college community within Metro Church, dedicated to students aged 18-22 living in the Philadelphia area. We bring together students from various schools to grow in their Christian faith through learning, encouragement, and challenge. We host Community Groups on Wednesdays or Thursdays from 7:00 PM to 9:00 PM on campus, with specific locations varying—text "Crosswalk" to 94000 to connect with us and get plugged in. Join us for Sunday Worship at Metro Church in East Falls at 9:30 AM or 11:30 AM. Rides are provided; text "Crosswalk" to 94000 for more details.

Instagram Link: @crosswalkfellowship

Website Link: metrophilly.org
summary: Crosswalk Christian Fellowship is your home away from home! We're a college fellowship within Metro Church, welcoming students aged 18-22 in the Philadelphia area. We unite students from various schools to learn, encourage, and grow in their faith.

name: DUCOM Diversity Week
summary: DUCOM Diversity Week

name: MedFit Mind and Movement
description:
MedFit Mind and Movement is an organization committed to empowering future healthcare professionals through a holistic approach to wellness. Our mission is to create a supportive community where students can engage in activities that nurture both their mental and physical well-being. We believe that prioritizing self-care is essential for making a meaningful impact in the healthcare field. By offering volunteer opportunities, fitness sessions, mindfulness practices, and educational events, we encourage students to embrace balanced, healthy lifestyles as they prepare for their careers in healthcare.

All healthcare majors will be working together in hospital settings, labs, and beyond, so we aim to build a safe place where we can all connect upon our experiences and help lean on each other when needed, especially being students in a rigorous academic program while bonding over mental and physical well-being exercises. MedFit Mind and Movement is designed to provide unique opportunities to network with fellow healthcare professionals and special guests.

No field should feel fragmented because, ultimately, we all share the same goal of helping our patients. Our aim is to create a safe space where people can build connections with one another while also fostering self-connection and developing habits that support both personal and professional growth.
summary: MedFit Mind and Movement promotes overall health and wellness of aspiring healthcare professionals and fosters a supportive environment to inspire students to live a healthy lifestyle, both mentally and physically.

name: Toxic Free Philly Drexel
description:
TFPD focuses on mitigating environmental toxin exposure on campus and in the surrounding communities! Joining TFPD can get you experience in areas below:

 

Organic Land Management Transition Research!

- Monthly research days where we randomly quadrat sample Lancaster Walk and Drexel Park to gather data on how the turf is reacting to the organic treatment

- Basic data analysis using Excel and R Studio if interested

- Grant applications

- Helping Grounds with the organic program

- Real world environmental field and data experience to add to your resume!

 

Community Building & Events!

- Help us plan events! Coordinate with other student organizations and organizations in Philly and advertise on our social media!

- Mantua Worldwide street clean ups 

- Environmental/nature related events
summary: TFPD focuses on mitigating environmental toxin exposure on campus and in the greater Philadelphia community, having started with synthetic herbicides and lead poisoning. Our work includes advocacy and research surrounding environmental toxins.

name: DUCOM Cycling Club
description: The DUCOM Cycling Club is a student-led organization dedicated to fostering a community of cycling enthusiasts (ranging from all levels including new comers) within Drexel University College of Medicine. Our club provides opportunities for students to engage in both indoor and outdoor cycling as a way to stay active, relieve stress, and build friendship outside the rigorous demands of medical school. Whether you are an experienced cyclist or just looking to explore cycling as a new hobby, the club offers a welcoming environment for all skill levels!
summary: Whether you are an experienced cyclist or just looking to explore cycling (indoor and/or outdoor) as a new hobby, the club offers a welcoming environment for all skill levels.

name: DUCOM Paddock
description: • Our main goal is to create a community for those that enjoy F1! Our plan is to host race days events and connect through our love of motorsports 🏎️
summary: A club for those that enjoy motorsports, mainly F1 but inclusive to all motorsports like Indy car or NASCAR!

name: DUCOM Bollywood Dance
description:
DBD is DUCOM's 1st Bollywood dance club that aims to create a fun and casual atmosphere for dancers to de-stress and enjoy Bollywood music and dance! Students learn Bollywood or Bollywood-fusion (ex. Hip-Hop or semi-classical) dances choreographed by fellow students. The club opens the opportunity of choreographing dances to its members. Bollywood does not require any technical experience and we welcome all levels and backgrounds of dancers! 

 

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/105730343/NA6Df3pO

Instagram: @ducom.bollywooddance
summary: DUCOM's 1st Bollywood dance club that aims to create a fun and casual atmosphere for dancers to de-stress and enjoy Bollywood dance! Members will learn Bolly or Bolly-fusion (ex. Hip-Hop or semi-classical) dances taught by fellow students.

name: International Cooking Club
description: This club will allow people to cook, interact, and share their cultural cuisines. It will include cooking classes, cultural sharing, and healthy eating tips.
summary: This club will allow people to cook, interact, and share their cultural cuisines. It will include cooking classes, cultural sharing, and healthy eating tips.

name: DUCOM K-Pop Club
description:
DUCOM’s K-Pop Club unites students through a shared interest in K-Pop. We learn about different groups, play K-Pop-related games, learn K-Pop choreographies, and watch concert streams. Our core values include respect, acceptance, and open-mindedness.

 

Email: ducomkpopclub@gmail.com

Instagram: @ducomkpopclub
summary: DUCOM’s K-Pop Club unites students through a shared interest in K-Pop. We learn about different groups, play K-Pop-related games, learn K-Pop choreographies, and watch concert streams. Our core values include respect, acceptance, and open-mindedness.

name: DUCOM Amputee Awareness and Advocacy Club
description: AAAC will host events like amputee panels, PM&R physician Q+A sessions, and a host of volunteering events with amputees to introduce DUCOM students to working with this population and promoting inclusive practices within medicine. AAAC’s main event each year will be a limb recycling event where we will take apart old prosthetics and donate the used pieces to make new prosthetics for people in need.
summary: The Amputee Awareness and Advocacy Club (AAAC) aims to foster understanding, empathy, and advocacy for individuals with limb differences by exposing DUCOM students to the unique physical, emotional, and social challenges faced by amputees.

name: Drexel Robotics
description:
At Drexel Robotics, we are passionate about innovation and hands-on learning. Our club brings together students from a wide range of disciplines to design, build, and program robots for competitions and projects. We participate in events like NASA's Lunabotics Challenge, where we tackle real-world engineering problems by creating robots capable of excavating lunar soil. Beyond competitions, we host workshops, technical talks, and social events to help our members grow their skills and connect with like-minded peers. Our goal is to foster a collaborative environment where every student can apply their knowledge in practical ways while exploring the frontiers of robotics. Whether you're an experienced engineer or a non-STEM major curious about robotics for the first time, Drexel Robotics welcomes you to join our community. Together, we'll push the boundaries of what robots can do!

 

Join our Discord for full team communications: Drexel Robotics Discord Server
summary: Join Drexel Robotics to design, build, and program innovative robots! Compete in events like NASA's Lunabotics Challenge while gaining hands-on experience in engineering, coding, and teamwork. Open to all interested students, regardless of major!

name: Drexel IEEE Graduate Chapter
description:
Drexel IEEE Graduate Chapter

The Drexel IEEE Graduate Chapter is a student-led organization dedicated to fostering a vibrant community of graduate students in engineering, technology, and related disciplines. As a part of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE), the chapter provides opportunities for professional growth, technical knowledge exchange, and interdisciplinary collaboration.

The chapter hosts workshops, seminars, and networking events, connecting students with industry experts, academics, and peers. Its initiatives aim to promote innovation, research, and leadership, empowering members to excel in their fields. Whether you're looking to develop technical skills, engage in cutting-edge research discussions, or expand your professional network, the Drexel IEEE Graduate Chapter offers a platform to enhance your graduate experience.
summary: The Drexel IEEE Graduate Chapter fosters a vibrant community for graduate students in engineering and technology. It offers workshops, seminars, and networking events, promoting innovation, research, and professional growth while connecting members.

name: Drexel Pickleball Club
description:
Drexel Pickleball Club hosts weekly Pickleball for all skill levels. We normally play pick-up games with the occasional tournament.

Whether you are a complete beginner or semi-pro, we hope you will come out for one of our events.
summary: Drexel Pickleball Club hosts weekly Pickleball for all skill levels. We play pick-up games with an occasional tournament.

name: Drexel Transfer Students Association
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to foster mentorship and community among new transfer students who will need more support and guidance, as stated in our mission statement. We aim to be the leading association for a transfer student’s long-term and short-term success as our vision. Our organization's objectives are to provide additional opportunities to transfer students by providing networking events, extra advising, and a safe place to come to for support.
summary: This organization is established to foster mentorship and community among new transfer students who will need more support and guidance, as stated in our mission statement. We aim to be the leading association for a transfer student’s long term success.

name: Engineering Technology Students Association
description:
The purpose of the Engineering Technology Student Association (ETSA) is to serve as Drexel University's primary hub for applied engineering and hands-on technological innovation. The association is dedicated to the following objectives:

  1. To provide a supportive community, dedicated resources, and professional mentorship for students in the Engineering Technology major.

  2. To bridge the gap between engineering theory and practice by offering skill-building workshops, collaborative projects, and industry networking events open to all interested Drexel students, regardless of their major.

  3. To foster a campus-wide appreciation for the principles of applied engineering and to create a collaborative environment where students from all disciplines can innovate and build together.

 

Make sure to join us on Discord through the link below!

 

https://discord.gg/vfujKWfvkh
summary: A place for the Engineering students to connect, collaborate, and receive help, guidance and mentorship as they navigate through Drexel University

name: Drexel Institute of Transportation Engineers
description:
The Drexel University Institute of Transportation Engineers (ITE) is dedicated to fostering an inclusive and collaborative community for students passionate about transportation engineering. Our mission is to provide networking and mentorship opportunities that bridge the gap between academia and industry, empowering members with the training, education, and resources necessary to innovate in the field. We strive to promote safe, efficient, sustainable, and resilient transportation solutions that enhance the future of mobility while embracing equity, diversity and inclusion in all aspects of our work. 

 

As part of the international community of the Institute of Transportation Engineers (ITE), Drexel’s chapter will leverage ITE’s affiliation with over 18,000 professionals, professors, lawmakers, and peers from around the world. By joining 170 other student chapters, we aim to create pathways for our talented students to engage with this vast network through conferences, professional development programs, collaborative projects, and competitions. This global connection will provide unparalleled opportunities for students to exchange ideas, stay informed on the latest innovations, and contribute to shaping the future of transportation engineering.
summary: Drexel ITE fosters an inclusive community for transportation engineering students, offering networking, mentorship, and resources to bridge academia and industry. We promote innovation, equity, and sustainable mobility solutions for a better future.

name: Bhagat Puran Singh Health Initiative
description:
BPSHI is an organization based on the life of Bhagat Puran Singh, a man who devoted his life to the service of those in need. Bhagat Puran Singh's life story took place in Punjab, India, where he created a free healthcare institution after the Partition. His legacy began with taking Piara Singh under his care, who was an abandoned child with leprosy. This led him to create a free healthcare institution, called Pingalwara, where the handicap and abandoned were cared for. 

The Bhagat Puran Singh Health Initiative takes inspiration from his legacy and strives to eliminate health and social disparities within underserved and underprivileged populations. Our hope is to better our community through providing access to health education for the betterment of the mind, body, spirit.
summary: The Bhagat Puran Singh Health Initiative (BPSHI) non-profit founded by students in CA, providing free health screenings and resources across the U.S. Inspired by Bhagat Puran Singh's selfless service, BPSHI fosters community health and volunteerism.

name: The Flame, Drexel's Majorette and Stomp & Shake Team
summary: Our team that focuses on blending majorette style with the stomp and shake tradition.We showcase a unique combination of precision, creativity, and athleticism, performing at various school and community events. Our dancers shine their spark everywhere!

name: Brighten A Day-Drexel
summary: Brighten A Day is a non-profit dedicated to fighting isolation and spreading cheer. With the mission to deliver love, hope, and joy through heartfelt letters, and encouraging video messages. Our goal is to extend the mission to our local community.

name: Hindu YUVA @ Drexel
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to bring together both Hindu and non-Hindu students to raise awareness about what Hinduism is. As one of the largest and oldest cultures in the world, we believe it is necessary that there be an organization on campus to represent the interests of the Hindu community present here. We hope to create a comfortable atmosphere where students can come and celebrate their culture. We hope to achieve these goals by having team-building games, yoga and meditation, and exciting debates and presentations at our weekly meetings. All in all, we want to build a small Hindu community within Drexel University so that students can come and discover a home away from home.
summary: To create a platform to protect, preserve, practice, and promote Hindu Dharma. We aim to develop character and leadership skills in our members by emphasizing values like self-discipline, self-confidence, and a spirit of selfless service to society.

name: Drexel East Africa Student Association
description: Drexel East African Students Association (DEASA) is a student-led organization dedicated to fostering a sense of community among East African students at Drexel University while promoting cultural awareness and inclusivity on campus. Our mission is to celebrate the rich diversity of East African cultures through engaging events, social gatherings, and educational initiatives. We aim to create a welcoming space where students can connect, share experiences, and support one another both academically and socially. DEASA also seeks to collaborate with other student organizations, participate in community service projects, and advocate for diversity and representation within the Drexel community. Whether you identify as East African or simply want to learn more about the region’s vibrant cultures, all are welcome to join!
summary: Drexel East African Students Association (DEASA) fosters community among East African students while promoting cultural awareness at Drexel. We celebrate East African heritage through events, social gatherings, and initiatives, creating a welcoming space

name: Jewish Girls Club
description: JGC's mission is to create a supportive community for Jewish girls, fostering friendships and empowerment through a deep understanding of our rich traditions and the vital role we play in Judaism.
summary: JGC's mission is to create a supportive community for Jewish girls, fostering friendships and empowerment through a deep understanding of our rich traditions and the vital role we play in Judaism.

name: Drexel University Club Boxing
description:
Join Our Discord: https://discord.gg/UWrzE4sKFd 

or select the earth icon next to IG icon below.

Drexel exel University Club Boxing is a student‑run organization that develops boxing fundamentals, technique, and conditioning in a welcoming environment. Open to all levels, we host regular sessions focused on footwork, combos, defense, and sparring. Members train under experienced boxers and coaches, emphasizing discipline, respect, and camaraderie. We also compete in USA Amateur Boxing events, providing athletes the opportunity to represent Drexel in sanctioned bouts across the region.

 

IMPORTANT!

Drexel Club Boxing is a student-run, competitive program that travels to sanctioned collegiate events—often out of state—to box other universities. Expect real rounds and real road trips.

Our primary training home is James Shuler’s Boxing Gym in West Philadelphia, about 0.8 miles west of campus near Lancaster Ave. It’s SEPTA-accessible via Trolley 10 or Bus 31. We also use the Drexel Rec Center for the first two weeks of each term for onboarding and non-contact technical work per university policy. Note: Drexel does not provide transportation.

 

We’re mostly self-funded and fully student-run, so standards are high. safety and performance come first. Time management with classes and work is non-negotiable; if something comes up, communicate with club leadership in advance (excused absences are case-by-case). USA Boxing registration is required for sparring and competition; non-sparring training members are welcome. We’re filling roster spots for both competitive boxers and training members—contact the Club President to get started.

Members who request to join will be added to the official roster upon completion of the required paperwork and consistent attendance to practice. Members who do not submit their paperwork will be considered inactive and removed from the pending list. If you have any questions regarding the paperwork or the club in general do not hesitate to email. 

 

Press the earth icon to join discord for more information on how to join in!!!!! 

Be364@drexel.edu
summary: Drexel University Club Boxing

name: Drexel DECA
summary: DECA's mission is to prepare emerging leaders and entrepreneurs in marketing, finance, hospitality, and management. DECA will engage students personally, professionally, and academically through workshops, competitions, and mentorship opportunities.

name: Drexel Residence Life
description:
About Us
Housing and Residence Life (HRL) is committed to creating a welcoming and supportive residential community at Drexel University.

Our Mission
We take a student-centered approach to help residents grow personally, solve problems effectively, and navigate conflicts collaboratively. HRL promotes safety through proactive education and accessible resources, while encouraging students to reflect on their actions and their impact on the community.

Our Vision
HRL strives to provide a residential experience where students build the knowledge, skills, and self-awareness needed to thrive academically, professionally, and personally — at Drexel and beyond.
summary:
About Us
Housing and Residence Life (HRL) is committed to creating a welcoming and supportive residential community at Drexel University.

name: Elkins Park Student Engagement
description:
Student Engagement is housed within the Office of Student Affairs. We encourage, develop, nurture and prepare students to lead their peers and, in the future, to be in the forefront of their profession. Additionally, we work with our many organizations, including Student Council, to create opportunities for students from all University programs to connect through sports, leadership, and social events.

 

Office of Student Affairs
Monae Kelsey, Director of Student Engagement &
Assistant Director of Admissions
Phone: 215.780.1313
Email: salus_saffairs@drexel.edu
summary: Office of Student Engagement at Elkins Park

name: Bentley Hall
description:
Welcome to Bentley Hall!

We’re so excited to have you as part of our community. Bentley Hall is more than just a place to live—it’s a space to connect, learn, and grow during your time at Drexel.

At Bentley Hall, we value:

  • Community & Civic Engagement – building meaningful connections and giving back
  • Learning & Integration – growing inside and outside of the classroom
  • Sense of Self & Purpose – discovering who you are and what drives you

Our staff is here to support you every step of the way:

  • Area Coordinator (AC): Saicha Crawford
  • Residential Desk Coordinators (RDCs): LaShay Spencer and Alicia Thomas
  • Resident Assistants (RAs): Rina Notani and Vivel Vishegonov (Floor 2); Sofia Arastu and Hitashi Kalra (Floor 3); Jordana Benblatt and Blaze Fogle (Floor 4); Sukriti Dhungel and Serena Osuagwu (Floor 5); Dwij Rana and Kayden Noel (Floor 5); Lorelei Booth and Aahil Hirani (Floor 6); Charis Trusty and Xochitl Cruz-Hernandez (Floor 8).

Check this page often to find upcoming events, ways to get involved, and opportunities to connect with your neighbors. We can’t wait to see you at programs and around the hall!

We’re so excited to have you as part of our community. Race Hall is more than just a place to live—it’s a space to connect, learn, and grow during your time at Drexel.

  • Community & Civic Engagement – building meaningful connections and giving back

name: Meor at Drexel
description:
The primary purpose of this organization shall be to create Jewish leaders literate in Jewish wisdom, culture, and heritage. It shall instill them with confidence and Jewish values and help them discover their identities as Jews.

 

Our objectives include, but are not limited to: bringing Jewish, text-based, mussar learning, which will allow students to learn about Judaism within context and be rooted in sources. Additionally, provide a place for students to be heard and share their experiences, and be a safe environment to express their experiences of being a Jew.
summary: A Jewish organization focused on providing a space to learn and experience Jewish values in a diverse and immersive Jewish community. Come learn about our culture, history, and beliefs in an inclusive and diverse community.

name: Drexel Nritya
description: We aim to bring the creativity and cultural richness of these traditions to Drexel's campus, fostering an inclusive environment where students can explore, learn, and express themselves through classical dance. By encouraging collaboration and artistic growth, we strive to make classical dance an integral part of Drexel’s diverse cultural landscape.
summary: Our mission is to build a passionate community of dancers dedicated to preserving and promoting Indian classical dance forms.
