name: Canadian Student Association
description: SCA is dedicated to fostering a vibrant and inclusive community for Canadian students. We aim to create a supportive environment where members can connect, share experiences, and celebrate Canadian culture. Through a variety of events, social gatherings, and networking opportunities, we strive to build lasting friendships and provide a sense of belonging. Whether you’re looking to engage in meaningful conversations, enjoy traditional Canadian festivities, or simply meet fellow Canadians, our club is here to ensure you feel at home. Join us in creating a welcoming community that enriches your student experience and strengthens our collective Canadian identity.
summary: CSA is dedicated to fostering a vibrant and supportive community for Canadian students to connect through a variety of events, social gatherings, and networking opportunities.

name: Coalition of Occupational Therapy Advocates for Diversity
summary: COTAD Chapters are OT/OTA student-led groups that work together to develop and promote JEDI, anti-racism, and anti-oppression within occupational therapy education programs and in the profession.

name: Drexel Association of Prosthetics and Orthotics
description: The Drexel Association of Prosthetics and Orthotics (DAPO) is a dynamic student organization dedicated to inspiring curiosity, compassion, and innovation within the field of orthotics and prosthetics. Through meaningful community outreach, adaptive sports experiences, and engaging educational presentations, DAPO connects students with real-world impact while fostering a supportive and inclusive environment open to all members of the Drexel community. Active participation encourages collaboration, professional growth, mentorship between experienced and newer students, and hands-on involvement in committees that bring fundraising, outreach, and social engagement initiatives to life. United by a shared passion for improving lives, DAPO empowers students to learn, serve, and lead with purpose while advancing awareness of prosthetic and orthotic care both on campus and beyond.
summary: DAPO is an organization that serves to educate and expose students and the community to the field of orthotics and prosthetics through community outreach, adaptive sports events, as well as various educational presentations at local schools and colleges.

name: National Student Speech Language Hearing Association
description: Success starts with National NSSLHA. We inspire, empower, and support students in the field of Speech Language Pathology. We give you the tools to navigate your academic career while preparing you for your professional one.
summary: The National Student Speech Language Hearing Association (NSSLHA) is the only national student organization for pre-professionals studying communication sciences and disorders (CSD) recognized by the American Speech-Language-Hearing Association (ASHA).

name: PCO Contact Lens Club
summary: Program specific organization that hosts events and workshops for members of the program interested in furthering their contact lens education

name: Private Practice Club
summary: The purpose of the Private Practice Club is to prepare optometry students at Pennsylvania College of Optometry to enter private/ independent optometry practice by providing a knowledge base about the business aspects of the profession.

name: Student Occupational Therapy Association
description:
The purpose of the Student Occupational Therapy Association is twofold. First, SOTA will increase campus and community awareness of the occupational therapy profession, as well as facilitate interaction between occupational therapy classes. SOTA encourages interdisciplinary participation from the student body by hosting charity events, social gatherings, and fostering a strong sense of belonging among fellow colleagues.

The Student Occupational Therapy Association of Salus at Drexel University is affiliated with the American Occupational Therapy Association.
summary: SOTA encourages interdisciplinary participation from the student body by hosting charity events, social gatherings, and fostering a strong sense of belonging among fellow colleagues.

name: Christian Fellowship Club
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to provide a welcoming community for students at Drexel University, Elkins Park Campus to grow in their Christian faith, encourage one another through fellowship, study the Word of God, and serve others in the love of Christ. The Christian Fellowship club seeks to encourage spiritual growth, friendship, and service through activities such as prayer, Bible studies, creative projects, and community service. This club aims to be a space where students- whether lifelong believers, new to the Faith, or simply interested in learning more about Christ- can explore faith in an open and respectful environment. Our mission is to strengthen individual faith, build genuine relationships, and reflect the love of Christ both on campus and in our local community through fellowship, lasting friendships, and shared experiences.
summary: The Christian Fellowship Club seeks to encourage spiritual growth, friendship, and service through activities such as prayer, Bible studies, creative projects, and community service.

name: Hearts In Motion
summary: The Salus at Drexel Hearts in Motion Club is an organization that partners with Hearts in Motion, Indiana to provide medical care and education to improve health outcomes for the people of communities in Zacapa, Guatemala.

name: Student Academy of the American Academy of Physician Assistants
description: SAAAPA is a national student organization that every Physician Assistant school across the country has for their students. Each Physician Assistant student becomes a member of SAAAPA when they are enrolled in their program. At Salus at Drexel, SAAAPA is mainly a fundraising organization for sending our second year students to the annual challenge bowl at the American Academy of Physician Assistants (AAPA) conference. The mission statement of SAAAPA is to continue the ongoing mission of the physician assistant profession in serving others at Salus University and promoting positive well-being for all members of our community. Our goal as a group is to integrate our profession into the Salus community, increase awareness about our profession, actively participate in AAPA conferences and the national medical challenge bowl, raise funds for local and global charities, and continuously strive to impact the future of the PA profession.
summary: We are a SAAAPA Student Society, a community of PA students dedicated to leadership, professional advocacy, and clinical excellence. We empower future PAs through networking, community service, and preparation for the national Challenge Bowl.

name: The American Optometric Student Association at Drexel
description: AOSA at Drexel is the Pennsylvania College of Optometry’s chapter of the American Optometric Student Association, the national organization for optometry students. It’s student-led and dedicated to advocating for the profession, enhancing optometry education, and empowering students to succeed as future doctors of optometry. Through events like quiz bowls, fundraisers, info nights, and national meetings, AOSA helps build community, leadership skills, and professional development among students at Drexel.
summary: AOSA at Drexel is a student-led organization that supports optometry students through advocacy, professional development, and community engagement, helping prepare future doctors of optometry for success.

name: Drexel University BRAG Chapter
description: Drexel University BRAG Chapter (Black Retail Action Group) is the student chapter of BRAG USA, a national nonprofit dedicated to advancing leadership in retail, fashion, and related industries.
summary: Drexel University BRAG Chapter (Black Retail Action Group) is the student chapter of BRAG USA, a national nonprofit dedicated to advancing leadership in retail, fashion, and related industries.

name: Student Optometric Service to Humanity
description: Student optometrist organization focused on providing care to those both domestically and internationally with optometry based needs.
summary: Service based organization which engages with the community both domestic and international via service based trips.

name: Central Asian Student Organization
description: Central Asian Student Organization (CASO) is a cultural and educational student organization dedicated to celebrating and promoting the diverse cultures, traditions, and histories of Central Asia. CASO creates inclusive spaces through cultural events, educational programming, and community engagement while fostering cross-cultural dialogue at Drexel University.
summary: Central Asian Student Organization (CASO) celebrates Central Asian cultures through cultural events, educational programs, and community building, creating an inclusive space for students at Drexel University.

name: Drexel Exercise Science Club
summary: This organization aims to support students in exercise science by creating an inclusive, educational community that offers resources, opportunities, and mentorship while reducing academic, financial, and experiential barriers.

name: Political Science Student Association
description: The Political Science Student Association (PSSA) is a student led organization committed to fostering an inclusive and engaged community for students interested in political science and related fields. PSSA encourages exploration of the discipline’s diverse areas, including political theory, public policy, international relations, and comparative politics, through discussions, guest speakers, academic events, and experiential learning opportunities. The organization works to strengthen connections between students and faculty, support professional and academic development, and promote thoughtful civic engagement.
summary: The Political Science Student Association is a student led organization that fosters community among political science students, encourages engagement with the field’s diverse disciplines, and promotes the practical application of political science.

name: Drexel P.U.L.S.E: Chapter of Global Public Health Brigades
description:
Drexel P.U.L.S.E. (Public Health, Unity, Leadership, Service, and Education) is a student-led organization committed to improving public health both in Philadelphia and internationally. Our mission is to create meaningful, hands-on opportunities for students to engage with public health challenges through service, education, and global health brigades.

P.U.L.S.E. focuses on strengthening public health infrastructure by organizing service projects, educational initiatives, and community partnerships that address real-world health needs. We emphasize unity by fostering collaboration among students and working alongside international communities to support sustainable, culturally informed public health efforts.

Leadership development is central to our organization. Members are encouraged to take on leadership roles within the club, contribute to project planning, and gain experience relevant to careers in public health and healthcare. Through our service initiatives—both on campus and abroad—students work directly to improve health outcomes and promote equity in underserved communities.

In addition, P.U.L.S.E. prioritizes education by raising awareness about public health systems, global health disparities, and the goals of our brigade programs. By combining service, learning, and leadership, P.U.L.S.E. offers students a supportive community and a practical pathway to make a lasting impact in public health.
summary: Drexel P.U.L.S.E. is a student-led organization focused on improving public health locally and globally. Through service, education, leadership, and global health brigades, members gain hands-on experience while creating community impact today worldwide.

name: ColorStack Chapter At Drexel University
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to increase the number of Black and Latinx Computer Science graduates that go on to launch rewarding technical careers.

name: Omani Students Association at Drexel University
summary: OSA brings students together to promote Omani culture, hospitality, and community through cultural, social, and educational events that support student connection and cross-cultural engagement at Drexel.

name: Drexel Black Pre-Law Association
summary: Drexel Black Pre-Law Association (BPLA)’s mission statement is to promote diversity within the legal field by providing mentorship, resources, and opportunities that prepare our members for law school and eventually, their career.

name: Drexel's Photography Club
description:
Who we are
Drexel Photography Club is a campus-wide community for anyone who loves making images—whether you shoot with a camera or your phone. We welcome absolute beginners, seasoned hobbyists, and aspiring professionals alike. Our goal is simple: learn together, create together, and share work that matters.

What we do

Hands‑on learning

  • Skill‑building workshops on exposure, composition, lighting, studio/strobe, film, editing (Lightroom/Photoshop/Capture One), color management, printing, and more.

  • Critique sessions that are supportive, structured, and focused on helping you grow (no gatekeeping).

  • Studio & lighting sessions with demo setups and guided practice.

  • Guest talks from working photographers, alumni, retouchers, art directors, photojournalists, and curators.

  • Portfolio reviews & career panels to help you move from “I take photos” to “I’m building a body of work.”

Go shoot (a lot)

  • Photowalks around Philly (from Rittenhouse to Chinatown to Fishtown to Fairmount Park).

  • Themed challenges (street, portrait, macro, long exposure, film-only, phone-only, 36 frames, etc.).

  • Trips/field shoots when possible (museums, galleries, events, hikes).

Show & publish

  • Seasonal exhibitions and pop‑up galleries on campus.

  • Club zines and digital publications showcasing member work (great for your portfolio).

  • Online galleries & social features to spotlight members regularly.

Collaborate & serve

  • Partnerships with other student orgs, academic departments, and campus offices to document events and tell visual stories.

  • Opportunities to assist on shoots, build teams (models, MUAs, stylists, designers), and experiment with creative concepts.

  • A strong emphasis on ethical image‑making: consent, privacy, cultural sensitivity, and copyright literacy.
summary: Join a campus community dedicated to camera and photo lovers: photowalks, workshops, studio time, critiques, zines, exhibitions, and portfolio reviews. Any type of camera, any level. Let’s make something worth sharing.

name: The Thomistic Institute at Drexel University
summary: The Thomistic Institute draws on Thomas Aquinas’s vision that faith and reason share the same source, truth, and cannot conflict. It fosters a culture where intellectual rigor and faith work together to pursue wisdom.

name: Girls Who Lead, Drexel Chapter
description:
Girls Who Lead is a student-led organization dedicated to empowering young women to become leaders in policy, business, and advocacy. Our chapter is open to all majors and provides experiences that are relevant across fields by exploring the intersection of public policy and social impact.

Members engage in workshops, speaker events, conferences, and hands-on projects that build practical skills and real-world understanding. Girls Who Lead provides a supportive space for students to connect, develop their ideas, and grow as changemakers on campus and in the broader community.
summary: Girls Who Lead empowers students of all majors to become changemakers by developing leadership skills through hands-on projects, workshops, professional panels, and events focused on policy, business, and social impact.
